title: How 90s Pop Culture Taught Women to Compete & How to Unlearn It with Sophie Gilbert
summary: What if the pop culture you loved growing up was quietly teaching you to shrink, perform and turn on other women?  In this episode, Pulitzer PrizeвҖ“nominated journalist Sophie Gilbert joins me to revisit the legacy of the 90s and 2000s вҖ” a time that likely shaped how you see yourself & other women.  We talk about the toxic version of вҖҳempowermentвҖҷ that dominated the era, why so many of us still feel stuck in competition mode and how we can start untangling the stories we were sold.  рҹ’Ў You'll hear:  вҖ“ How porn and pop culture collided to redefine girlhood  вҖ“ Why so many women still struggle with internalised competition  вҖ“ WhatвҖҷs changed in how women are portrayed вҖ” and what hasnвҖҷt  вҖ“ How to start spotting (and unlearning) the cultural scripts you didnвҖҷt choose  вҖ“ And what reclaiming your story can actually look likeВ   рҹ”— Mentioned:  Girl on Girl by Sophie Gilbert  SophieвҖҷs writing at The Atlantic   Ed's Doco Series on what led to Britney's conservatorship    рҹ’ҢВ More from That's Helpful:  Subscribe to my Substack  Watch the podcast on YouTube  Follow ThatвҖҷs Helpful on Instagram  рҹ“¬ Got thoughts or guest suggestions? title: Show Up Daily вҖ“ Andrew and Tristan Tate Explain 'The Unstoppable Power of Consistency!"
summary: I was broke. Starving. Driving my busted Volkswagen Golf to KFC, waiting for people to leave behind perfectly good chicken so me and Andrew could survive. That was life at 21. But I refused to lose. I refused to let hunger stop me from winning my fights, from making it out. Fast forwardвҖ”now itвҖҷs my birthday, and weвҖҷre eating KFC as a joke. The struggle turned into triumph.В  Most men waste time crying over a breakup, scrolling social media, making excuses. Weak. If youвҖҷre not using pain as fuel, youвҖҷre failing. The world is ultra-competitiveвҖ”every dollar, every woman, every bit of respect is a fight. You donвҖҷt get ahead by sitting around feeling sorry for yourself. You get ahead by taking full accountability, grinding relentlessly, and becoming the best version of yourself.В В  You either take control of your lifeвҖ”or life controls you. title: #443 Ask Me Anything: ADHD, Money Blocks & Manifestor Momentum
summary: In this episode IвҖҷm joined by my amazing COO, Taylor, to bring you Part 2 of the вҖңAsk Me AnythingвҖқ podcast. I answer your most burning human design questions (and questions that have nothing to do with HD at all!). QUESTIONS ANSWERED IN THIS EPISODE: Right at the time I was diagnosed with ADHD a few months ago and chronic sleep disorder from childhood, I found you. And as I study my human design chart, it seems I'm in my not self in burnout and have been since primary school or since I remember. I'm not on medication as I feel I want to explore my human design more in depth to see if that helps me. Do you think that there's something in your chart that can be your not self instead of something you need lifelong medication for? Should we explore other people's crosses and allow them to reveal why those people have come into our lives? Any thoughts or stories about this?I want to know how to get more momentum with making money. Starting to feel more abundant. I'm doing nervous system work. I've integrated how to initiate and inform. I'm a Manifestor. It just doesn't seem to take off. I'm a 5'1 and also have the 2145 channel.What I would really love to know is what if I was born in Australia, but three months before I was born, my mom was in Europe, a different time zone during the design date. Does the math still math?I trust you will get what you need from this episode, and make sure you come let me know how it resonated with you on instagram @the_human_design_coach Big love, Mxx OTHER RESOURCES Want more on Human Design? title: Abundance вҖ“ The No. 1 Thing People Want to Manifest
podcast_name: All Up In Your Biz with Yvonne Tchrakian
summary: In this episode, Yvonne is talking manifesting abundance - the number 1 thing people want. She explains how to identify what abundance means for you personally, recognize and celebrate the abundance you already have to fuel your manifestation efforts, and how to incorporate mindset habits into your daily routine that will supercharge your ability to manifest more abundance.  Yvonne is currently working on generating more abundance as a way of funding her upcoming Manifestation Masterclass with Matt Cooke. So, you can follow along with her, see exactly how she does it and implement what you learn.  KEY TAKEAWAYS   Abundance is the No. 1 thing people want to manifest. Learning to recognise, acknowledge and celebrate abundance is an important part of successfully manifesting more. Abundance is already everywhere in your life, that coffee a friend bought you, or the special offer on your favourite cosmetic - thatВҙs abundance. Those little things already add up to make your life so much better. Take joy from them. Use regular alarms throughout the day so that you pause and remind yourself of things you are grateful for within the last 24 hours e.g. the beautiful sunshine, being fit enough to walk to work. 100% trust that greater abundance is going to come for you. But let go of when, it might not happen as quickly as you expect, donВҙt worry, it will still happen. Be clear on what abundance means for you. Without clarity it wonВҙt happen. Yvonne explains how to fully understand what type of abundance you want. Throughout the day use affirmations to keep your mind in the right place to attract abundance. Yvonne shares some great ones. Download and use the Pause Penny check and letter to the universe. This powerful technique worked for Jim Carey and other celebrities. If you are feeling a bit low, do the abundance challenge to lift your vibe. Yvonne explains how. Feel the fear, put yourself out there and go for it. You have to grab the opportunities that will fuel and attract abundance.  BEST MOMENTS  вҖңI want more abundance and less work.вҖқ  вҖңStop and pause and just ask yourself the question, вҖҳWhat are the first words that come into my head when I say money?вҖҷвҖқ  вҖңYou don't need to overthink the money and how it's going to come. How is not your problem.вҖқ  вҖңFeel the fear and do it anyway.вҖқ  вҖңAbundance is everywhere.вҖқ  вҖңThe more grateful you are, the more that's going to come into your life.вҖқ  вҖңI am manifesting abundance for my sponsorship event in September.вҖқ  EPISODE RESOURCES  Free Manifestation and Abundance tool kit that includes the Pause Penny check and letter to the universe -В https://pausepenny.com/download/  ManifestieBestie Club reel -В https://www.instagram.com/yvonnetchrakian_manifest/reel/DFQIVOQsf8y  https://www.pcmag.com/how-to/how-to-make-song-your-alarm-iphone-android  VALUABLE RESOURCES  website: pausepenny.com  Instagram: @yvonnetchrakian_manifest @pause.penny  LinkedIn:В https://www.linkedin.com/in/yvonne-tchrakian-64257619/  HOST BIO  Yvonne started her career as a corporate lawyer for one of Ireland's top law firms. After the loss of a baby during pregnancy, Yvonne founded Pause. Penny - which was created to inspire people to manifest their dream lives. She is also a mom of four. Yvonne is a serial manifester, manifesting everything from her dream jobs (yes, multiple), her soulmate, dream house and even her twins! title: 654: Why Are We Raising Our Kids With No Screens, Sugar Or Dairy? & The AвҖҷs To All Your QвҖҷs
summary: ItвҖҷs been a while since IвҖҷve done a Q&A episode вҖ” so this one is extra stacked! You sent in your questions about motherhood, business, routines, screen time, nutrition, boundaries, and so much moreвҖҰ and IвҖҷm here to answer them all вҖ” no holds barred, complete transparency, from my heart to yours. Tune in as I pull back the curtains on: how weвҖҷve navigated four years without screen time for my daughter (along with no dairy, gluten or sugar), how I transition between work and mum life, our plan for BambiвҖҷs education, the newborn essentials I swear by, my thoughts on playdates, boundaries, potty training, how we navigate eating in social settings, and what really happens when I feel like IвҖҷm failing at both motherhood and business вҖ” itвҖҷs all in here. Whether you're a new mama, a soon-to-be mama, or deep in the sacred trenches of running a business and caring for others, this episode is full of practical tips, honest reflections, and a behind-the-scenes look at how we really do things in our home. Press play now for an honest, unfiltered chat about whatвҖҷs working, what weвҖҷre still figuring out, and everything IвҖҷve learned along the way. title: Maintenance & Mindfulness: Midweek Motivation - May 14, 2025
summary: Welcome to your Midweek Motivation! This week, Gina and Odette get real about the stuff that often gets brushed asideвҖ”mindset, sleep, stress, and the routines that either support or sabotage your goals. From finding meaning in a rainy day to redefining whatвҖҷs non-negotiable in your life, they explore how even small shifts in thinking can change your trajectory. They dig into the power of rest, why quality always trumps quantity, and how motivation doesnвҖҷt show upвҖ”you have to. Plus, Odette shares a little вҖңlong weekend homeworkвҖқ. You can find the full video hosted at: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ginalivymaintenanceandmindfulness To learn more about The Livy Method and our Maintenance & Mindfulness group, visit www.ginalivy.com.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: #139 Martin Sheppard - The Energy, Mindset and Resilience to be Australian Chairman & sit on the Global Board of Consulting Firm KPMG
summary: This week, Andrew sits down with Martin Sheppard, the National Chairman of KPMG Australia, a visionary leader whose drive and dedication have shaped the firmвҖҷs strategic direction. MartinвҖҷs journey is a testament to the discipline and resilience required to thrive in todayвҖҷs competitive landscape. With nearly 3 decades of experience at KPMG, Martin has mastered the art of merging governance with innovation, all while stressing the importance of being "MatchFit" and creating "Leadership Capacity" - a holistic approach that combines physical and mental readiness for peak performance. "In terms of leadership, I'm not a big emailer and I'm not a big meeting requester...I think true leadership is actually knocking over and getting things out of the way and empowering other people..." In this episode Andrew and Martin discuss: 2:30 Andrew and MartinвҖҷs long term professional and personal relationship, AndrewвҖҷs ADHD theory in relation to productivity and the brutal feedback Andrew gave to Martin on his presentation. 7:10 How showing vulnerability and putting a bit of himself in the presentation helped Martin, believing in yourself and following through on it and learning as much about leadership outside of work as inside. 11:55 Advice on leadership from the former premier of NSW, the rise of digital natives in the current crop of new hires and why KPMG bought AndrewвҖҷs previous business. 19:20 Why Martin looks 10 years ahead, not just 10 months, Martins philosophy on mentors and some of his and AndrewвҖҷs mentors throughout the years. 27:50 MartinвҖҷs natural curiosity and how that leads him to new skills, taking a year off when he turned 50 and spending that time renovating his own house. 32:40 Leading from the back of the room, doing a shred a couple of times a year to keep good habits and the start of the shred being a terrible feeling until you get used to it. 36:40 Spending the first half of your life training to be healthy for the second half, how Martin researches guests for the KPMG podcast and Gary WindgroveвҖҷs quote about Martin. 40:20 MartinвҖҷs partner SuzanneвҖҷs thoughts on Martin, doing the Sydney to Hobart race makes the rest of the year seem easier and learning leadership skills from yacht racing. 47:10 MartinвҖҷs disastrous first Hawkesbury kayak race, not being able to walk past a piece of rubbish without picking it up and starting work at Spotless during a time of crisis. 55:20 being under pressure from day 1 to do something clever at Spotless, some of Andrew and MartinвҖҷs favourite AFR cartoons from that time and Martins safety net being his family and his values. 1:02:40 Dealing with tall poppy syndrome, how consulting firms can adopt AI and using AI technology for the benefit of the customers. 1:09:45 MartinвҖҷs love for KPMG and all it has done for him, removing the hard limit of retiring at 58 in KPMG and how Martin is able to synthesise information so effectively. 1:20:00 Making sure to take time off and recharge, recognising the tough times Andrew and Martin have been through and you canвҖҷt control the wind but you can control the sails. РЈРҝРҫРјРёРҪР°Р»Рё:рҹ”ё РЎСӮР°СҖСӮ РёРҪСӮРөРҪСҒРёРІР° В«РҗСғСӮРөРҪСӮРёСҮРҪРҫРө Р»РёРҙРөСҖСҒСӮРІРҫ: СғРҝСҖР°РІР»РөРҪРёРө СҒРҫРұРҫР№ РёР·РҪСғСӮСҖРёВ» рҹ”ё РҡР°РҪР°Р» Р”Р°СҲРё РІ СӮРөР»РөРіСҖР°РјРө: DashaвҖҷs notes | Р»СҺРҙРё Рё СӮРөС…РҪРҫ рҹ”ё ABBA Voyage вҖ” СҲРҫСғ СҒ СҶРёС„СҖРҫРІСӢРјРё Р°РІР°СӮР°СҖР°РјРё СғСҮР°СҒСӮРҪРёРәРҫРІ ABBA, РҫСҒРҪРҫРІР°РҪРҪРҫРө РҪР° motion capture рҹ”ё РҡРҪРёРіР° В«Р’РөСҖРҪСғСӮСҢ СҚРҪРөСҖРіРёСҺ: РҡР°Рә РҪР°СғРәР° РҝРҫРјРҫРіР°РөСӮ Р¶РөРҪСүРёРҪРө СҒРҫС…СҖР°РҪРёСӮСҢ РјРҫР»РҫРҙРҫСҒСӮСҢ, Р·РҙРҫСҖРҫРІСҢРө Рё Р¶РёР·РҪРөРҪРҪСғСҺ СҒРёР»СғВ», РңРҫР»Р»Рё РңР°Р»СғС„ рҹ”ё РҡРҪРёРіР° В«РҗСғСӮРөРҪСӮРёСҮРҪР°СҸ РәРҫРјРјСғРҪРёРәР°СҶРёСҸВ», РҗР»РөРәСҒРөР№ РҳРІР°РҪРҫРІ Рё РЎРІРөСӮР° РЁРөРҙРёРҪР° рҹ”ё РһС…Р°Рҙ РқР°С…Р°СҖРёРҪ вҖ” РёР·СҖР°РёР»СҢСҒРәРёР№ С…РҫСҖРөРҫРіСҖР°С„, Р°РІСӮРҫСҖ Gaga-РјРөСӮРҫРҙР° рҹ”ё РӯРәСҒСӮР°СӮРёРә-РҙСҚРҪСҒ Рё 5 СҖРёСӮРјРҫРІ вҖ” РҝРҫРҝСғР»СҸСҖРҪСӢРө СӮРөР»РөСҒРҪСӢРө РҝСҖР°РәСӮРёРәРё/С„РҫСҖРјР°СӮСӢ СӮР°РҪСҶРөРІР°Р»СҢРҪРҫР№ РёРјРҝСҖРҫРІРёР·Р°СҶРёРё.

title: 25. Confidence, Courage and Resillence - A Magician's Story
podcast_name: Who Do You Think You Are Talking To Podcast
summary: How IrelandвҖҷs #1 Magician Turned Childhood Shyness into a Career of Confidence, Creativity, and Courage  Ever wonder how someone goes from being painfully shy to performing for thousands on stage, and loving it?  In this episode, I sit down with IrelandвҖҷs number one magician, Joe Daly, who opens up about the moment magic found him at six years old and the inspiring, twist-filled journey that followed. If youвҖҷve ever doubted your dreams or struggled with imposter syndrome, JoeвҖҷs story is the real-life plot twist you need to hear.  We speak about the emotional and practical side of pursuing a creative life, especially when the world tells you to take the вҖңsafeвҖқ path. Joe doesnвҖҷt just pull rabbits out of hats, he pulls wisdom from the hardest moments in life and shares it generously. Whether you're chasing a dream, rebuilding after disappointment, or just trying to find your voice again, this conversation is for you.  In this episode, we explore:    How Joe overcame childhood shyness through the power of magic    The moment a career guidance counsellor told him magic wasn't a вҖңreal jobвҖқвҖ”and how that fired him up even more    What it really means to invest in yourself when no one else gets the vision    How imposter syndrome showed up at the peak of his career and the one phrase that grounds him every time    The devastating Vegas "almost" and how losing it all led him to something even better    How collaboration and creative control can co-exist without burning out    The daily mindset habit that helps Joe stay focused and inspired (yes, even after rejection)    JoeвҖҷs story is the perfect reminder that your path might not unfold the way you thought it would, but that doesn't mean it's not the right one.  Tune in to hear how resilience, risk-taking, and radical belief in yourself can change everything. This episode will leave you motivated, grounded, and ready to step into your own spotlight.  Connect with Joe:    Joe on Instagram: @joe_thatmagician    Joe's website: closeupmagician.ie | kidsmagician.ie    Check out Joe's show, вҖҳJoe the Magician - LiveвҖҷat the 3Olympia on Sunday, 18th May -В LEARN MORE   Also you can watch JoeвҖҷs show вҖҳDizzy DeliveriesвҖҷ weekdays on RTГүjr  FREE Mental RehearsalВ     To get my free bonus mental rehearsal make sure you sign up to my weekly newsletter:В Sign up here

title: SMALL VICTORIES: Why tiny wins matter more than overnight transformations
summary: Ever planned a total life overhaul, only to burn out in three days and feel even worse? Yeah, us too. In this episode of LATE BLOOMERS, Rich and Rox get brutally honest about why we chase fantasy fixesвҖ”like crash diets, productivity sprints, or becoming a totally new person overnightвҖ”and why those plans always fall apart. From ADHD brains addicted to dopamine and all-or-nothing thinking, to trauma-based shame spirals and the lure of a brand new notebook, we unpack the psychology behind self-sabotage. But more importantly, we talk about the antidote: small victories. YouвҖҷll hear about money meltdowns, fantasy meal plans, unfinished wardrobes, and why building trust with yourself through micro-changes is the only sustainable way to grow. If youвҖҷve ever thought, вҖңI should have my life together by nowвҖқвҖ”this oneвҖҷs for you.

title: Toxic Positivity to Truth: Shame, Rage & Parenting Through Trauma w/ Alwynn
summary: What happens when the memories come backвҖ”but no one believes you? This is a story about shame, survival, and what it means to heal while raising the very children youвҖҷre terrified of hurting. In this episode, Andrea sits down with Alwynn, former host of Let Me Be Free, to talk about the long, messy road from toxic positivity to truth. From repressed memories and rage to parenting through trauma, this conversation is a raw look at what it really means to heal. рҹ”‘ Key Takeaways: Repressed trauma doesnвҖҷt stay buried forever вҖ” it shows up through the body, through emotional flashbacks, through parenting patterns, and through the parts of ourselves we donвҖҷt fully understand until weвҖҷre ready to remember.Toxic positivity is a survival strategy вҖ” and letting go of it can be the first step toward facing what weвҖҷve spent a lifetime avoiding.Parenting while healing is messy, triggering, and sacred work вҖ” and real repair starts with self-forgiveness and honesty, not perfection.Shame thrives in secrecy вҖ” and healing begins when we can name our experiences, be witnessed, and know that weвҖҷre not the only ones.Breaking the cycle means confronting family denial, rewriting narratives, and grieving the childhood we didnвҖҷt have вҖ” even when others would rather we stay quiet. в¬ҮпёҸDAMN THE JOIN SHITSHOW - ADULT CHILD HEALING COMMUNITYв¬ҮпёҸ https://theshitshow.mn.co 4 weekly zoom support groups (Sun 3: 30 pm EST, Mon 8:30 pm EST, Tues 1 pm EST, & Thurs 8:30 pm EST)Support community at your fingertips with discussion boards and chat рҹ“°SIGN UP FOR SHITSHOW NATION - ADULT CHILD NEWSLETTER рҹ‘©HELP A GIRL & CAT EATрҹҗұВ В  www.buymeacoffee.com/adultchild рҹӣҚпёҸSHITSHOW MERCHрҹӣҚпёҸ www.adultchildpodcast.com/shop рҹҺҷпёҸNEW TO THE PODрҹҺҷпёҸ How I learned I was an Adult Child - The Tale of 2 Brians THE LAUNDRY LIST - Common Characteristics of an Adult Child Follow Andrea on social www.instagram.com/adultchildpod www.tiktok.com/@adultchildpod https://www.youtube.com/@adultchildpod Our Sponsors: * Check out Happy Mammoth and use my code ADULTCHILD for a great deal: https://happymammoth.com * Check out La Pure and use my code ADULTCHILD for a great deal: https://www.lapurecosmetics.com Advertising Inquiries: https://redcircle.com/brands Privacy & Opt-Out: https://redcircle.com/privacy

summary: This is a PSA for your expansion! ItвҖҷs time to dial into our programming and rip the limited beliefs to shreds. title: 2. Project of your Life
podcast_name: Yellow Eyed Cats with Bernie Roth
summary: вҖңPipe dreams are okay, but they really get in the way of stuff.вҖқ вҖ” Professor Bernie Roth StanfordвҖҷs The Designer in Society isnвҖҷt your typical academic course вҖ” and BernieвҖҷs assigned project wasnвҖҷt your typical assignment. It was a bold challenge to stop thinking, start doing. In this episode, weвҖҷre joined by former student Janay Smith, who shares how that experience assisted her in facing later setbacks or as she puts it: when she got вҖңstuck with a wet fish in my faceвҖқ. Her biggest lesson from learning and occasionally sparring with Bernie? вҖңEverything in life is a choice.вҖқ Note: This episode includes swear words and some emotionally intense stories. Listener discretion advised. 00:16 Intro 01:27 BernieвҖҷs guidelines for the student projectsВ  02:47 Bernie-ism: Stop Thinking, Start Doing 09:25 Fooling yourself 10:29 JanayвҖҷs introduction 18:00 JanayвҖҷs project 20:05 вҖңвҖҠThat project made me schedule an attack for fear. title: A MOTIVE - Rain Dance - Andrew Tate. The Power of Visualization: Manifesting Success
summary: Andrew Tate explains the art of visualization and its role in achieving success. Whether you win or lose, imagining your ideal life with intensity fuels motivation and pushes you to work harder. Learn how to harness your mindвҖҷs potential and vividly picture the life you want to create. title: #263: The Hidden Treasures of Previewing
podcast_name: The Simplicity Parenting Podcast with Kim John Payne
summary: In this episode, Kim expands on the idea of previewing as a key parenting tool that brings calm and predictability to family life. He describes two types of previews: larger ones that look ahead to the day or week and smaller, moment-to-moment ones that guide children gently through transitions. Previewing helps children develop an inner picture of whatвҖҷs to come, which in turn reduces anxiety and fosters co-regulation. This shared picture builds trust, soothes nervous systems, and even supports healthy brain development by helping children move from stress to calm thinking. Previewing becomes a quiet but powerful way of helping a child feel safe and seen. вқӨпёҸВ Support the Podcast рҹ“‘В Simplicity Parenting Starter Kit рҹ“ІВ Request a Consultation with Kim John Payne рҹ“ҡВ Simplicity Parenting Book Store

title: Under the Noise: How Understanding Thought Changes Everything

Together, we explore:    Why our thoughts are not who we are    How emotions are created вҖ” and why they're temporary    The suffering that comes from resisting our feelings    The innate wisdom and peace that emerge when we understand the nature of thought    The miracle of simply being a conscious human    Your thoughts are not permanent truths вҖ” theyвҖҷre temporary experiences. Resistance to feelings creates suffering вҖ” non-resistance dissolves it. title: How to Personalize Your Healthspan Journey with Rachele Pojednic
summary: When you search online for ways to live a healthy life, you're bombarded by charismatic speakers, some with medical degrees or PhDs, promising miracle cures. From cancer treatments to fat-burning morning rituals and an endless array of supplements claiming to unlock the fountain of youth, the noise is overwhelming.  Some of these claims contain a grain of truth; others are pure fiction. But in nearly every case, they distract from what truly mattersвҖ”focusing on minor details that wonвҖҷt meaningfully impact your health tomorrow, next year, or even a decade from now.  The fundamentals of health, fitness, and longevity may not be flashy, but they are essential. In this weekвҖҷs podcast, we hear from a PhD researcher and educator whose work focuses on healthspan and longevity.  Listen and learn:   Why the science community often lacks the communication and marketing skills for their ideas How quickly simply-sounding health data can be misinterpreted The real risk of sarcopenia (muscle loss) and what to do about it Why your health in later life is often the compounding of small behaviors over years or decades  Links:  www.rachelepojednic.com  @rachelepojednic  About Our Guest:  Dr. Rachele Pojednic is a researcher specializing in human performance and healthy aging, serving as the Director of Scientific Research at Restore Hyperwellness and a faculty member in StanfordвҖҷs Program in Human Biology. She also leads education at Stanford Lifestyle Medicine and is a research affiliate at Harvard Medical SchoolвҖҷs Institute of Lifestyle Medicine. With a PhD in nutritional biochemistry from Tufts and postdoctoral training at Harvard, her work explores the impact of nutrition, supplementation, and physical activity on muscle physiology and longevity.В   Like the Show?   Leave us a review Check out ourВ YouTube channel

title: (#77) Rediscovering Wholeness:  A Somatic Approach to Identity, Creativity & Self-Trust

title: Self-care when you feel like it the least. 3-part emotional resilience script that helps you make better choices when feeling low.

title: You Are At Your Best When You Are Not Thinking
summary: Which is easier, to think about doing something, to annoy yourself that you're thinking about doing it instead of doing it, to get frustrated and, perhaps, even guilty that you haven't done what you know you need to do or... to just do what you need to do? Which is easier? You know that answer.  What you don't know is that everything, other than doing what needs to be done, is the result of thought, your thought, repetitive thought, the thoughts that hold you back. And, then, what you probably will not know is that, when you actually go and do what needs to be done, you'll do it through the fog of thought that our brain's "Autopilot" uses to enable us make it through the day unharmed. Let's go further.  You'll probably be aware that you have strengths and weaknesses - probably more weaknesses (there's a reason for that).  What you won't be aware of is that neither your strengths nor weaknesses are real.  Simply more thinking. You really need to listen to this episode - it will awaken you to what is truly possible in your life - starting now.

title: 441: Unshakeable Faith in Your Future
summary: The Power of Believing: How to Cultivate Unshakable Faith in Yourself   To have unshakable faith in your future, you need three types of faith:    Faith in Your Future: Believe in your vision and choose thoughts that make your future feel exciting and aligned.    Faith in Yourself: Trust yourself and keep promises to yourself. This builds self-confidence and reduces fear of the future.    Faith in the Process: Trust that every experience, good or bad, is part of your evolution. Life's challenges are stepping stones to something meaningful.     Host Tonya Leigh explores the transformative power of unshakable faith in shaping our actions, mindset, and resilience during uncertain times. Tonya shares her excitement about an upcoming workshop focused on cultivating inner calm and confidence, emphasizing the importance of believing in our ability to rise and thrive. She reflects on her personal journey of shedding subconscious patterns over the past six months, revealing how this growth has prepared her to bring a renewed perspective to the workshop.   Tune in to discover how to strengthen your faith in the future and embrace the changes necessary for personal development.   Talking Points:   01:15 - Shedding Layers  03:35 - Excitement About the Future  06:16 - Believing Before Seeing  08:47 - Unshakable Faith in Your Direction  11:49 - Three Types of Faith Needed  17:09 - Keeping Promises to Yourself  20:00 - Criticism and Staying True to Yourself  24:17 - Falling in Love with the Process  25:11 - Connection Between Faith and a Calm Body  26:04 - Visualization Exercise   Quotes:  "None of us are here to live a small life."  "More than ever, we need to have faith in our futures because faith acts as an anchor during uncertainty."  "Why not take back imaginative control as to what you want to think and believe about your future?"  "The beauty of having unshakable faith in yourself is that you stop being afraid of the future."  "It's when we doubt our future, when we doubt ourselves and we doubt the process. That's when we don't show up."  "Your future is shaped by the faith that you practice today." title: The weight of history: How Holocaust trauma shapes Jewish responses to Israel
summary: Disclaimer: This episode contains personal opinions and perspectives on complex geopolitical issues. The views expressed by the host and guest do not represent an official position on the Israel-Palestine conflict and are shared as part of an exploration of psychological and emotional dimensions of identity, trauma, and human connection. This conversation is meant to provoke thought and empathy rather than promote any particular political stance. In this thought-provoking episode, I speak with journalist and writer Joseph Dana about the nuanced relationship between Jewish identity, trauma, and Israel-Palestine. Joseph shares his personal journey from American Reform Judaism to Israeli immigration and eventual residence in Ramallah, offering unique insights as someone who has lived on both sides of the conflict. We explore how Holocaust trauma continues to shape modern Jewish identity, creating what Joseph describes as a "continuation of the Holocaust period" rather than a post-Holocaust era. This trauma response helps explain the powerful emotional connections many diaspora Jews feel toward Israel, even as the state pursues policies that may not serve Jewish safety worldwide. Joseph challenges the Zionist notion that Israel represents "the end of Jewish history," suggesting instead that we need to reconnect with the broader Jewish historical experience. This conversation examines identity, intergenerational trauma, and the possibility of finding common humanity across divides that often seem unbridgeable. Resources: Support humanitarian aid in Palestine: https://giftofthegivers.org/where-we-work/palestine/ Recommended Reading: A eulogy for Israeli activist Ezra Nawi: https://www.tabletmag.com/sections/israel-middle-east/articles/in-memory-ezra-nawi South African Jews and Palestine: https://newlinesmag.com/argument/south-africas-stance-on-palestine-opens-questions-about-apartheid-and-history/ The Crisis of Zionism: https://www.thenationalnews.com/arts-culture/books/the-crisis-of-zionism-undeterred-by-unavoidable-realities-1.439490 Follow Joseph Dana Website: https://www.josephdana.org/about Twitter: https://x.com/ibnezra?lang=en Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ibnezra/reels/ Follow Carly on: Website: https://onthecouchwithcarly.com/ YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCfBi56xQookfRGL3zvWVzCg Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/onthecouchwithcarly/?hl=en Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/onthecouchwithcarly/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@onthecouchwithcarly Apple Podcasts: https://podcasts.apple.com/za/podcast/on-the-couch-with-carly/id1497585376 Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/3t7A2FMnISQ2fz9D5p0Xuw

title: Mom tribe as hype women: Celebrating the unseen work of motherhood
summary: In this heartfelt solo episode, I explore what we've lost in modern motherhood - the village, the tribe, the collective female support that once surrounded new mothers. Patriarchal systems have stripped value from traditionally feminine roles, leaving mothers isolated in their achievements and struggles. From my therapy practice, I witness mothers performing daily acts of heroism that go unseen: handling potty training disasters with grace, soothing babies while maintaining professional conversations. These "invisible" achievements deserve celebration. The problem isn't that we're worse at mothering than previous generations - it's that we lack the chorus of voices telling us we're doing well. This episode honors all mothers - those who struggled with infertility, NICU moms, those questioning their abilities, and everyone showing up daily for their children. Join the #MomTribe movement by acknowledging and celebrating the mothers in your lives. title: The art of fatherhood: Justice Mukheli on redefining masculinity
summary: In this powerful episode, I sit down with artist, filmmaker and photographer Justice Mukheli, who I've followed for over 12 years. Known for his groundbreaking project "I See A Different You" which beautifully reshapes African narratives, Justice shares his transformative journey into fatherhood with his seven-month-old daughter. We explore how this experience has fundamentally changed him as a man and artist, challenging traditional ideas of masculinity and caregiving. Justice opens up about the reality behind the fantasy of parenthood - from the humbling experience of watching his wife's pregnancy to the intense mental load of caring for a newborn. He offers candid insights about the privilege of being a man in parenting, the challenges of balancing work with being present, and how his relationship with his daughter has influenced his creative work. His powerful perspective: "Do what feels right for you. Nothing is wrong." summary: Send us a text In Episode #16 of Chatting with Slava the Jordanian, host Dr. Slava Abdelrehim invites listeners into a heartfelt and necessary conversation about mental well-being within the Arab immigrant experience. Joined by her cohost JD Yusef and special guest co-hostВ  Dr. Ihab Abdelrehim, the trio dives into how emotional health is shapedвҖ”and often silencedвҖ”by cultural norms, spiritual foundations, and traditional belief systems. Together, they explore: How mental health was (or wasnвҖҷt) part of their upbringing.The tension between emotional vulnerability and cultural expectations.How spirituality and tribal connection often served as the unspoken support system in times of struggle.This episode opens the door to a conversation many have quietly held insideвҖ”what does healing look like when itвҖҷs filtered through heritage, honor, and faith? And how can todayвҖҷs generation of Arab immigrants and their children begin to normalize seeking help, embracing emotions, and redefining strength? title: 166: How do the elite develop well-being? with Lisa Trainor
summary: In this episode of the Supporting Champions Podcast, IвҖҷm joined by Lisa Trainor вҖ“ a former varsity athlete turned researcher вҖ“ to explore the evolving landscape of athlete well-being. Drawing from her own experiences and her academic work, Lisa discusses how injury and major disruptions like the postponement of the Tokyo Games can influence an athleteвҖҷs psychological state. She offers insight into the distinction between hedonic (feeling good) and eudaemonic (functioning well and finding purpose) well-being, and how each contributes to sustainable elite performance.   We unpack key themes including the importance of psychological safety, the value of self-confidence and identity in sport, and how athletes can remain fulfilled even when faced with setbacks. Lisa provides a compelling case for redefining success beyond medals and rankings вҖ“ encouraging us to view thriving and performance not as opposites, but as intertwined outcomes of a well-supported sporting experience.   рҹ”Қ Key Takeaways: вң… Well-being isnвҖҷt just about feeling goodвҖ”itвҖҷs about functioning meaningfully. вң… Injury and disruption can offer valuable moments for reflection and growth. вң… Athletes may perform without well-being, but itвҖҷs not sustainable long-term. вң… Psychological safety is a foundational component of thriving environments. вң… Pursuing purpose and personal agency is central to eudaemonic well-being.   рҹ“Ң Chapters: 00:00 Introduction and Background of Lisa Trainor 03:06 Injury, Well-Being, and Athlete Support 05:53 Coping with the Tokyo Games Postponement 08:49 Understanding Well-Being: Hedonic vs. Eudaemonic Perspectives 11:45 The Role of Well-Being in Elite Athlete Performance 15:03 Exploring Athlete Well-Being Components 17:59 The Interplay of Eudaemonia and Hedonia in Sport 21:00 Research Insights on Athlete Well-Being 23:55 Conclusion and Practical Applications 35:49 Understanding Athlete Well-Being 42:13 The Role of Performance in Well-Being 50:51 Purpose and Fulfillment in Sport 57:09 Psychological Safety and Relationships 01:05:21 Developing Eudaemonic Well-Being   рҹҺ§ Whether youвҖҷre an athlete, coach, psychologist, or someone passionate about high performance and mental health, this conversation with Lisa offers rich insight into the deeper layers of what it means to thrive in sport.   рҹ”” Subscribe, like, and share to stay updated on future episodes. рҹҺ“ Use code SC10 for 10% off all Supporting Champions Courses: https://www.supportingchampions.co.uk/onlinecourse/#courses   #AthleteWellbeing #SportsPsychology #MentalHealth #Eudaemonia #HighPerformance #SupportingChampions

title: 397: The Hidden Link Between Your Nervous System And Manifestation
summary: вҖңWhen your nervous system is grounded and your heart is open, you become a magnet for miracles."В В   What if the reason your manifestations arenвҖҷt coming true isnвҖҷt because youвҖҷre doing it wrong, but because your nervous system doesnвҖҷt feel safe enough to receive them?  In todayвҖҷs episode of Soul Talk, we go beyond mindset and vision boards to explore the real missing link in manifestation: your nervous system. Most of us try to manifest from a place of hustle, willpower, and positive thinking. But if your body is stuck in survival mode, wired for fear, protection, or scarcity, then it will subconsciously reject the very things you say you want.  This episode is an invitation to slow down and look within. To recognize that trauma, even from childhood, can condition your energy and block abundance, love, and expansion, not because youвҖҷre broken, but because your system is trying to keep you safe.  Whether youвҖҷre stuck at an upper limit, sabotaging your success, or feeling disconnected from your dreams, this conversation offers a new perspective. Manifestation isnвҖҷt just about calling things in, itвҖҷs about becoming the version of you that can hold them. And that begins with safety in the body.  Tune in to learn powerful tools for regulating your nervous system, grounding your energy, and becoming a true vibrational match to the life you desire.  Timestamps:В    (00:03:39) - Why mindset isnвҖҷt enough for manifestation. (00:05:43) -The nervous system as a foundation for manifesting. (00:08:07) - How past trauma programs our nervous system. (00:10:19) - The 3 nervous system states and how they affect your energy. (00:12:28) - Why do we sabotage our dreams even when we think we want them. (00:13:47) -В В How unresolved trauma creates time loops and mixed messages. (00:15:14) -В  The key: energetic coherence between intention and emotion. (00:17:27) - Practical nervous system regulation tools for manifestation. (00:19:58) - Live guided practice: regulate, ground, and visualize. (00:22:35) - The breakthrough question for true transformation.  Some questions I ask:В    Why do we struggle to manifest even when doing вҖңeverything rightвҖқ? How does unresolved trauma affect our ability to receive? What nervous system state are you operating from, fight/flight, freeze, or safety? Are you energetically aligned with what you're asking for? What does your body need to feel safe enough to receive your dreams?  In this episode, you will learn about:В    Why mindset alone is not enough to manifest what you want. How your nervous system and past trauma impact manifestation. The three nervous system states, how they shape your energy, and how to properly regulate it. Why people sabotage success, love, or abundance, which unconsciously makes them stagnant. Practical tools to regulate your nervous system and align energetically. How to bridge the gap between where you are and where you want to be. How to stop sending mixed signals to the universe to access abundance. title: 137. Quiet Observations, Quick Corrections. Parenting Mindset You'll Love
summary: Choose quiet observations and follow it with quick, intentional corrections. summary: Full episode now on the Living Proof Patreon. Ali Boulala is a skate icon who came to prominence through his association with Flip Skateboards in the late 1990s. He was one of the original members of the вҖңPiss DrunxвҖқ, a notorious skate crew that embodied a raw, punk-infused ethos that left a lasting imprint on skateboarding culture.В  On March 7, 2007, Ali Boulala was involved in a tragic motorcycle accident in Melbourne, Australia. Boulala sustained severe head injuries, leading to a coma that lasted four months. He underwent extensive rehabilitation to relearn basic functions such as walking and eating independently. Due to the accident, he was convicted of culpable driving and sentenced to four years in prison, serving two years before being released and deported back to Sweden. The accident had a profound impact on Boulala's life, leading to years of substance abuse and personal turmoil. In 2013, he began a journey toward recovery and has maintained sobriety since then. Finding new purpose in life and moving forward with a renewed spirit, he has channeled his creative energy into music.В  http://patreon.com/livingproofnewyork http://livingproofnewyork.com

title: #289 Business and Bear & Moo with Hannah Porter
summary: This week I get to chat with Hannah, from the New Zealand Brand, Bear & Moo. We real-talk about business, cloth nappies and everything in between!  Hannah says: I am the Founder & Director of Bear & Moo, an online store based in Hamilton for your everyday family.В  As a new Mum, I found there were lots of little costs that added up.В  One of the things that I used to hate spending money on was nappies, so I looked into cloth nappies. They just made sense to me and so I wanted to find a way to make them more accessible to kiwi families.В  Since launching Bear & Moo in 2018 weвҖҷve also created our own kids clothing range called Hello Poppet and added a huge number of products to the Bear & Moo range like change mats, bibs, wet bags, day bags and recently silicone toys.В  My husband Richard and I now have 3 kids, 4 including Bear & Moo, and our youngest has just toilet trained so we are no longer a cloth nappy family but I absolutely adore helping other families get started and save a ton of money. IвҖҷve built an epic community of wonderful customers and love sharing the behind the scenes of running a business, juggling parenthood and trying to squeeze a moment in there for me too! title: The Power of Influence
summary: What is Influence?Influence is the ability to affect someoneвҖҷs thoughts, feelings, or actions.ItвҖҷs not about controlling others.ItвҖҷs about inspiring them to take action or trust your guidance. Why Influence Matters in Real EstateBuyers and sellers rely onВ yourВ expertise to make big financial decisions.They are looking for someone they feel confident following.Influence builds trust faster than facts alone. Example: Two agents give the same market report. The agent who confidently recommends a clear path forward earns the listing. How Realtors Use Influence DailyDuring Consultations: Guiding clients toward realistic expectations.Pricing a Home: Helping sellers understand market value vs emotional value.Negotiations: Persuading the other side to accept terms favorable to your client.Referrals: Influencing people to recommend you to others.Social Media: Building a brand peopleВ wantВ to engage with. Positive vs Negative InfluencePositive = Empowering, Educating, Building TrustNegative = Manipulation, Fear Tactics, Pressure Example of Positive Influence: Helping a first-time buyer feel confident buying despite fear. Example of Negative Influence: Pushing a buyer into a home they donвҖҷt love just to close a sale. How to Strengthen Your InfluenceBe Authentic. People can spot fake energy fast.Be Consistent. Stay active where your audience sees you often.Educate First. People follow those who teach them, not those who sell to them.Show Proof. Share client wins, testimonials, and success stories.Listen More Than You Talk. Understand theirВ realВ needs before advising. Thought-Provoking Questions for Group DiscussionWhen have you been positively influenced by someone in business? What stood out?Have you ever been negatively influenced? What did you learn from it?How can you shift from вҖңsellingвҖқ to вҖңguidingвҖқ more in your client conversations?What small change could you make this week to increase your influence?

title: #264 Radical Listening | Robert Biswas-Diener, PhD
summary: In this insightful episode of SuperPsyched, psychologist Dr. Adam Dorsay sits down with Dr. Robert Biswas-Diener, co-author of the book 'Radical Listening: The Art of True Connection.' title: #161- Paul Tembunde explain 4 Life Quadrants That Lead to Health, Wealth, and Fulfillment
podcast_name: Peak Performance Perspectives with Mark Talukdar
summary: In this inspiring episode of the Peak Performance Perspectives Podcast, host Mark Talukdar sits down with mindset and success coach Paul Tembunde to talk about how to build confidence, overcome fear, and live with purpose. If you want to grow in life, business, or relationships, this episode is your guide! Paul shares his powerful personal story from humble beginnings to starting a successful home care business and becoming a sought-after entrepreneurship mentor and personal development speaker. He explains how positive thinking, daily habits, and faith helped him rise above fear and live a purpose-driven life.Paul talks about the four вҖңlife quadrantsвҖқ he lives by: health and wellness, love and relationships, career/vocation, and time and money freedom. His approach to life coaching and entrepreneurship development is simple: focus on getting better every day. Paul also shares how he used mindset strategies to grow his home care company from a basement startup into a 23-year success story. Learn how he and his wife took big risks, built resilience, and created a life of meaning and impact. рҹ“Ҳ Whether youвҖҷre on a self-improvement journey, starting a new business, or just want to boost motivation, this episode is full of action-ready wisdom and real-life tools. Offer Check out our Website for details:www.premieredestiny.comwww.askjoanandpaul.com Bio: Paul Tembunde is a business executive, mindset and transformation coach, and co-founder of Premiere Destiny LLC. He lives in the Washington, D.C. Metro Area with his wife and high school sweetheart, Joan, and their three adult children. After graduating cum laude with a degree in Business Finance, Paul pursued various jobs before following his entrepreneurial instincts. In 2002, driven by a shared passion to serve, he and Joan took a leap of faith and purchased a homecare agency franchiseвҖ”funded by a $12,000 credit card advance. Though they lacked business experience, JoanвҖҷs background in senior care and their shared determination helped them build a successful and respected agency. Over two decades, they have served thousands of seniors and provided employment to just as many. Their journey has been marked by personal growth, deep community impact, and a broadened vision of whatвҖҷs possible. They credit much of their success to the foundational training and support from their franchisor. In time, Paul began to feel a deeper calling. He sought something that would challenge him, expand his consciousness, and create greater impact. This led him to the personal development world, where a yearlong mentorship program sparked a profound transformation in his thinking and purpose. The program awakened in him a deep belief in personal agency, abundance, and the power of mindset. As his outlook shifted, Joan joined him on this path, and together they began integrating these principles into their businessвҖ”with outstanding results. Motivated by their own transformation, Paul and Joan founded Premiere Destiny LLCвҖ”a coaching company designed to inspire others to uncover their potential. Their mission is to вҖңenlighten, empower, and inspireвҖқ individuals to awaken their inner greatness, tap into the abundance of life, and realize their true potential. Paul believes we all have a stronger, more capable version of ourselves waiting to be released. Through Premiere Destiny, he and Joan guide others in becoming that versionвҖ”equipped with vision, clarity, and purpose. title: The Psychology of Sleep with Dr. Beverley David - Spring 2025
summary: In this episode, Gina and Dr. Beverley are talking psychology and sleep. Dr. Beverley breaks down why sleep is more than just restвҖ”it's brain maintenance. She explains how sleep affects everything from your mood to your hormones to your ability to make solid choices during the day. If youвҖҷve ever felt like youвҖҷre doing everything right but still struggling, your sleep might be the missing piece. From why you canвҖҷt actually вҖңcatch upвҖқ on lost sleep to how your daily habits shape your nights, this conversation shifts the way you think about winding down. Dr. B also gets into whatвҖҷs happening in each sleep stage, whether or not working out late is a good idea, and how to track your sleep without adding pressure. PlusвҖ”spoiler alertвҖ”men and women do experience sleep differently. The takeaway? Sleep isnвҖҷt just part of your wellness routineвҖ”it is the routine. Dr. Beverley is a Clinical Psychologist registered with the College of Psychologists of Ontario. She also holds a Ph.D. in Sleep Research (Insomnia) and a Master's in Health Psychology. title: The Danger of Spiritual Blind Spots: Are We Replicating The World We Seek To Heal?
summary: Azrya sits down with the incredible Rha Goddess at our beautiful neighbouring retreat center in Mexico, MUSA. title: Master Your Focus and Achieve Anything!
summary: Life isn't about chasing happinessвҖ”it's about being strong, respected, and valuable. If you're focused on "feeling good," you're setting yourself up for failure. Happiness is a byproduct of achievement, not the goal itself. You donвҖҷt wake up and ask,В Am I happy?В You wake up and ask,В What must be done? Men earn respect, not sympathy. No one cares if you're happy. YouвҖҷre judged by what you build, what you conquer, and how you handle adversity. Struggle is the price of greatness. Weak men chase comfort. Strong men embrace hardship and come out the other side as winners. So make a choiceвҖ”suffer as an unknown nobody, or suffer to become someone worth remembering. The world owes you nothing. title: Escaping the Figure Life Out Paradox
summary: Start Your Transformation NowВ  Are you stuck trying to вҖңfigure life outвҖқ before taking your next step? In this episode, Jim Fortin unpacks the illusion behind this common mindset and reveals why overthinking keeps so many people trapped in a cycle of confusion, frustration, and inaction. Through real-life stories and his own experience, Jim explains why waiting for clarity often leads nowhereвҖ”and how taking inspired action is the true path to freedom and fulfillment.В  Jim explores the psychological trap of needing all the answers before moving forward, a mindset that holds countless people in place. He introduces a powerful shift: clarity doesnвҖҷt come before actionвҖ”it comes because of it. Whether youвҖҷre trying to make a big life move or simply feel stuck, this episode offers the key to break free from the paradox and reclaim your momentum.В  If youвҖҷve spent far too long trying to think your way to clarity, this episode is the wake-up call to stop overthinking and start living.В  What YouвҖҷll Discover in This Episode:В     Why Trying to вҖңFigure It OutвҖқ DoesnвҖҷt Work (01:05)В Jim explains why most people stay stuck not because they canвҖҷt move, but because they believe they need all the answers before doing so.В     JohnвҖҷs Story: The Paralysis of Overthinking (03:00)В A powerful example of how overthinkingвҖ”and the need for certaintyвҖ”can keep someone stagnant for years.В     The Real Solution: Action Creates Clarity (06:45)В Jim shares the philosophy вҖңsomething leads to somethingвҖқ and shows how taking any action can unlock new opportunities and awareness.В     How to Make Calculated, Inspired Risks (10:30)В Jim contrasts reckless risk with intuitive, aligned decision-makingвҖ”and how to know the difference.В     When Analytical Thinking Becomes the Enemy (17:50)В A discussion on why too much thinking actually blocks intuition, inspiration, and growth.В     The Heart-Led Way to Move Forward (23:40)В Jim introduces a more effective approach to decision-makingвҖ”one guided by joy, peace, and your inner knowing.В  Listen, apply, and enjoy!В  Transformational TakeawayВ  You donвҖҷt need to вҖңfigure it all outвҖқ to start moving forward. In fact, the longer you wait for perfect clarity, the further you drift from the life you want. Clarity is born from actionвҖ”not thought. Trust that taking one small, inspired step will reveal the next. Life always supports movement. And remember: everything always has been, is now, and always will be fine.В  LetвҖҷs Connect:В  Instagram | Facebook | YouTube | LinkedInВ  LIKED THE EPISODE?В  If you're the kind of person who likes to help others, then share this with your friends and family. If you have found value, they will too. Please leave a review on Apple Podcasts so we can reach more people.В  Listening on Spotify? Please leave a comment below. We would love to hear from you!В  With gratitude,В Jim

title: Productivity Smarts 101 - How to Make Life Simple, Happy, and Successful: The Taoist Way
summary: In this episode of Productivity Smarts, host Gerald J. Leonard explores the intersection of ancient wisdom and modern productivity with Gerard Wouters, a Taoist therapist and former financial manager whose unorthodox methods heal trauma in just two sessions. With over 28 years of experience, Gerard reveals how Taoist principlesвҖ”like deciphering setbacks as messages and leading through harmony, not pressureвҖ”can transform overwhelm into flow.В В   В   From his shocking body-mind therapy breakthroughs to surviving 150 daily calls as a corporate leader, GerardвҖҷs stories prove that real productivity starts with self-awareness, boundaries, and the courage to say "no."В В   В   Tune in to discover why managing "people, not numbers" unlocks peak performanceвҖ”and how to spot the hidden meaning behind lifeвҖҷs obstacles.В В   В   What We Discuss    [00:02:01] Introduction to Gerard Wouters: Taoist therapist and authorВ В     [00:04:41] The body-mind connection: Where trauma hides (shoulders = anger, pelvis = abuse)В В     [00:07:10] GerardвҖҷs 2-session therapy vs. years of conventional treatmentВ В     [00:10:26] Fear vs. danger: Why "spiders vanish when you unlearn fear"В В     [00:13:06] Taoist flow: How setbacks guide you (flat tire = donвҖҷt meet that friend!)В В     [00:17:22] Leadership hack: The "3-minute rule" for emails/callsВ В     [00:19:12] The power of "no"вҖ”why boundaries create respectВ В     [00:23:31] "Stop managing numbers; nurture people": Teams thrive without pressureВ В     [00:26:38] Where to find GerardвҖҷs book and workВ В     В   Notable Quotes    [00:03:46] вҖңThe treatment that I developed is absolutely unique and it helps people heal from all kinds of mental issues in two-hour sessions or a two-day workshop.вҖқ вҖ” Gerard Wouters    [00:10:41] вҖңDanger is a reality, but fear is a choice, and that's how it is. You choose if you fear something or not.вҖқ вҖ” Gerard Wouters    [00:22:43] вҖңBy saying no, you create respect. We are only human, not robots. You need to set priorities and take care of yourself.вҖқ вҖ” Gerard Wouters    [00:26:38] вҖңStop guiding and directing based on numbers because you don't work with numbers; you work with people. When people are comfortable and happy in their job, they will produce more automatically without pressure.вҖқ вҖ” Gerard Wouters    В   Our Guest  Gerard Wouters is a Dutch Taoist therapist, speaker, and former financial manager who blends ancient philosophy with pragmatic healing. His groundbreaking therapy resolves deep trauma in two sessions, and his book, How to Make Life Simple, Happy & Successful, redefines productivity through presence and self-trust.В   В   Resources  В   Gerard Wouters  В     Website - https://taoistisch.nl    LinkedIn - https://nl.linkedin.com/in/gerard-wouters-1062231    Instagram - https://www.instagram.com/wouters.gerard?igsh=Nm16c3ozYjhkeTln    Book -  How to Make Life Simple, Happy, and Successful: The Taoist Way by Gerard Wouters    В   Productivity Smarts Podcast    Website - productivitysmartspodcast.com    В   Gerald J. title: What Dating Securely Has Taught Me
summary: In this personal episode, I take you behind the scenes of a recent dating experience that stirred up both growth and grief вҖ” and revealed just how far IвҖҷve come on my attachment healing journey. As someone who used to operate from a fearful avoidant (leaning anxious) attachment style, I now move through dating with much more self-trust, clarity, and compassion. But that doesnвҖҷt mean the old patterns donвҖҷt still surface вҖ” especially when connection, chemistry, and unmet needs collide. IвҖҷll walk you through how I navigated this dynamic from a secure place, the conversations I had, and ultimately, how I honored myself enough to walk away when alignment wasnвҖҷt there вҖ” even when it felt good in the moment. If youвҖҷve ever struggled to balance attraction with long-term compatibility, or felt pulled between your heart and your values, this episode is for you. Takeaways include: The difference between chemistry and compatibility How to identify your unmet needs in real time Why even "kind and available" people might not be the right fit What it looks like to actually date from your secure self How to exit with integrity when your paths donвҖҷt align рҹҺ§ Plus: Only 2 more days to join the My Secure Self Membership for just $1 your first month! Get access to group coaching, monthly workshops, and tools to help you build secure, aligned relationships HERE. Looking for 1:1 support, learn more about attachment coaching here.

title: 108. The Art of Simplified Success: Scaling to Nearly $3M with Less Hustle and More Flow
summary: In this deeply personal and behind-the-scenes episode of Plenty, Kate shares the journey of how her company scaled to just shy of $3 million in revenueвҖ”not by doing more, but by doing less. Recorded live at Relaxed Money Live, Kate walks us through the exact steps she and her team took to transition from hustle and complexity to clarity and flow. From living out of a Prius and launching a $97 course that changed everything, to recovering from business debt and letting go of multiple revenue streams, Kate unpacks the surprising truth: simplicity is the most powerful growth strategy. She reveals how one offer, one problem, one customer, and one sales system helped her not only grow her income but significantly increase her profitabilityвҖ”the part that actually changes your life. If youвҖҷve ever felt overwhelmed, overcommitted, or like youвҖҷre reinventing the wheel in your work, this episode is a breath of fresh air. YouвҖҷll hear real numbers, real lessons, and a clear invitation to shift from overdoing to potent, focused action. YouвҖҷll learn: Why 78% of KateвҖҷs revenue came from people who had been on her list for less than 6 monthsHow simplicity created more revenue and more peaceThe somatic and emotional work Kate did to feel safe holding more successThe liberating power of building a business that truly feels as good as it looksThis is more than a story about moneyвҖ”itвҖҷs a lesson in alignment, sustainability, and leading with integrity in the age of overflow. вҖңWeвҖҷre taught that more equals better. But in my experience, lessвҖ”when itвҖҷs alignedвҖ”is the secret to sustainable success.вҖқ вҖ“ Kate Northrup рҹҺӨ LetвҖҷs Dive into the Good Stuff on Plenty рҹҺӨ (00:33) Journey of Starting an Online Business(01:07) Growth and Profit Margins(02:24) Understanding Revenue Sources(06:44) Challenges of Overcomplication(07:36) Simplicity as a Growth Strategy(10:01) Lessons from Past Mistakes(11:05) Personal Growth and Business Evolution(12:31) Creating and Selling Courses(21:08) Crisis and Recovery in Business(26:31) Financial Restructuring and Profitability(29:30) Healing Relationships with Money(38:50) Successful Launch of Relaxed Money(43:44) Focus on Profitability Over Revenue(49:11) The Future of Business Simplification

title: 81: Network Marketing (The Catalyst to the Success You're Craving)
podcast_name: Expand and Evolve with Carly Pinchin
summary: Lets talk about the importance of redefining success and failure in network marketing. We will always feel like we are coming up short or failing if we only measured our success by monetary achievements... I wanted to share a few examples of people who maybe haven't seen the tangible business growth or made it to the top of the company but did rediscover their value and self-worth. And maybe you haven't grown to the top of the company yet, but you have found the ability to hold yourself accountable, ask for help or stepped outside your comfort zone in ways you never could have imagined.  So i encourage you to embrace your unique journey, step into facing your personal challenges, and reframe your perspectives on what success and failure means to you, because no matter where you are at in your business or journey, feeling like a failure will never lead to you experiencing the success you are looking for and the fulfilling life you deserve to live. KEY TAKEAWAYS: Success is not just about reaching the top of the company.Personal growth is a significant measure of success.Network marketing can open doors to new life perspectives.Facing personal challenges can lead to growth in business.Asking for help is crucial for success.Self-sabotage often stems from past conditioning.Building self-accountability is essential in entrepreneurship.Transformative personal growth can lead to improved self-worth.Relationships may change as individuals grow and evolve.Reframing negative thoughts can change your reality. ___ CONNECT WITH ME: INSTAGRAM - вҒ https://www.instagram.com/carlypinchin/ TIKTOK - вҒ https://www.tiktok.com/@carlypinchin YOUTUBE - вҒ https://www.youtube.com/channel/UChhOERGYhv-0s-KZtp9HosQ/  Become Empowered + Powerful as EFF by tapping into your feminine with this 6 week course I created to help women who feel they're constantly living in their mind tap back into the wisdom of their bodies and their hearts to attract their deepest desires toward them: вҒ  Empowered Feminine Course.вҒ  https://carly-pinchin.mykajabi.com/offers/FVEdM52q/checkout

title: My Childhood & Narcissistic Abuse Caused My Lyme Disease
summary: This is not the kind of story that wraps up neatly with a bow. Rebecca FraserвҖҷs story is raw, messy, and unfinished because healing after deep betrayal, family fracture, and years of abusive relationships with narcissists is never clean or simple. ItвҖҷs the kind of survival story that digs into the parts most people donвҖҷt want to talk about, the shame, the loneliness, the repeated heartbreak, and the unbearable weight of carrying trauma in your body for years.In this episode, Rebecca shares her lived experience of growing up in a home torn apart by infidelity and addiction, being cast as the вҖңproblem child,вҖқ shuffled into foster care as a teenager, and entering a series of romantic relationships that only deepened her wounds. Alongside this emotional pain, she has been fighting a long battle with Lyme disease, a battle she believes was intensified by the unrelenting stress and trauma she endured.We explore the nervous system scars left by betrayal trauma, the painful roles children are assigned within dysfunctional families, and the long-term impact of being repeatedly gaslit, invalidated, and silenced not just by partners, but by family and community. Rebecca speaks openly about the heartbreak of losing friendships, the feeling of being вҖңtoo muchвҖқ for others, and the internal struggle of not trusting herself to choose safe people anymore. DonвҖҷt ever be alone in your journey and make sure you come and join my private Facebook community, No Visible Bruises where you can connect with other survivors of narcissistic abuse, domestic abuse and coercive control:https://www.facebook.com/groups/novisiblebruises. Disclaimer: The views and opinions shared in this podcast are those of the individuals involved and are intended for informational and educational purposes only. They do not substitute professional or medical advice. If youвҖҷve been affected by anything discussed in todayвҖҷs episode, please consider reaching out to a qualified healthcare provider or mental health professional for support. YouвҖҷre not alone, and help is always available.

title: 4. Things Look Different
summary: In this episode, I open up about how life, relationships, careers, and even holidays can evolveвҖ”and still be completely right for where we are now. I share personal stories, including how IвҖҷve handled grief during MotherвҖҷs Day, navigated changes in my direct sales career, and adjusted my health routines as IвҖҷve grown older. These experiences have taught me that just because something looks different, doesnвҖҷt mean itвҖҷs wrong. In fact, embracing change has helped me feel more grounded and confident. My hope is that youвҖҷll use this podcast as a toolboxвҖ”filled with practical lessons and real-life insightsвҖ”to guide you through your own transitions and help you live a calm, intentional life.  "Things can look different and still be right." вҖ“ Trina Gray  Key Points:  Life changesвҖ”big or smallвҖ”can still align with who I am today. вҖңRightвҖқ for me now doesnвҖҷt mean perfect; it just means what fits in this season. Holidays have changed after personal loss, but they still hold space for gratitude. IвҖҷve accepted that relationships evolve, and thatвҖҷs part of growing with grace. My approach to health and self-care now looks different, and thatвҖҷs perfectly okay.  В   Connect with Trina:  Instagram | LinkedIn | Website

title: Why You Do What You Do: ADHD Meets the Enneagram
summary: Send us a text Exploring the Intersection of ADHD and the Enneagram with Angela from Your Space Reclaimed Angela is a professional organizer and Certified Enneagram Coach who helps overwhelmed familiesвҖ”especially parents of special needs kidsвҖ”create calm, clutter-free spaces. After moving 25+ times and raising her own daughter with Down syndrome, she knows how powerful an organized home can be. Angela also uses the Enneagram to help clients understand the вҖңwhyвҖқ behind their habits so they can make lasting change. Learn more at Your Space Reclaimed. Check Angela out on Instagram: @Yourspacereclaimed In this episode of 'Organizing an ADHD Brain,' the host talks about the value of understanding one's Enneagram type to better manage ADHD symptoms. This week features Angela, a professional organizer and Enneagram expert who is also a parent to a disabled child. They delve into how knowing your Enneagram type can provide insights into behaviors and motivations, which can be particularly helpful for those with ADHD. Angela shares her journey into professional organizing and provides practical tips on how different Enneagram types can approach organization and relationships. title: The Best Way to End Jealousy
summary: In 2020, Mel Robbins found herself spiraling with panic and fear in the thick of the pandemic. It was the same panic and anxiety she felt when her entire world fell apart in 2008, and she found herself knee-deep in $800,000 of debt. She saw herself in the mirror, raised her hand, and gave herself a high-five. She did it again the next day and the day after that. Her fear subsided, and The High 5 Habit was born. Morning Microdose is a podcast curated by Krista Williams and Lindsey Simcik, the hosts and founders of Almost 30, a global community, brand, and top rated podcast. With curated clips from the Almost 30 podcast, Morning Mircodose will set the tone for your day, so you can feel inspired through thought provoking conversationsвҖҰall in digestible episodes that are less than 10 minutes. Wake up with Krista and Lindsey, both literally and spiritually, Monday-Friday. If you enjoyed this conversation, listen to the full episode on Spotify here and on Apple here.

title: Episode 52 | The Little Ways We Show Up for Our Partners
summary: Hosted by Dr. Sarah Hensley, Specialized Social Psychologist, Attachment Theory Expert, and Founder & CEO of The Love Doc Relationship Coaching Services with Co-host Raina Butcher, Owner/CEO of Joyful Consulting, LLC.В  Welcome to "The Love Doc Podcast" where Host Dr. Sarah Hensley and her co-host Raina Butcher dive deep into the intricacies of love, attraction, attachment, relationships, and self-awareness. Dr. Hensley brings a wealth of knowledge and experience to help listeners navigate the complexities of modern romance. In each episode, Dr. Hensley tackles burning questions about love, relationships, and the mindвҖҷs complexities, drawing from her psychological research, real-life experiences, and her own individual expertise, to provide insightful perspectives and practical advice. Episode 52 вҖ“ The Little Ways We Show Up for Our Partners This episode explores one of the most powerful yet often overlooked truths in romantic relationships: itвҖҷs the small, consistent acts of connection that build true intimacy. Dr. Sarah Hensley breaks down how everyday micro-momentsвҖ”like offering a kind word, turning toward your partner when they speak, or expressing gratitude for ordinary tasksвҖ”become the building blocks of lasting emotional closeness. Rooted in psychological science and attachment theory, Dr. Hensley explains how emotional bonds are formed and strengthened through quiet, consistent presence rather than grand, infrequent gestures. These subtle interactionsвҖ”what we choose to notice, how we respond, and the tone we useвҖ”send powerful signals of care, attunement, and respect. When these small moments are neglected, emotional distance grows, often without either partner realizing it. Listeners will gain a deeper understanding of how love is shaped in the moment-to-moment choices we make in daily life. The success of a relationship doesnвҖҷt hinge on extravagant efforts, but on the willingness to show up in the вҖңnowвҖқвҖ”especially when itвҖҷs inconvenient or uncomfortable. Ultimately, The Little Ways We Show Up for Our Partners is a powerful reminder that love is reciprocal. ItвҖҷs the accumulation of everyday choicesвҖ”done with intentionвҖ”that fosters lasting connection, emotional safety, and relational success. Ultimately, The Little Ways We Show Up for Our Partners is a powerful reminder that love is reciprocal. ItвҖҷs not built in the highlight reelsвҖ”itвҖҷs built in the in-between. In the everyday. And when both partners learn to consistently show up for each other in these little ways, the relationship doesnвҖҷt just surviveвҖ”it thrives. Tune in to "The Love Doc Podcast" every Tuesday morning for candid conversations, expert guidance, and a deeper understanding of life, love and relationships in the modern world. title: 1077. Inherited Patterns Are Keeping You STUCK (& With Less Money): Emotional DNA & Generational Fear w/ Judy Wilkins-Smith
summary: Hey Heal Squad! Welcome to Part 1 of a special conversation with a familiar guest, Judy Wilkins-Smith, a leading expert in emotional DNA, systems work, and ancestral healing! Judy dives deep into how our unconscious family patterns (especially around money, fear, and identity) get passed down generation after generation. From emotional imprints to inherited beliefs, these вҖңinvisible loyaltiesвҖқ can keep us stuck in cycles of scarcity, self-sabotage, and even illness. This good news is, Judy explains how to recognize a generational pattern when itвҖҷs running your life, how to trace it back to its origin, and most importantly, how to shift it using elevated emotion and intentional language. We also explore how inherited trauma shapes your nervous system, how your relationship with money is rarely just yours, and why feeling safe is the starting point for real growth. This episode will change how you see your past AND give you tools to create a different future! Enjoy!!В  HEALERS & HEAL LINERS:В     Inherited patterns are running the show. Many of your thoughts, beliefs, and behaviorsвҖ”especially around money, fear, and identityвҖ”arenвҖҷt yours. TheyвҖҷre inherited through your family system and can keep you stuck until you become conscious of them.    Emotional DNA is just as powerful as physical DNA. The emotional patterns passed down from your ancestors impact your relationships, your career, and your health. Recognizing these patterns gives you the power to rewrite them.    Scarcity is often a family story. Money blocks and financial fears are frequently passed down through generations. Until you identify the root belief, youвҖҷll continue reliving the same cycle. -- HEAL SQUAD SOCIALS IG: https://www.instagram.com/healsquad/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@healsquadxmaria HEAL SQUAD RESOURCES: Heal Squad Website:https://www.healsquad.com/ Heal Squad x Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/HealSquad/membership Maria Menounos Website: https://www.mariamenounos.com My Curated MacyвҖҷs Page: Shop My MacyвҖҷs Storefront Prenuvo: Prenuvo.com/MARIA for $300 off Delete Me: https://bit.ly/43rkHwi В  code: SQUAD EMR-Tek Red Light: https://emr-tek.com/discount/Maria30 for 30% off JUDY WILKINS-SMITH RESOURCES: Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/judywilkinssmith/В  Website: https://judywilkins-smith.com/В  Book: https://www.amazon.com/Hidden-Power-Your-DNA-Genealogy/dp/B0D3414MVL  Check out her upcoming event: https://judywilkins-smith.com/events/breakthrough-dna/В  ABOUT MARIA MENOUNOS: Emmy Award-winning journalist, TV personality, actress, 2x NYT best-selling author, former pro-wrestler and brain tumor survivor, Maria MenounosвҖҷ passion is to see others heal and to get better in all areas of life. title: Lead Beyond Limits with Jennifer Koster (Part 2)
summary: Send us a text рҹҺ§ Episode 164: Lead Beyond Limits: Thriving with a Hidden Disability with Jennifer Koster (Part 2) In this second part of our conversation with Jennifer Koster, we talk through the deeper spiritual dimensions of her journey with HEDS and how her experiences have shaped her approach to leadership, vulnerability, and community. Jennifer shares powerful insights about overcoming internalized ableism and offers wisdom that extends far beyond those living with chronic conditions. We explore: The integral role of faith during both challenging and good timesThe importance of not underestimating yourself or your teamBalancing energy management with ambitious goalsThe power of vulnerability as a leadership toolRecognizing and addressing internalized ableismBuilding supportive communities that understand practical needsLeaving a meaningful legacy through presence and serviceрҹ”‘ Key takeaways: Sometimes we experience the most growth in our faith during the most difficult timesLeaders often underestimate both themselves and their teams - limiting vision limits possibilitiesEnergy management requires clarity about vision and values to know when to say "no"Vulnerability might feel uncomfortable but can be a powerful tool for connectionAddressing internalized ableism starts with identifying limiting beliefs and replacing them with empowering onesSupport comes in many forms - practical help can be just as valuable as emotional encouragementрҹ’Ў Quotes to remember:В  "Sometimes I listen to my body and sometimes I tell it because if I listened all the time I wouldn't be making any progress toward any goals." - Jennifer Koster "Don't underestimate what you're capable of." - Jennifer Koster "We're built for community. We're built to be there for one another and try our best to understand and have a sense of belonging for people." - Jennifer Koster "My disability can't be thought away... I can't just wish my disability away. I can't take a vacation from it... it's always there as an undercurrent and all of us have undercurrents." - Jennifer Koster рҹҢҹ Featured Insights: The transformative power of reframing limiting beliefsHow the EDS community demonstrates exceptional resilienceThe importance of showing up for others even when you don't know what to sayFinding purpose through connecting leadership principles with personal challengesBalancing practical and emotional support for those facing difficultiesрҹ“ҡ Wisdom to Remember: Focus your vision beyond the day-to-day struggles, love yourself and others, and build your community. If we leave our family, workplace, faith community, and friends better because of our presence, we create a lasting legacy. A rising tide raises all ships, and I invite you along on this journey to Evoke Greatness! title: Dave Asprey Reveals the New Frontier of Biohacking
summary: What if living to 180 isn't about Hacks, but about Healing? In this conversation, I sit down with my friend Dave Asprey, the godfather of biohacking, to uncover what might be the most important conversation we've ever had on this show. And I donвҖҷt say that lightly. You know Dave for pushing the envelope on longevity, nutrition, and performanceвҖ”but what we got into here, this is the stuff thatВ actually sets you free. We talk about how every single one of us is born with the capacity forВ intuitionвҖ”a gut knowing that transcends logicвҖ”and how most of us have let our ego, emotional baggage, and mental chatter completely drown it out. Dave breaks down the five FвҖҷs that drive human behavior: Fear, Food, Fertility, Friendship, and ForgivenessвҖ”and how forgiveness, more than anything else, gives you energy, peace, and power. вҖңForgiveness is simply becoming non-reactive to something that was still causing you pain,вҖқ Dave says. That line hit me hard. We also dig into the practical sideвҖ”because if thereвҖҷs no strategy, IвҖҷm not interested. Dave shares how to train your intuition like a muscle, what happens to your body when you forgive, and why practices like meditation, breathwork, and evenВ brief, intentional exposure to painВ (what he calls BICEP) can radically change how you show up in your life. This isn't woo-woo stuffвҖ”itвҖҷs actionable and measurable, and heвҖҷs got the brainwave data to back it up. DaveвҖҷs new book,В Heavily Meditated, might be his most important one yet. Not because itвҖҷs flashy, but because it gives you the tools to clear the blocksвҖ”emotional, mental, even generationalвҖ”that are stealing your energy and keeping you from your purpose. If youвҖҷre biohacking your body but ignoring your spirit, youвҖҷre leaving the most important gains on the table. Key Takeaways:    Why forgiveness isn't a feelingвҖ”it's a performance enhancer    How to recognize and build your intuitive muscle    The biology behind вҖңfemale intuitionвҖқ (youвҖҷll want to hear this)    How meditation actually rewires your nervous system    Why exposing yourself to pain builds motivation and emotional resilience    What to do when your heart rate variability just wonвҖҷt budge    A surprising connection between altered states and intimacy Let me tell you something: it's not just about living longerвҖ”it's about livingВ better. With more joy, more clarity, and more freedom. ThatвҖҷs what weвҖҷre after.
