title: Angela Maxwell: Walking 20,000 Miles Around the World вҖ” Choosing Courage Every Step of the Way
summary: Angela Maxwell spent nearly seven years walking around the world вҖ” solo. Covering over 20,000 miles across four continents and 16 countries, she braved the Australian outback, battled dengue fever in Vietnam, and faced the daily challenge of finding safe places to sleep, often in a tent.В   A self-described introvert and novice adventurer when she began, AngelaвҖҷs journey was as much an inner transformation as a physical one вҖ” fuelled by fear, curiosity, and an unwavering commitment to explore what it means to live with courage.  Angela walked with purpose, raising funds to support girls rescued from human trafficking, and continues that mission through mentoring and womenвҖҷs empowerment workshops. SheвҖҷs been featured by Lonely Planet, BBC Travel, and Outside Magazine, and most recently became one of the first women to swim across the Caspian Sea вҖ” a feat that took 28 days and redefined her relationship with fear and endurance.  Now based in Wales (for the moment), Angela is writing a book about her world walk and sharing her philosophy thatВ вҖңcourage is a verb.вҖқ Whether sheвҖҷs walking, swimming, or simply choosing the unknown, Angela lives by example вҖ” proving that adventure doesnвҖҷt require expertise, just the decision to begin.  ***  В New episodes of the Tough Girl Podcast drop every Tuesday at 7 AM (UK time)!В Make sure to subscribe so you never miss the inspiring journeys and incredible stories of tough women pushing boundaries.  В Do you want to support the Tough Girl Mission to increase the amount of female role models in the media in the world of adventure and physical challenges?В Support via Patreon! Join me in making a difference by signing up here:В www.patreon.com/toughgirlpodcast.  В Your support makes a difference.В  Thank you x  ***  Show notes   Who is AngelaВ  Spending 6.5 years walking west around the worldВ  Setting a world record by swimming across the Caspian SeaВ  Being settled in Wales for the Winter Having a mutual friend - Miriam LancewoodВ  Her childhood and growing up Getting chicken pox at 30 Not having any friends and being an introvert as a young kid Her love for nature coming later on in life Liking her own companyВ  Daydreaming about travel Spending time alone Being inspired from a young age WHY?! Starting walking because she was terrified Where does an idea come from? The route and what the journey looked like Starting in Australia and walking North to South on the West Coast Heading over to VietnamВ  Heading to Europe and the UK Flying back and forth due to visa issues Writing a book about the walk The challenge of writing a book Not wanting to share the journey on social media Wanting to be totally present and have the journey for herself Raising funds for women and girlsВ  Self funding the walk Being inspired by Rosie Swale Pope who ran around the worldВ В  Book: Just a Little Run Around the World: 5 Years, 3 Packs of Wolves and 53 Pairs of ShoesВ  Living on a budget of $5 per day Women for Women InternationalВ  Adventuring with a purpose Bad days while walkingВ  Thriving on the little daily adventuresВ  The joy of not knowing and what might be around the corner Still enjoying walking now The classic thru-hikes Walking the Pennine Way in the UK Walking part of Te Araroa in New Zealand Her thoughts on courage Dealing with self doubt, before and during the walk Ted Talk - вҖңCourage is a verbвҖқВ  Making the decision to continueВ  The most dangerous place for a woman is in the homeВ  The fear at the end of the walk, what was going to be next Little feeling of depression at the end Swimming across the Caspian SeaВ  Swimming V walkingВ  Having a fear of open water and not being a good swimmer Funding the challenge What it was like day to day on the water Taking 28 days to do the crossing, swimming 6hrs per day Other around the world challenges?! Not having a compulsion to share her storiesВ  Living on very little and funding her current lifestyle Using Trusted Housesitters Running workshops for women Guilty pleasuresвҖҰ. Injuries? title: How To Stay Grounded When Feedback Hits A Nerve
summary: In this episode, IвҖҷm talking about something most of us struggle with - feedback. Even when itвҖҷs well-intentioned, it can feel incredibly personal, especially if weвҖҷve tied our sense of worth to how we show up, perform, or succeed. I share a challenging feedback story from early in my career that really stayed with me, and how it helped me understand why feedback can land so hard. I also offer two simple but powerful questions I now use to process feedback with more clarity and self-trust - without spiraling or shutting down. This isnвҖҷt about pretending feedback doesnвҖҷt hurt. ItвҖҷs about creating enough space between who you are and what you do, so you can stay open, grounded even when the feedback stings. If youвҖҷve ever replayed a comment over and over in your mind, or felt a pang of self-doubt after someone вҖңhelpfullyвҖқ pointed something out, I hope this offers a shift in perspective.  Connect with me: https://linktr.ee/amykiernan  You can also find more reflections over on my Substack, where I share a weekly companion post to this podcast for discussion: https://the10minuteshift.substack.com

title: Kelsey Grammer Reveals All (Pt 2): Lessons on Love, Loss, Healing & Remembering
summary: Full Video Available on YouTube вҒ вҒ @JamieKernLimaOfficialвҒ вҒ . Are You Ready to believe in YOU?рҹҷҢвҒ вҒ  jamiekernlima.comвҒ вҒ  рҹ‘Ҳ Sign up for my FREE Inspirational Newsletter here and youвҖҷll ALSO get special prompt questions to help you grow in your self-worth-building that pair with each episode!рҹ©·В  Make sure to click the вҖңFollowвҖқ button for the show on your favorite podcast app, so youвҖҷll be the first to get each episode!В  In this incredible Part 2 with Kelsey Grammer, we GO THERE on ALL the things! Will he run for office? His big new role on the big screen! His candid insights on all the hot topics and more! Plus get your tissues out (again), and get ready to remember the experiences, feelings and people who matter most to you in your life! This is a side of Kelsey Grammer youвҖҷve never seen before, heвҖҷs truly heart-wide-open in this soulful, vulnerable, emotional interview!  В  How do you remember the people in your life who you loved, and have lost? And have you considered how that impacts your life, your healing, your spirit and your joy today? Today weвҖҷre talking about love, loss, healing,В rememberingвҖҰ and celebrating a joy-filled lifeвҖҰwith my guest today, Kelsey Grammer. В  Kelsey is a Golden Globe Award winning, Emmy Award winning, Tony Award winning, Screen Actors Guild Award winning, PeopleвҖҷs Choice Award winning actor, comedian and producer. He first gained fame for his role as Dr. Frasier Crane on this hit TV show Cheers, and later itвҖҷs spin-off Frasier, making primetime television history playing one of the longest-running roles, for more than 20 years, by a single actor. He is the founder of the Faith American Brewing Company, a husband to his wife Kayte, and the father of 7 children! It was recently announced heвҖҷll be playing BEAST in the much anticipated AvengerвҖҷs Doomsday, and today, weвҖҷre actually seeing a deeply personal and intimate part of Kelsey, in perhaps one of the most important and meaningful works of his life, KelseyвҖҷs brand new book: where he explores love, loss, healing and celebrating the memory of a life filled with joy. In Karen: A Brother Remembers, Kelsey shares the tragic story of the death of his sister, Karen, who was brutally murdered at the age of eighteen, and the journey of his own path to healing in his life. In Karen, Kelsey aims to help others who have experienced similar loss, offering solace and encouragement to cherish the love they knew, however brief, on their own path toward healing.  And whether you're joining me today for yourself or because someone that you love shared this episode with you, I want to welcome you to the Jamie Kern Lima Show podcast family. And remember this episode is not just for you and me. Please share it with every single person that you know because it can change their life too. ItвҖҷs such an honor to share this podcast together with you. And please note: I am not a licensed therapist, and this podcast is NOT intended as a substitute for the advice of a physician, professional coach, psychotherapist, or other qualified professional. title: Episode 179: Ш№ШҙЩӮ ЫҢШ§ Ш№Ш§ШҜШӘШҹ
summary: Ш§ЩҫЫҢШІЩҲШҜ ЫұЫ·Ы№ ЩҫШ§ШҜЪ©ШіШӘ "Щ„Ш§Щ… ШӘШ§ Ъ©Щ„Ш§Щ…" : Ш№ШҙЩӮ ЫҢШ§ Ш№Ш§ШҜШӘШҹ ЫҢЩҮ ЩҲЩӮШӘШ§ЫҢЫҢ ЩҶЩҮ ШҜЫҢЪҜЩҮ ШҜЩ„ШӘ ШӘЩҶЪҜ Щ…ЫҢШҙЩҮ ЩҶЩҮ Ш§ШІ ШҜЫҢШҜЩҶШҙ Ш°ЩҲЩӮ Щ…ЫҢЪ©ЩҶЫҢШҹ ЩҒЩӮШ· ЩҮШіШӘ! ЩҒЩӮШ· ШЁШ§ЩҮШ§ШҙЫҢ... ШЁЩҮ ЩҶШёШұШӘ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ШіЩ…Шҙ Ш№ШҙЩӮЩҮШҹ ЫҢШ§ ЩҒЩӮШ· Ш№Ш§ШҜШӘЫҢЩҮ Ъ©ЩҮ ШӘШұШі Ш§ШІ ШӘЩҶЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ ЩҶЩ…ЫҢШ°Ш§ШұЩҮ ЩҲЩ„Шҙ Ъ©ЩҶЫҢШҹ ШӘЩҲЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩҫЫҢШІЩҲШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШӘЩҒШ§ЩҲШӘ Ш№ШҙЩӮ ЩҲ Ш№Ш§ШҜШӘ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІЫҢЩ…. ШӘШ№Ш§ШұЫҢЩҒШҙЩҲЩҶ ШұЩҲ ШЁЩҮШӘЩҲЩҶ Щ…ЫҢЪҜЩ…ШҢ ЩҶШҙЩҲЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮШұ Ъ©ШҜЩҲЩ… ШұЩҲ Щ…ЫҢЪҜЩ… ЩҲ ЫҢЪ© ШіШұЫҢ ШұШ§ЩҮЪ©Ш§Шұ Ъ©ЩҮ Ш§ШІ Ш№Ш§ШҜШӘ ШЁЩҮ Ш№ШҙЩӮ ШЁШұШіЫҢ ЫҢШ§ ШӘЪ©Щ„ЫҢЩҒ Ш№Ш§ШҜШӘ ШұЩҲ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШҙЩҮ ШұЩҲШҙЩҶ Ъ©ЩҶЫҢ ШЁЩҮШӘ Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶЩ…. ЩҶШ§ЪҜЩҒШӘЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЪҶШ§Щ„Шҙ ЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұЩҲШІШ§ЩҶЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШҜШұ ШұЩҲШ§ШЁШ·В  ЩҲ Ш§ШӘЩҒШ§ЩӮШ§ШӘ ШІЩҶШҜЪҜЫҢВ  ШЁШ§В  ШІШЁШ§ЩҶ ШіШ§ШҜЩҮ ЩҲ Ш№Ш§Щ…ЫҢШ§ЩҶЩҮ ШӘЩҲШіШ· ШіЩҲЩ„Щ…Ш§ШІ ШЁШұЩӮЪҜЫҢШұШҢ Щ„Ш§ЫҢЩҒ Ъ©ЩҲЪҶ ЩҲ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢШіШӘ ШұЩҲШ§ШЁШ· Щ…ЩҲШ«ШұШҢ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҶ ШҜШұЩ…Ш§ЩҶЪҜШұ ШҜШұ ШӘЩҲШұЩҶШӘЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ЩҶШ§ШҜШ§ ЩҲШұЪ©ШҙШ§Щҫ ШӘЪ©ЩҶЫҢЪ© ЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұЩҲШ§ШЁШ· ШЁШ№ШҜ Ш§ШІ Щ…ЩҮШ§Ш¬ШұШӘШіЩҒШ§ШұШҙ Ъ©ШӘШ§ШЁ "ЩҶЩӮШҙ ШҜШұЩҲЩҶ" Ш§ЩҫЫҢШІЩҲШҜ ШІШЁШ§ЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш№ШҙЩӮ ЩҲШұЪ©ШҙШ§Щҫ вҖңШӘЪ©ЩҶЫҢЪ© ЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШұШІЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢвҖқВ  ШҜЩҲШұЩҮвҖҢ В«Щ…Ш№Ш¬ШІЩҮвҖҢ ЩӮЩ„Щ… ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШәШ° ШЁШ§ Щ…ШҜЫҢШӘЫҢШҙЩҶ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш§ШіШ§ШұШӘ ШӘШұШіВ» ШҜШұЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘ ШҜЩҲШұЩҮ "ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ш§ШІ ЩҮЫҢЩҲЩ„Ш§ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘШұШі ЩҲ ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ" ШҜШұЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘ ШҜЩҲШұЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Щ…ШӯЪ©Щ… ШҜШұ ЩҮЩҒШӘ ЩӮШҜЩ… ШҜШұЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘ ШҜЩҲШұЩҮ ЩҮЩҒШӘ ШіЩҶЪҜ ШЁЩҶШ§ЫҢ ШұШ§ШЁШ·ЩҮ Щ…ЩҲШ«Шұ Lam Ta Kalam is a Well-received Farsi Podcast Episode 179: Love Vs. title: The Science of Love, Desire and Attachment - Psychology vs your Biology
podcast_name: Growth Mindset Psychology: The Science of Self-Improvement
summary: Love isnвҖҷt just a feeling, itвҖҷs a pattern. And patterns, when understood, can be changed. We are the descendants of beings who bonded, lusted, and survivedвҖ”not by chance, but by biology. In this episode, we explore the elegant architecture of love, divided into three distinct experiences: lust, attraction, and attachment. Each has its own hormonal symphony: testosteroneвҖҷs drive, dopamineвҖҷs thrill, oxytocinвҖҷs warmth. But these chemicals, while essential, are not our destiny. We must choose how we respond to them. The opportunity lies in seeing love not just as passion, but as a practiceвҖ”a practice of self-awareness, empathy, and intentionality.    Reframe love from chaos to conscious experience.    Observe your emotions without becoming them.    Build connection that outlasts dopamine spikes. Your biology is the system, but your awareness can evolve it. title: Surviving Traumatic Childhood Sexual Abuse | Seth Gehle
summary: Welcome back to The Journey Podcast in today's episode Seth Gehle shares his deeply personal journey as a male survivor of childhood sexual abuse and domestic trauma. From enduring an abusive home environment with his mother to being manipulated and sexually abused by a predator from ages 10 to 16, Seth's story is one of pain, survival, and ultimately hope. Seth opens up about what it was like to live in fear, the emotional toll of grooming, and how escaping one form of abuse led him into another. Now, years later, heвҖҷs using his voice to raise awareness, challenge stigma around sexual abuse victims, and offer hope to others walking a similar path. title: What is a Good Life? #122 - When Our Messiness & Souls Meet with Felicity Reed
summary: On the 122nd episode of the What is a Good Life? podcast, IвҖҷm delighted to welcome our guest, Felicity Reed. Felicity is a Psychotherapist and expert in leadership, who, alongside a private practice and consultancy, has worked as a clinical lead across the NHS, local authorities and the voluntary sector, working at the sharpest edges of trauma and health inequalities, supporting people facing lifeвҖҷs toughest circumstances to turn their pain into power. She first came into therapy because of her own difficulties and ADHD, and uses this lived experience of messiness to bring humour, joy and humanity to the trickiest places of the human soul. She is the founder of Mic Drops: tiny pops of tools, techniques and training that highlight health inequalities, and demonstrate tiny but powerful actions each of us can take to improve social justice. She also shares tiny and powerful daily tips on Instagram to de-mystify therapy and make mental wellbeing easier. In this wonderful conversation, we explore the themes of connection, soul, vulnerability, and embracing and sharing our messiness. We explore what type of relating is possible on the other side of this openness. Ultimately, this episode points to a richness of life that can be experienced when we allow ourselves to be seen. For more of Felicity's work, check out the following:Felicity's website: https://www.felicityreed.co.uk/ Felicity's Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/felicitypsychotherapy/ Contact me at mark@whatisagood.life if you'd like to explore your own lines of self-inquiry through 1-on-1 coaching, take part in my 5-week What is a Good Life? group courses, discuss experiences I create to stimulate greater trust, communication, and connection, amongst your leadership teams, or you simply want to get in touch. - For the What is a Good Life? podcast's YouTube page: https://www.youtube.com/@whatisagoodlife/videos- My newsletter: https://www.whatisagood.life/- My LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mark-mccartney-14b0161b4/- Exploring one-on-one coaching: https://www.whatisagood.life/p/one-on-one-coaching- What is a Good Life? title: Daring to LIVE Fully: Fear-less and be enlivened with Cassie Miller
podcast_name: Designed As You Human Design Podcast
summary: In todayвҖҷs incredibly inspiring conversation, I have the honour of sitting down with Cassie Miller - the founder of the SheBelieve Movement - about living from the heart, and daring to live life fully and fear-less. Two years ago, Cassie was diagnosed with metastatic neuroendocrine cancer. Since then, her path has become one of courage and fierce devotion to life, to healing and to something much deeper. Her story isnвҖҷt about illness; itвҖҷs about awakening. About choosing to meet life not in spite of its fragility, but because of it. Cassie speaks with breathtaking honesty about what it means to live fully, even when the future is unknown. She shares how she is walking hand-in-hand with western medicine and holistic healing, how sheвҖҷs come to trust her body again, and how sheвҖҷs redefining what it means to be вҖңwell.вҖқ In this deeply moving conversation, we explore: Living in alignment with the soul even in the face of uncertainty.How fear can soften when met with compassion and curiosity.Redefining "being well" as presence, love, and inner peace.The power of community, connection, and being witnessed.Spiritual awakenings, subtle miracles, and listening to the whispers of the body.Loving ourselves through the mess, the unknown, the miracle of being here.CassieвҖҷs diagnosis and her unfolding journey of healing.Holding space for both grief and hope.The dance between western medicine and holistic healing.More than anything, this episode is a reminder of the human spirit: to resilience, to softness, and to the quiet bravery of showing up for your life one breath, one heartbeat, one sacred moment at a time. Throughout our conversation, Cassie reminds us that while medicine may mend us, it is magic, the kind rooted in human spirit, love, and life force that truly morphs us. Let this episode be a gentle companion for wherever you are on your journey. title: Bird Brain | S8E14 (313): Mothering Nature or Masked Neglect?
summary: We often pride ourselves on being the nurturersвҖ”the ones who show up, fix, soothe, and hold space. But what if that instinct to mother others is actually masking our own unmet needs? In this episode, we explore the thin line between compassion and codependency, care and control, nurture and neglect. When youвҖҷre always the one pouring into others, whoвҖҷs replenishing you? LetвҖҷs unpack how well-meaning over-giving can disconnect us from our own healing, how people-pleasing becomes mistaken for purpose, and what it really means to mother yourself. title: Why as a man your feelings shouldn't dictate what you do. Andrew Tate.
summary: Life is a competition, and if you're a man, you're in the game whether you like it or not. Every dollar, every girl, every bit of statusвҖ”youвҖҷre fighting other men for it. And if youвҖҷre out here worrying about how you feel instead of handling business, youвҖҷve already lost. The most powerful men donвҖҷt wake up and ask themselves if theyвҖҷre happy; they get up and do what needs to be done. The ones who win at life donвҖҷt let emotions dictate their actions. When things go wrongвҖ”heartbreak, failures, setbacksвҖ”thatвҖҷs the fuel. ThatвҖҷs when you double down, hit the gym harder, and get creative. Weak men fold; strong men take that pain and turn it into success.В  Every single negative emotionвҖ”anger, sadness, frustrationвҖ”itвҖҷs all energy. ItвҖҷs unlimited fuel if you use it right. But most guys just sit there, crying about life instead of fixing it. You know exactly what you need to do. Get in shape. Learn how to fight. Make money. Build yourself up. And if you choose to do nothing, thatвҖҷs on you. NobodyвҖҷs coming to save you. I donвҖҷt feel sorry for people who refuse to step up because being weak is a choice. If youвҖҷre not pushing yourself to your full potential, then you deserve to be mocked. The reality is simple: strong men win, weak men lose.В  Depression? Feeling down? Forget all that. If youвҖҷre struggling, go to the gym, get a six-pack, start winning. Stop telling yourself itвҖҷs out of your controlвҖ”itвҖҷs not. The only thing you truly control in this world is your mind. You decide whether to be strong or weak, successful or a failure. This is life, this is the game. You either level up or you stay a loser. Me? IвҖҷm playing this game for real. My life is GTA in real lifeвҖ”I see what I want, I take it. No excuses. No complaining. You either wake up, look in the mirror, and say, "IвҖҷm a beast, IвҖҷm going to dominate today," or you sit around feeling sorry for yourself. But if thatвҖҷs your choice, donвҖҷt expect respect. title: Inside Pure Green: The Franchise Transforming Health in North Carolina

title: Menopause Expert Dr. Jennifer Zelovitzky
summary: Host Alanna McGinn welcomes Dr. Jennifer Zelovitzky to delve into the complexities of menopause and how it impacts sleep for women during this stage of life. They discuss the various stages of menopause, common symptoms, and the emotional changes women experience during this transition.  They also discuss hormone replacement therapy (HRT), the importance of communication in relationships, cognitive changes such as brain fog, shifts in life priorities, changes in libido, and the significance of creating a comfortable sleep environment. They also highlight practical strategies for navigating these changes together. More About Dr. Jennifer Zelovitzky: Dr. Jennifer Zelovitzky is a certified Menopause Specialist and the Clinical Director of WomenвҖҷs Health at Medcan Toronto.В  She is the host and co-producer of Medsplaining, a podcast and social media platform designed to help women better understand their bodies and advocate for their needs in the Canadian Healthcare system.В  She is also the mom of 2 amazing daughters and 3 fur babies. title: Dennis Han, Co-Founder of calmi.so : 100,000 Users from 6 Instagram Videos | The App ThatвҖҷs Redefining Mental Health Support
summary: Dennis Han didnвҖҷt set out to build a mental health app. He was just trying to get through a breakup. Like so many of us, he started looking for support вҖ” only to hit wall after wall. Long waitlists. High costs. Confusing systems. All at a time when he just needed someone to talk to. So, he built what he couldnвҖҷt find. That idea becameВ CalmiВ вҖ” a voice-based AI that offers real-time emotional support in 10вҖ“30 minute sessions. No waitlists. No pressure. Just a calm, empathetic voice when you need it most. WhatвҖҷs wild is how fast it resonated. Calmi reachedВ 60,000 users off just six Instagram videos. No funding. No launch strategy. Just something real, built with care. By the time youвҖҷre hearing this, theyвҖҷve crossed overВ 100,000 users. In this episode, Dennis and I talk about what it looks like toВ actuallyВ back yourself, the quiet confidence that comes from building with intention, and how to stop talking yourself out of the thing you know youвҖҷre meant to do. If youвҖҷve ever sat on an idea, second-guessed your gut, or waited for the perfect time to start вҖ” this oneвҖҷs for you. Check out Calmi:В https://calmi.soFollow Dennis on Instagram:В Dennis Han

title: EP. 7: Smartphones & ADHD: A Complicated Relationship | How Your Device Is Changing Your Brain | ADHD with Jenna Free
summary: You can get your free ADHD Regulation Guide here - adhdwithjennafree.com/adhdguide  Chapters  00:00 Introduction to Smartphone Addiction and ADHD  04:29 Understanding the "Alternative Effect"  09:43 Radical Approaches to Technology Separation  15:00 Exploring Practical Solutions for Phone Use  20:35 Flexible Strategies for Managing Phone Addiction  30:59 Mindset of Curiosity and Reflection  Summary  In this episode of the ADHD with Jenna Free podcast, I discuss the relationship between smartphones and ADHD, exploring the addictive nature of technology and its impact on attention and regulation. I introduce the concept of the 'alternative effect' and share both radical and practical strategies for managing phone use, emphasizing the importance of separating technology and fostering a mindset of curiosity towards our habits. All I'm suggesting is to reflect on your phone usage and consider ways to create healthier boundaries with technology.  Takeaways   Smartphones are designed to be addictive, especially for ADHD brains. The 'alternative effect' shows how options can lower our motivation. Separating technology can help reduce phone addiction. Using a dumb phone or MP3 player can create distance from distractions. Apps like ClearSpace can help limit social media use effectively. Flexible strategies can help manage phone addiction without drastic changes. Mindfulness and curiosity are key in addressing phone habits. Creating a personal entertainment ladder can help retrain attention spans. Reflection on phone use can lead to healthier habits over time.  Connect with Jenna   Instagram TikTok

title: Is It ADHD or Am I Just Bad at Adulting? Expert Dr. Ari Tuckman Explains the Difference | Clutterbug Podcast # 273
podcast_name: ClutterBug - Organize, Clean and Transform your Home & Life
summary: If youвҖҷve ever felt like youвҖҷre constantly messing up вҖ” forgetting things, zoning out, missing steps, or just trying to keep your head above water вҖ” youвҖҷre not alone. Maybe youвҖҷve wondered if you have ADHDвҖҰ or maybe youвҖҷre just chronically overwhelmed and tired of feeling like youвҖҷre failing at вҖңbeing an adult.вҖқ In this episode, I sit down with Dr. Ari Tuckman вҖ” psychologist, speaker, and leading expert on adult ADHD вҖ” to talk about what it really means to be productive when your brain works differently. We dive deep into how ADHD affects not just your focus, but your self-esteem, your past experiences, and your ability to trust yourself. Ari shares science-backed, shame-free strategies from his new book The ADHD Productivity Manual вҖ” and gives us permission to stop striving for perfection and start creating a life that works for us. Whether youвҖҷve been diagnosed, suspect you might have ADHD, or just want to finally feel a little less behind all the timeвҖҰ this episode is packed with validation, laughter, and practical advice that will leave you feeling seen and supported. вңЁ We chat about: How ADHD shows up in adults (and why so many go undiagnosed) Why your messy past doesnвҖҷt mean youвҖҷre broken вҖ” or doomed The powerful link between clutter, distraction, and overwhelm Medication myths (and why itвҖҷs just one helpful tool, not a cure-all) Small shifts that make a big difference in productivity and self-worth The importance of compassion вҖ” for yourself and others рҹ§  Learn more from Dr. Tuckman: вҶ’ Website: https://adultadhdbook.com вҶ’ New book: The ADHD Productivity Manual вҖ” available everywhere books are sold рҹ‘Ү WhatвҖҷs one ADHD-friendly strategy that works for you? Let us know in the comments! title: Together Miniseries #2: The Death of External Power
summary: In this episode of The Gary Zukav Podcast, Gary continues the Together Miniseries by exploring one of the most defining shifts of our time: the death of external power. He explains how the structures that once defined successвҖ”control, dominance, manipulationвҖ”are no longer sustainable in the new consciousness that is emerging. Humanity is in a rapid transformation, moving away from a five-sensory perception rooted in survival and toward a multi-sensory experience centered on spiritual evolution. At the heart of this conversation is a powerful realization: the pursuit of external power, once necessary for our survival, now leads only to destructionвҖ”internally and collectively. Gary examines how this outdated mode of power is still embedded in our institutions, relationships, and fears, and why it is now incapable of guiding us forward. Through compelling metaphors and deeply reflective insights, he challenges listeners to notice how fear masquerades as control and how our collective suffering stems from this dying model. The path forward, Gary says, lies in creating authentic power: aligning your personality with your soul. The new consciousness invites each of us to embody harmony, cooperation, sharing, and reverence for life. Transformation begins within. Please do not take anything that Gary or Linda share as true just because they say it. If it resonates with you, experiment with it in your life. If it doesn't, let it go. What YouвҖҷll Discover in This Episode: (01:12) вҖ” Why the old definition of powerвҖ”manipulation and controlвҖ”is now obsolete and dangerous (05:20) вҖ” The metaphor of the steam engine and what it reveals about the end of external power (08:58) вҖ” How the fuel of our old societyвҖ”external powerвҖ”has run out, and whatвҖҷs replacing it (13:10) вҖ” What authentic power looks like and how it differs from external power (15:48) вҖ” How fear-based leadership fuels destruction and the role of the law of attraction in global events (18:40) вҖ” Why change must come from within, not from trying to fix the world externally Embrace the Soul Step Challenge: Between now and the next episode, take time to journal about the external sources of power you may still be seekingвҖ”whether through achievements, influence, appearance, or control. Ask yourself: What fear is driving this desire? How might I respond differently if I were choosing from love instead of fear? This week, challenge yourself to take one small action that reflects the intentions of your soulвҖ”harmony, cooperation, sharing, and reverence for life. Discover a new way of seeing and experiencing the world. Start your journey to multisensory perception HERE. summary: How should we think about aging when the impacts of climate change can make the future feel so uncertain? How to Age Up co-hosts Yasmin Tayag and Natalie Brennan talk about how current climate concerns compare to the existential crises of previous generations, and how to practice hope during uncertain times. title: 1076. Is Your Health Getting Hijacked at HOME?! EMFs, Microwaves & Health Anxiety DEBUNKED w/ The Heal Squad Roundtable
summary: Hey, Heal Squad! The Roundtable is back, and this week weвҖҷre diving into two household staples that might be doing more harm than good: electric toothbrushes and microwaves. рҹҳі Maria brings in a viral headline that claims electric toothbrushes are вҖңmicrowaving your brain.вҖқ Is it clickbaitвҖ”or is there truth to the EMF concerns? Dr. Alison breaks down the science, and we explore how seemingly harmless daily habits could be adding to your health load. We also get into microwave use and how it can degrade food quality and alter molecular structure. title: #229 | Why вҖңBeing NiceвҖқ Keeps HSPs OverwhelmedвҖ”And How Inner Work Can Break the Cycle
podcast_name: Stress Management for Highly Sensitive People (HSP): Inner Work and Strategies for Coping with Stress, Overwhelm, and Negativ
summary: Are you stuck in a cycle of being вҖңniceвҖқ that secretly drains your energy as a Highly Sensitive Person?  If you're constantly overwhelmed from trying to please others while suppressing your true needs, you're not alone. Many HSPs struggle with setting boundaries without feeling like theyвҖҷre being unkind. But what if your people-pleasing isnвҖҷt actually kindnessвҖ”and what if real compassion starts with inner clarity?  In this episode, youвҖҷll discover:    Why the thought вҖңI need to be niceвҖқ may be fueling your stress and holding you back from true connection.    How to gently work with the stuck part of you that fears saying no or taking up spaceвҖ”without shaming it.    A guided inner work practice using the Work of Byron Katie to transform emotional patterns from within.    Listen now to reconnect with your authentic kindnessвҖ”and set boundaries without losing your compassionate heart.  Todd  Dreaming of a stress-free, balanced life? VisitВ trueinnerfreedom.comВ and complete the HSP Stress Survey. Gain clarity on your stress triggers and enjoy a free 15-minute Inner Freedom Call designed to guide you toward lasting inner peace and fulfillment.  Are you a highly sensitive person (HSP) or someone who identifies as hypersensitive or neurodivergent? This podcast is dedicated to helping highly sensitive people (HSPs) navigate overwhelm and stress by using The Work of Byron KatieвҖ”a powerful method for questioning stressful thoughts and finding true inner freedom.  We dive deep into stress management strategies, coping with stress, and stress relief methods specifically tailored for HSPs. Learn how to manage emotions, especially negative ones, and explore effective stress reduction techniques that go beyond the surface to address the root causes of anxiety and pressure.  Whether youвҖҷre interested in learning how to lower stress, handle stress and pressure, or reduce stress through practical techniques, we provide insights and support based on The Work of Byron Katie. Discover how this transformative approach can help you decrease stress, find inner peace, and create balance in your life.  Join us to learn about various coping strategies for stress, all designed to support HSPs in their journey toward emotional well-being.

title: Psychedelics & Plant Medicine - Dr Prash and Michael Gueron
summary: For centuries possibly even millennia, cultures have used psychedelics and plant medicines such as psilocybin (mushrooms), ayahuasca, wachuma and more to heal deep trauma and to awaken themselves to altered states of consciousness, and expand their sense of spirituality. In July of 2023 The Therapeutic Goods Administration here in Australia, permitted the use of psilocybin for the treatment of certain mental health conditions. The beginning of what feels like a shift in the way both the health system and society view tryptamineвҖҷs for therapy. On this episode IвҖҷm joined by Dr Prash a psychiatrist, psychotherapist and psychedeliciatrist and Shaman Michael Gueron, to go behind the mask of plant medicine and psychedelics. On this episode we explore: What psychedelics are and the difference between them. The difference between the therapeutic experience and the ceremonial experience. How it can help people suffering with anxiety and depression and a host of other traumas. The necessity for cleansing before, intention during, and integration after any experience. Prash explains what happens to the mind during a psilocybin experience and how it helps us to see our own minds more clearly. Michael shares how Ayahuasca and Wachuma help to take people out of the head and into the heart. How to work through fears approaching an experience. Why purging is necessary during big plant medicine experiences. Josh shares his psychedelic experiences. We discuss the future of these medicines in mainstream and the concerns in the space. Keep up to date with Michael on instagram @mihcael_kintu_ and learn about Dr Prash by visiting drprash.com Be sure to subscribe to our YouTube channel to WATCH the full episode @gobehindthemask Follow us on Instagram and TikTok @gobehindthemask Visit www.gobehindthemask.com.au for more information and sign up to our вҖҳWeekly WisdomsвҖҷ to begin YOUR journey behind the mask. title: Integration, Neuroplasticity, and the Art of Rewiring
summary: вҖңIntegrationвҖқ is a word that gets thrown around in the healing world, but what does it really mean?  In this powerful episode of Trauma Rewired, Elisabeth and Jennifer unravel the true meaning of integration, not as a final step, but as the heart of sustained transformation. Without integration, even the most profound somatic release, emotional breakthrough, or psychedelic insight can quickly fade back into old survival patterns. The nervous system, always seeking safety and familiarity, needs time, rhythm, and repetition to embody change.  We explore the neurobiological foundation of integration through the lens of Nneurosomatic Iintelligence. YouвҖҷll learn about neuroplasticity, minimum effective dose, how somatic preparation sets the stage for transformation, and the dangers of skipping integration in psychedelic or peak somatic experiences.  Jennifer shares her deeply personal journey through years of NSI, medicine work, and vision quest, offering lived insight into how patterns like unworthiness get rewired over time. Together, we invite you to rethink healing as an ongoing relationship with your nervous system, your intuition, and your body.  Whether you've had a mystical experience, a big emotional release, or a subtle insight in therapy, integration is how you make that change last. If you're navigating your own healing journey, or supporting others in theirs, this episode is essential listening.  Topics discussed in this episode:   Why integration is the process, not the end      The brainвҖҷs need for pattern repetition and safety      What neuroplasticity is and how NSI uses it for lasting change      Somatic preparation before peak experiences like psychedelics      The three measurable types of psychedelic breakthroughs      Emotional, psychological, mystical, and somatic insights      Signs your nervous system isnвҖҷt ready to receive a breakthrough      The dangers of bypassing integration      Practical examples of integrating new beliefs like worthiness      How NSI supports embodied transformation      Get started training your nervous system with our FREE 2-week offer on the Brain Based Membership site: https://www.rewiretrial.com  В   Connect with Jennifer https://calendly.com/traumarewired/nsi-1-1-rewire-consultation  В   Learn more about the Neuro-Somatic Intelligence Coaching program and sign up for the next cohort now!В https://www.neurosomaticintelligence.com  В   Connect with us on social media: @trauma.rewired  В   Join our Trauma Rewired Facebook Group!В https://www.facebook.com/groups/761101225132846  В   FREE 1 Year Supply of Vitamin D + 5 Travel Packs from Athletic Greens when you use my exclusive offer:В https://www.drinkag1.com/rewired  В   Trauma Rewired podcastВ  is intended to educate and inform but does not constitute medical, psychological or other professional advice or services. title: Attacks on the Separation of Church and State, The Lawless Supreme Court, and the Update on Public Schools
podcast_name: Getting Better with Jonathan Van Ness
summary: This week on The Monday Edit - weвҖҷre making public push back fun with Strict ScrutinyвҖҷs co-host Leah Litman talking all about her new book Lawless: How the Supreme Court Came to Run on Conservative Grievance, Fringe Theories, and Bad Vibes. WeвҖҷre also talking about why public schools are becoming the battle ground for the separation of church and state with Alessandro Terenzoni, VP of Public Policy from Americans United for Separation of Church and State - weвҖҷre going IN on the supreme court this week, if you canвҖҷt already tell. Plus! WeвҖҷre serving you a healthy dose of queer joy (like we always do over here on the Monday Edit, duh!). Leah Litman is a professor of law at the University of Michigan and a former Supreme Court clerk. In addition to cohosting Strict Scrutiny, she writes frequently about the Court for media outlets including The Washington Post, Slate, and The Atlantic, among others, and has appeared as a commentator on NPR and MSNBC, in addition to other venues. She has received the Ruth Bader Ginsburg award for her вҖңscholarly excellenceвҖқ from the American Constitution Society and published in top law reviews. Follow her on X @LeahLitman and Instagram @ProfLeahLitman. Alessandro Terenzoni is Vice President of Public Policy at Americans United. He leads Americans UnitedвҖҷs policy team in its critical work to promote positive policy that defends and supports church-state separation at the federal and state levels. Before joining Americans United, he was Deputy Director of the Office for Civil Rights within the U.S. Department of JusticeвҖҷs Office of Justice Programs. He led the officeвҖҷs civil-rights enforcement and technical-assistance work, ensuring that DOJ grant recipients did not unlawfully discriminate based on race, sex, religion, disability, and other protected bases. Follow us on Instagram @gettingbetterwithjvn Jonathan on Instagram @jvn and senior producer Chris @amomentlikechris New video episodes Getting Better on YouTube every Wednesday. Senior Producer, Chris McClure Producer, Editor & Engineer is Nathanael McClure Production support from Julie Carrillo, Anne Currie, and Chad Hall Our theme music is also composed by Nathanael McClure. title: If You Feel Overwhelmed & Uncertain About Money, Listen to This
summary: If youвҖҷre feeling overwhelmed about money, this episode is for you.В  Whether you want to pay off debt, increase your income, or have more money in the bank, this episode is a must-listen.В  For the first time on the podcast, Mel is sharing, in detail, the step-by-step approach she took to get out of $800,000 in debt, create financial freedom, and get good with money. Mel is also joined today by Lewis Howes, two-time New York Times bestselling author and host of the hit podcast The School of Greatness. Before he reached the success he has today, Lewis was broke, living on his sisterвҖҷs couch, with no college degree. He shares the specific steps he took to start over, learn new skills, and the mindset and success habits he used to make and save money. Together, Mel and Lewis share the exact formulas they used to turn their lives around. Whether youвҖҷre trying to pay down debt, create an emergency fund, start a side hustle, or just have more money in the bank, youвҖҷre going to learn the things you should be doing right now to change your financial future. By the end of this episode, you will have the simple tools you need to start getting good with money. YouвҖҷll know that change is possible. And that you are worthy of financial peace and ease.В  You CAN get good with money. Starting today.В  For more resources, click here for the podcast episode page.В  If you liked this episode, and want to hear more about improving your financial life, listen to this episode next: 5 Rules of Money: How to Make It, Save It, & Be Smarter About It Connect with Mel:В  В  Get MelвҖҷs #1 bestselling book, The Let Them TheoryWatch the episodes on YouTubeFollow Mel on InstagramВ The Mel Robbins Podcast InstagramMel's TikTokВ Sign up for MelвҖҷs personal letterВ Subscribe to SiriusXM Podcasts+ to listen to new episodes ad-freeDisclaimer

title: Kate Moyle: Improving your Body Image and Your Love Life
podcast_name: The Meaningful Life with Andrew G. Marshall
summary: How can we learn to be at ease in our bodies so that we can be fully вҖңin the momentвҖқ during sex? Anxiety about body image is one of the biggest obstacles to a happy, fulfilled sex life. Many people seek therapy to help them relax and enjoy sex with their partner, others struggle on in low-sex or no-sex relationships.В  In this classic episode, Andrew talks to KATE MOYLE, a psychosexual and relationship therapist, about the knots we tie ourselves up in over how we look. Our cultureвҖҷs esteem for youth and beauty, (expressed in social media images of physical perfection) can make it very hard to inhabit our normal, imperfect bodies. Yet the self-confidence we need to enjoy sex is grounded in how we feel, not how we look.В  As well as her work as a therapist, Kate Moyle is host of The Sexual Wellness Sessions Podcast, and also appeared on BBC ThreeвҖҷs Sex on the Couch. Kate helps people to work through challenges in their sex lives and relationships; moving towards a place of sexual health, happiness and wellbeing. Her podcast hosts discussions that focus on normalising sex and relationships talk, and tackling the topics that are often left out of mainstream conversation; and she is a regular media contributor on the topic of sex and relationships. If YouвҖҷre Looking for MoreвҖҰ. You can subscribe to The Meaningful Life (via Apple Podcasts, Spotify or Google Podcasts) and hear a bonus mini-episode every week. Or you can join our Supporters Club on Patreon to also access exclusive behind-the-scenes content, fan requestsВ  and the chance to ask Andrew your own questions. Membership starts at just ВЈ4.50.В  This week supporters will hear: Three Things Kate Moyle knows to be true.В  AND subscribers also access all of our previous bonus content - a rich trove of insight on love, life and meaning created by Andrew and his interviewees. Follow Up Attend Andrew's new men's retreat, Reconnect With Yourself, this autumn in the Brandenberg countryside near Berlin. Get AndrewвҖҷs free guide to difficult conversations with your partner: How to Tell Your Partner Difficult Things  Listen to Kate MoyleвҖҷs The Sexual Wellness Sessions Podcast Take a look at KateвҖҷs website Follow Kate on Instagram @katemoyletherapy Read AndrewвҖҷs book Have the Sex You Want: a CoupleвҖҷs Guide to Getting the Spark Back Take a look at AndrewвҖҷs new online relationship course: My Best Relationship Tools https://courses.andrewgmarshall.com/relationship-toolsВ  Join our Supporters Club to access exclusive behind-the-scenes content, fan requests and the chance to ask Andrew your own questions. Membership starts at just ВЈ4.50.В  В  Andrew offers regular advice on love, marriage and finding meaning in your life via his social channels. Follow him on Twitter, Facebook and YouTube @andrewgmarshall

title: EP23: YouвҖҷre Feeling Stuck Because You Forgot How Multidimensional You Are
summary: Feeling stuck might have less to do with your circumstances and more to do with only seeing one version of who you are. В  As May rolled in, I realized I hadnвҖҷt made the progress I hoped for this year, and that left me feeling stuck. IвҖҷve heard the same thing from so many smart, accomplished women in business who are committed to personal development but still feel like somethingвҖҷs missing. В  I started asking myself: are we stuck, or are we just seeing ourselves through too narrow a lens? So often weвҖҷre reduced to one roleвҖҰ entrepreneur, parent, partnerвҖҰ and itвҖҷs hard to imagine what else we could be. But living in Paris has shown me how much power there is in being multi-dimensional. The culture here values boundaries, and those boundaries make space for different sides of a person to exist. В  In this episode, IвҖҷm sharing what IвҖҷve been learning, the practices that are helping me reconnect with other versions of myself, and a few questions I think are worth asking. What would happen if you stopped waiting for the next version of you to show up and started making space for her instead? В  I hope this episode encourages you to do just that. В  Episode Breakdown: 00:00 Reflections on Feeling Stuck 02:18 The Pressure of Being One-Dimensional 05:10 What Paris Taught Me About Complexity 07:56 Boundaries and Identity in French Culture 14:07 Tools for Personal Development 19:04 Do the Work В  Links Connect with Jane Bertch: Website: www.janebertch.comВ  Instagram @janebertch Podcast production and show notes provided by HiveCast.fm

title: Psychopathy versus altruism: the neuroscience of caring about others (w/ Abigail Marsh)
podcast_name: How to Be a Better Human
summary: Why is American culture so fascinated with psychopathic people and true crime stories? Why donвҖҷt billionaires give more? What makes some people so generous that theyвҖҷd undergo surgery to donate an organ to a complete stranger? These are the kinds of questions that Abigail Marsh, a professor of psychology and neuroscience at Georgetown University, studies. Chris and Abigail discuss the brain differences between extreme altruists and psychopathic individuals, why psychopathic traits do not necessarily correlate with aggression, how parents can support children with behavioral issues, and what we can all do to train ourselves to be more altruistic. title: 5 Game-Changing Alcohol-Free Insights From The 500-Mile Camino Way
podcast_name: Alcohol-Free Advantage Podcast with Andy Ramage
summary: I did not think this would happen so quickly. Not even a week into my 500-mile Camino walk across Spain. And already, the game-changing insights are beginning to surface. People walk the Camino for three reasons: Forgiveness Help And thanks I am here to give thanks for everything I have. And I owe so much to one change!В  I will say this for a fact.В  Had I not quit alcohol, this adventure would NEVER have begun. There was no time, energy or clarity. I was stuck in survival mode. This week, on the Camino, I was in thrivival mode (ThatвҖҷs not even a word, but you get what I mean) As I walked, I was overwhelmed with gratitude. I am grateful that my relationship with alcohol woke me up. Woke me up to my authentic self. And I am so grateful that it has done the same for so many others. So this message is not too long.В  I will cover two of the insights, and I save the BIG ones for my Alcohol-Free Advantage podcast.В  Click the links below to listen to this week's conversation.В  The hardest move in yoga is getting onto the mat. It is the same for the Camino It is the same for all our goals in life. Once started, the adventure springs to life.В  вҖңWhatever you can do, or dream you can; begin it. Boldness has genius power and magic in it.вҖқ GoetheВ  But too many dreams and goals are never started. They get pushed into the background, drowned by the busyness of life. How many unfinished books lie dormant on laptops? How many businesses never make it out of the pub? How many meaningful careers never happen? The secret, therefore, to the Camino and all our goals is the boldness to begin. Stop worrying about the future When you walk for hours without distractions, you hear your mind speaking.В  And mostly what it says is fear-mongering nonsense. A MAJOR insight for me already is that most of the noise in my head is about the future. As I continuously remind my coaches and clients. If it is in the future, it is made up. It canвҖҷt be a fact. All it is, at best, a best guess. But it's never a good guess. It is usually the worst possible imagined thing in the future.В  It is the crash and burn scenario.В  Well, thatвҖҷs what I found in my brain. I now understand whatВ  Mark Twain meant when he said, вҖңI am an old man who has known a great many troubles, but most of them never happenedвҖқ What a tremendous insight. I know this is not new wisdom, but it was the first time I have EVER had this experience of hearing my brain so clearly that I saw it for what it is. And now I will STOP trying to guess a made-up future.В  And begin to live more in the present. ** I am already in danger of becoming like Uncle Albert from Only Fools and Horses, when he says, вҖңDuring the warвҖҰвҖқ and everyone rolls their eyes - here he goes again. вҖңWhen I was on the CaminoвҖҰ.вҖқ My takeaway from this first week is to get out into nature and turn off your phone. And take your mind out for a long walk and see what you notice. Until next week, Buen Camino. Your Coach, Andy

title: Why you're still disorganized (EVEN with all the AI)
summary: TUNE IN TO LEARN: В  What if the solution to your productivity struggles isn't found in the next AI tool, but in mastering something you already possess?В  В  This episode explores a simple truth about personal growth and leadership: we've mistakenly believed that better tools, more knowledge, or the perfect system will finally help us get organized and reach our potential. Yet despite having access to all the knowledge we need to live better lives, most of us still struggle with consistency and follow-through. В  Why is that?В   The real operating system for success isn't external - it's internal. В  Drawing from research on emotional regulation and insights from experts and entrepreneurs like Derek Sivers and Yu-kai Chou, we unpack how the most important human problem to "fix" isn't knowledge or access to tools - it's self-leadership.В   At the heart of this episode is a practical five-step framework for emotional mastery to unlock self-leadership skills you need to get the most of all the tools and knowledge humanity has to offer - you'll discover how to cultivate your "emotional garden" to drive meaningful change. В  Ready to move beyond the endless search for the perfect productivity system? В  Join us to learn how emotional mastery creates the foundation for everything else in your life to work successfully. title: ШөЩ„Шӯ ШҜШұЩҲЩҶ ШҜШұ ЫҢЩҲШӘЫҢЩҲШЁ - ЩҮЩҒШӘ ШұЩҲШҙ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЩҮ ШЁШ§ ШӘЩҶШЁЩ„ЫҢ
summary: рҹ”әЩҫШ§ШҜЩғШіШӘ ШөЩ„Шӯ ШҜШұЩҲЩҶ ШҜШұ ЫҢЩҲШӘЫҢЩҲШЁ В  в–ӘпёҸЩ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ :В  7 ШұЩҲШҙ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЩҮ ШЁШ§ ШӘЩҶШЁЩ„ЫҢ В  (ЩҶШіШ®ЩҮ ЩҲЫҢШҜЫҢЩҲЫҢЫҢ Ш§ЩҫЫҢШІЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ ЩҮЩ… Щ…ЫҢШӘЩҲЩҶЫҢШҜ ШЁШ§ Ъ©Щ„ЫҢЪ© ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ Щ„ЫҢЩҶЪ© ШўШЁЫҢ ШІЫҢШұ ШҜШұ ЫҢЩҲШӘЫҢЩҲШЁ ШЁШЁЫҢЩҶЫҢШҜ) https://youtu.be/6xTMbBuQNac?si=MgCI5KgNBbm9DOYe В  ШӘЩҲШ¶ЩҠШӯШ§ШӘ : ШўШ®ШұЫҢЩҶ ШЁШ§ШұЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШӯШі Ъ©ШұШҜЫҢШҜ ШЁЫҢвҖҢШ§ЩҶЪҜЫҢШІЩҮ ЩҲ ШӘЩҶШЁЩ„ ЩҮШіШӘЫҢШҜШҢ ЪҶЩҮ Ш§ШӘЩҒШ§ЩӮЫҢ Ш§ЩҒШӘШ§ШҜШҹ Ъ©ШҜШ§Щ… Ъ©Ш§ШұЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұ ЩҫШҙШӘвҖҢЪҜЩҲШҙ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜШ§ШІЫҢШҜШҹ ШўЫҢШ§ Ш§Щ„ЪҜЩҲЫҢ Ш®Ш§ШөЫҢ ШҜШ§ШұШҜШҹ ШўЫҢШ§ ШӘЩҶШЁЩ„ЫҢ ШҙЩ…Ш§ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұ Ш°ЩҮЩҶЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ(Щ…Ш«Щ„Ш§ЩӢ ШЁЩҮШ§ЩҶЩҮвҖҢШӘШұШ§ШҙЫҢ) ЫҢШ§ Ш¬ШіЩ…ЫҢ(Ш®ШіШӘЪҜЫҢ Щ…ЩҒШұШ·)Шҹ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩҫЫҢШІЩҲШҜ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ ШӘЩҶШЁЩ„ЫҢ ЩҫШұШҜШ§Ш®ШӘЩ… ЩҲ Ы· ШұШ§ЩҮЪ©Ш§Шұ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЩҮ ШЁШ§ ШӘЩҶШЁЩ„ЫҢ ШұЩҲвҖҢ ШЁШұШ§ШӘЩҲЩҶ ЪҜЩҒШӘЩ… ШҙЩ…Ш§ ЩҮЩ… ШӘШ¬ШұШЁЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШҜШӘЩҲЩҶ ШұЩҲ ШЁЩҶЩҲЫҢШіЫҢШҜ В  Щ„ЫҢШіШӘ Ъ©Ш§Щ…Щ„ ЩҫШ§ШҜЪ©ШіШӘ ШөЩ„Шӯ ШҜШұЩҲЩҶ ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ЩҶШ§Щ„ ШӘЩ„ЪҜШұШ§Щ… ЩҮШіШӘ https://t.me/Sepblog/1998 В  .вҖў.вҖў.вҖў.вҖў.вҖў.вҖў.вҖў.вҖў.вҖў.вҖў.вҖў

title: From Sacrifice Mindset вҶ’ To Strategy Mindset
summary: Hi my besties! рҹ’•  In todayвҖҷs episode, weвҖҷre diving deep into mindset вҖ” specifically, how to shift from a sacrifice mindset to a strategymindset.  So often, we think weвҖҷre giving things up in order to grow... but what if we reframed that? What if instead of seeing change as loss, we saw it as a powerful, intentional move toward the life we actually want?  This episode is all about taking control of your life, shifting your perspective, and turning every вҖңsacrificeвҖқ into a smart strategy. Think of it as flipping a negative into a positive вҖ” and feeling empowered instead of deprived.  I LOVED this topic, and I think itвҖҷs going to seriously hit home for so many of you. title: Sydney Graves: From UK Midwife to Australian Content Creator (A Story of Reinvention & Manifestation)
summary: TodayвҖҷs guest is the brilliant and bold Sydney Graves, known online as @ukmidwife_down_under, whose journey from being told she вҖңwouldnвҖҷt amount to muchвҖқ to building a dream life across the world is nothing short of powerful. In this episode, we explore how Sydney went from struggling in school and being underestimated, to becoming a midwife in the UK, landing her dream hospital job, and eventually leaving it all behind to move to Australia and start over. After documenting her relocation journey online, SydneyвҖҷs following grew rapidly, leading her to pivot from her midwifery career into full-time content creation, travel and helping others relocate to Australia. Along the way, she overcame limiting beliefs, leaned into personal growth and even manifested many of the things she once hid on a vision board. We chat about:вңЁ How negative labels in childhood shaped (and motivated) her journeyвңЁ Landing her dream job as a UK midwife, then choosing to leave itвңЁ Moving to Australia and starting over from scratchвңЁ Building a personal brand and growing a social media followingвңЁ Her evolving relationship with manifestation and mindset workвңЁ What sheвҖҷs learned about alignment, risk and trusting the process Whether youвҖҷre craving reinvention, curious about starting over abroad, or wondering how to trust your intuition more deeply, this episode is for you. title: Stop Letting Shame Define YouвҖ”Transform it Into Your Power
summary: Shame can be a powerful and paralyzing emotion that keeps us stuck in unhealthy patterns. In this video, we dive deep into the difference between shame and guilt, explore the origin of shame, and discuss how to reframe it into a tool for personal growth. Learn how to take responsibility for your past without letting it define you, and discover how to transform shame into power, healing, and self-compassion. ItвҖҷs time to break free from the cycle of self-judgment and reclaim your true worth.

title: Andrew Tate Talks About Love. "Love Is Power, and Weak Men Have None"
summary: Love isnвҖҷt kindness. Love isnвҖҷt tolerance. Love isnвҖҷt letting people do whatever they want while you sit back and accept it. ThatвҖҷs weakness, and weakness destroys everything it touches. Real love is authority. Real love is discipline. A father doesnвҖҷt love his son by letting him be softвҖ”he pushes him, tests him, makes him strong. Society has rejected this, replacing a fatherвҖҷs love with blind acceptance, stripping men of standards, and calling it progress. ItвҖҷs not. ItвҖҷs the reason the world is falling apart.   Men chase money, status, and power because they crave loveвҖ”the respect of their peers, the loyalty of their women, the admiration of their children. But love without strength is worthless. A weak manвҖҷs love has no value because he has no value. If you want love to mean something, you must become something. That means suffering, struggle, and absolute capability. The most loved men in history werenвҖҷt softвҖ”they were powerful. They built, they conquered, and they protected what was theirs. ThatвҖҷs what love is.   You donвҖҷt beat the Matrix by begging for freedom. You donвҖҷt defeat evil by tolerating it. Love isnвҖҷt surrenderвҖ”itвҖҷs war. A man who truly loves stands firm, sets rules, and enforces them. ThatвҖҷs what the world has lost. ThatвҖҷs why men are lost. And until they understand that love comes with responsibility, pain, and power, they will remain weak, and the world will keep burning. summary: Healing trauma is more than psychological. It's physical, it's practical -- and here's the part where trauma experts are curiously silent -- it's spiritual. Abuse and neglect are injuries to the spirit, and where we are spiritually injured, a vulnerability opens to further harm from destructive people, and even from our own self-defeating behaviors. This is how the battle between good and evil is waged in our own lives, and in this four-video compilation, I share four of my most popular videos teaching how you can work with the power of good through prayer, meditation, courage and the willingness to take courageous actions to transform the damage that was done to you. title: When did you last feel excited?
summary: When was the last time you felt excited?В   Properly excited, like jump up and down excited? Light you up from the inside excited?В   In this episode, we explore what excitement actually is, why it matters more than we often realise, and how it quietly disappears from our lives, especially in seasons of burnout, responsibility, or emotional flatness. We unpack the difference between big, macro excitements (like holidays, love, or big life changes) and micro excitements (like receiving a text from a loved one or how happy your dog is at the beach).  This episode also looks at what blocks us from accessing excitement altogether. I share research-backed insights, embodiment practices, and reflection questions to help you reconnect with this essential and often neglected emotion. If youвҖҷre craving aliveness, meaning, or just a little spark again, this episode is for you. title: No Grail Without Dragons: Victor Giusfredi on Overcoming Trauma and Finding Purpose
podcast_name: Healing From Abuse & Trauma - Victim 2 Victor
summary: Send us a text вҖӢVictor Giusfredi is an author, mindset coach, and entrepreneur dedicated to guiding individuals toward self-actualisation and inner peace. His journey from a challenging childhood in Mendoza, Argentina, to becoming a successful entrepreneur and coach in Dingmans Ferry, PA, has equipped him with profound insights into resilience and personal growth. Victor's experiences, including launching multiple businesses and overcoming personal adversities, have shaped his mission to help others navigate their paths to fulfilment.В  In his book, No Grail Without Dragons: A ManвҖҷs Unconventional Path to Love, Purpose, and Peace, Victor shares his personal journey of overcoming trauma and limiting beliefs. The memoir offers readers tools and strategies for achieving lasting change, emphasizing the importance of confronting one's inner challenges to find meaning and purpose.В  Victor has also been featured in various media outlets, discussing topics ranging from navigating modern relationships to the benefits of running a purpose-driven business. His insights have been shared in interviews with Authority Magazine and on podcasts like The Doubtless Podcast, where he delves into themes of confidence, resilience, and personal development. вҖӢ Through his work, Victor emphasises the importance of self-knowledge, resilience, courage, faith, and direction as essential components in navigating life's complexities and finding love and purpose. вҖӢ For more information on Victor's work, visit his website: https://victorgiusfredi.com/ Support the show Connect with me: - Sign up to my Webinar: https://victim2victor.net/webinar-registration - Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/victim2victor  - Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/victim2victor_anuverma/ - Linkedin: https://www.linkedin.com/in/anuvermav2v - TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@victim_2_victor/ - Website: https://victim2victor.net - Victim 2 Victor Audio Book: https://adbl.co/3akVNCu  - Victim 2 Victor Book - Amazon:  https://www.amazon.com/Victim-Victor-inspirational-struggle-overcoming/dp/1649530951/ref=cm_cr_arp_mb_bdcrb_top?ie=UTF8

title: Compete, Conquer, Dominate: Blueprint to the Life You Should Be Living.

title: Episode 54 - Thomas Steinhauer
summary: Episode 54: Thomas Steinhauer вҖ” A Life in Service, A Legacy of Leadership In this powerful episode of The Conscious Hospitality Podcast, host David Arraya sits down with Thomas Steinhauer, a celebrated leader whose career at Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts helped define a generation of service excellence.  From his humble beginnings in the Austrian Alps to becoming one of the most respected figures in global hospitality, Thomas shares a journey filled with meaningful lessons, cultural insights, and enduring values. This conversation explores the deep influence of family, the power of gratitude, and the importance of staying grounded through decades of growth, change, and leadership. Thomas reflects on his formative years, the pivotal moments that shaped his career, and his unwavering belief in the importance of people, purpose, and presence. Whether youвҖҷre a student of leadership, a hospitality professional, or someone passionate about service and growth, this episode offers timeless wisdom and a behind-the-scenes look at what it truly means to lead with heart. Key Takeaways: Gratitude shapes our ability to reflect, grow, and lead. Early exposure to hospitality through family can instill lifelong values. Career transitions offer valuable insights into adaptability and resilience. Culture and connection are the foundation of exceptional service. Leadership begins with how we treat people every day. Listen now and explore the legacy of one of luxury hospitalityвҖҷs most beloved mentors. #conscioushospitality #thomassteinhauer #fourseasons #hospitalityleadership #hotellife #mentorship #serviceculture #executivemindset #gratitude #legacy

title: A Mother's Day Special: When You Thought It Would Be Better Than This w/Jessica Turner, Author, 8
summary: Just in time for Mother's Day, this special episode features Wall Street Journal bestselling author Jessica Turner sharing hard-earned wisdom about rebuilding when life veers dramatically off-script.  Whether you didn't get to have kids, your marriage is different than what you wanted, or like Jessica, your marriage took a turn in a different direction, Jessica discusses how to navigate heartache, as she had to at the end of her 16-year marriage after her husband came out, transforming heartbreak into a powerful story of resilience that's now the foundation of her upcoming book "I Thought It Would Be Better Than This." As Mel Robbins notes, Jessica's journey will "speak right to your soul if you're wrestling with disappointment, heartache, or the curveballs life throws at you." Through a heartfelt lens with Jessica, today we process grief, reclaim identity, and find moments for self-care during life's biggest transitions. This conversation goes beyond typical Mother's Day sentiments to honor the strength it takes to write a new story when the original draft falls apart. Jessica's authentic insights offer both practical strategies and emotional permission to anyone standing at the intersection of "this isn't what I expected" and "where do I go from here?" title: The Sunday Set Up Ep. 5: Proof Behind the Bet Manifestation Tool
summary: Happy MotherвҖҷs Day, Heal Squad! ItвҖҷs another Sunday Setup, still not greenlit but vibing high anyway, as Kev and his co-conspirator Anne ease us into the week. We kick things off with melancholy theme songs, Lutheran Easter specials, and why life isnвҖҷt about chasing joy or dodging painвҖ”itвҖҷs about being present for both. (Kitty rollercoaster > Space Mountain. Debate settled.) From there, itвҖҷs a gratitude deep dive, courtesy of The Magic and Dr. KimвҖҷs MindBody work. The lesson? Ungratefulness isnвҖҷt neutralвҖ”it takes from you. So when the negative thoughts hit, stand by... and respond with thanks. Even if itвҖҷs for the very thing that triggered you. Especially if it is. We also get a simple but game-changing reframe on manifestation. Forget complicated vision boardsвҖ”just feel the feeling now. Want love? Feel lovable. Want wealth? Feel successful. Want to move like youвҖҷre still 22? Feel like itвҖ”and maybe throw in a basketball. Kev walks us through his own case study, from spiritual mogul-ing to BD(E) energy, all wrapped in gratitude, shadow work, and a few Captain Lou Albano references for good measure. PLUS: meditation tips for movers, ADHD learning hacks, and why window sales legend Big Ed is KevвҖҷs new inner mascot. This weekвҖҷs Setup takeaway? YouвҖҷre not waiting to becomeвҖ”you already are. title: Does Remodeling Pay Off - Probably Not by Sam of Financial Samurai on Real Estate Investing
podcast_name: Optimal Living Daily - Personal Development and Self-Improvement
summary: Discover all of the podcasts in our network, search for specific episodes, get the Optimal Living Daily workbook, and learn more at: OLDPodcast.com. Episode 3593: Sam from Financial Samurai dismantles the myth that remodeling guarantees a return on investment, explaining that most renovations recoup only about 65% of their cost. He emphasizes that true profit comes from expansion, not remodeling, and encourages homeowners to remodel for personal enjoyment rather than profit, unless increasing livable square footage is the goal. Read along with the original article(s) here: https://www.financialsamurai.com/does-remodeling-pay-off-make-money/ Quotes to ponder: "Almost all property remodeling does not recoup the cost spent remodeling based on national averages." "Remodeling is a lifestyle choice with generally negative returns." "Increase the livable square footage of the home, and you increase the homeвҖҷs selling price. ItвҖҷs that simple." Episode references: 2023 Cost vs. Value Report by Remodeling Magazine: https://www.remodeling.hw.net/cost-vs-value/2023/ Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

title: Healing the Grief, the Love, and the Silence Around Your Mother
summary: If youвҖҷve ever wondered why certain relationships feel so hard or why youвҖҷve spent a lifetime craving a kind of love you canвҖҷt quite name, this special MotherвҖҷs Day edition will give you language for what youвҖҷve lived. Tamsen sits down with licensed professional counselor and bestselling author Kelly McDaniel to talk about Mother Hunger, a term she coined to describe the deep, often hidden longing many women carry from not getting the love, protection, or guidance they needed as daughters. They talk about: - Why so many women struggle with emotional eating, self-worth, and romantic patternsвҖ”and how it ties back to early attachment - What вҖңmother hungerвҖқ really means, and why most women donвҖҷt recognize it until midlife - How your earliest experiences shape your relationships, your body image, and your ability to trust - The emotional cost of caring for a mother who couldnвҖҷt care for you - What it means to crave love that feels safe, steady, and nourishing - How to begin healing, even if your mother is gone, unavailable, or unwilling - Why midlife is often the moment women finally face the grief theyвҖҷve been carrying for decades - How unresolved mother hunger can show up in love, sex, food, and friendships and what to do about it - Whether forgiveness is necessary in order to move on and what to do if an apology never comes This episode is for every woman who has ever said вҖңIвҖҷm fineвҖқ when she wasnвҖҷt and is finally ready to tell herself the truth. title: Celebrating Mother's Day: Balancing Traditions and Modern Expectations
podcast_name: Life after Kids with Drs. Brooke and Lynne
summary: About the Guest(s): Dr. Brooke and Dr. Lynn are the insightful voices behind the "Life After Kids" podcast. With a wealth of experience balancing careers and motherhood, these doctors bring relatable, deeply honest conversations to their audience. They focus on helping women navigate the complexities and joys of life after raising children, combining professional wisdom with personal anecdotes to support and empower their listeners. Episode Summary: In this engaging episode of the Life After Kids podcast, Dr. Brooke and Dr. Lynne explore the evolving meaning of Mother's Day. They delve into how perceptions and celebrations change from juggling young children to enjoying the company of grown kids, highlighting the differences in expectations and realities. The duo draws from personal experiences to offer relatable insights, providing listeners with a deeper understanding of what Mother's Day means at various stages of motherhood. The conversation moves through personal anecdotes, the significance of communicating needs, and the joys of shared experiences with older children. Topics like the cultural differences in celebrating Mother's Day internationally are explored, broadening the perspective on this universal celebration. Dr. Brooke and Dr. Lynne also touch on practical ideas to ensure Mother's Day is both meaningful and memorable, emphasizing the importance of intentionality and clear communication with family members. Their discussion not only provides practical advice but also celebrates the unique role of motherhood in and beyond the family unit. Key Takeaways:  	The celebration of Mother's Day evolves significantly as children grow, often shifting from a focus on rest to a desire for quality time with family.  	Clear communication about expectations can lead to more satisfying Mother's Day experiences, avoiding disappointment and unmet needs.  	Shared activities, like concerts or sporting events, create memorable family bonding moments and are a great way to celebrate.  	When planning Mother's Day, being intentional and proactive can help in creating a fulfilling experience for all involved.  	Understanding cultural perspectives on Mother's Day can enrich how we celebrate and appreciate this special occasion globally. Resources:  	Life After Kids Podcast Website  	Life after Kids Goals Framework Listen to the full episode for insightful discussions about the changing meaning of Mother's Day, and be sure to stay tuned for more enlightening content from the Life After Kids podcast. title: Ep 503 - Cut prescription costs, my 10 year portfolio insights & get an energy refund
podcast_name: The Money To The Masses Podcast
summary: On this week's show Damien explains how to cut the cost of prescriptions, particularly for those requiring multiple items or those receiving HRT. Damien then shares some key lessons from successfully managing his ВЈ50k investment portfolio over the last 10 years. title: Daren Knight
summary: This week I'm joined by my great friend and Coach Daren Knight @amatsudundanuclinic, an Amatsu therapist who has been alcohol-free for nearly seven years. This conversation is raw and deeply moving. Daren shares the profound story of losing his son, Conor and how that unimaginable loss cracked him open, forever changing him, his relationship with himself, with life and with alcohol. Daren reflects on grief, healing and the clarity that alcohol free living has brought him.  Find Daren at  DГәn Danu Amatsu Clinic

title: Authentic Embodiment with Full Moon in Scorpio: Weekly Astrology for May 12 - 18
summary: The astrology this week reflects deep feelings! It's a powerful time for getting more acquainted with the true nature of your Self. This week's astrology includes: Full Moon in Scorpio on Monday, May 12 at 9:56am PT: Illumination of deep feelings that may feel unexpected or revelatory, feeling or craving more freedom or autonomy, a release of long held bondage, feeling at odds or pulled in different directions. The asteroids Pallas Athene and Juno in aspect to this Full Moon symbolize an added desire to be strategically creative (Pallas Athene) and to protect what is most important to you (Juno). Sun conjunct Uranus in Taurus: Liberating parts of your Self that have been hidden away or unknown until now, inspired to seek a greater sense of authentic self-expression, interest in eclectic or unconventional ways of being. Mercury-Mars-Vesta-Pluto grand cross: Passionate thoughts or communication, upwelling of strong feelings or urges about a situation that can no longer be ignored or pushed away, agitation or anger that wants to be voiced. Unconscious material may be revealed. This may be a time of great inspiration where you feel compelled to find the answers to something thatвҖҷs important to you. Ceres moves into Aries on Friday, May 16: The asteroid Ceres symbolizes our experience of separating from our family of origin and creating our own unique and distinct life. Themes of interdependence vs. enmeshment in the family line may arise. You may become aware of your desire to be your own person vs. honor your family wishes when Ceres is activated. Ceres will be in Aries for nearly a year, marking an emphasis on prioritizing your independence, courage, and managing anxieties about embarking on your own journey. Ceres conjunct Saturn-Neptune: Questioning whatвҖҷs real vs. imposed. What are your thoughts, feelings, desires, and needs? Are there some things youвҖҷve internalized from your family that maybe are not yours to keep (such as expectations, guilt, or values). ... Reflection Questions: 1. Is there something youвҖҷve been feeling thatвҖҷs been itching to get out? 2. What do you really want this week? 3. How can you give voice to your feelings? ... Wishing you a week of ease, security, and safety рҹҰӢ Find out more about me and my work вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ hereвҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ .

title: Navigating Sobriety at 57: Donna's Five-Year Journey
podcast_name: The Not Drinking Alcohol Today Podcast
summary: Donna Bennett shares her five-year sobriety journey that began with an "Easter epiphany" during COVID lockdown, revealing how removing alcohol from her life led to profound personal transformation and newfound freedom. We cover so much in this episode, including: вҖў Recognising how alcohol had "sneakily" increased its influence over the years, particularly during lunchtime socialising вҖў Experiencing frightening blackouts and memory gaps during drinking sessions вҖў Using COVID lockdown as an opportunity to attempt sobriety without social pressure вҖў Discovering that moderate drinkers exist and learning to socialise without alcohol вҖў Finding connection and support through the Daybreak app after six weeks of solo sobriety вҖў Reading about alcohol's effects removed shame and the notion that lacking control was a personality flaw вҖў Experiencing better sleep, weight loss, and improved mental clarity after quitting вҖў Moving from "filling in time before dying" to finding joy and purpose in simple things If you're curious about taking a break from alcohol and are looking for inspiration, take a listen to this episode. title: #159- The Regenerative Power Within: Trinn Hitch on Minerals and Holistic Recovery
podcast_name: Peak Performance Perspectives with Mark Talukdar
summary: Ready to unlock your full potential and achieve peak performance in all areas of your life? Join host Mark Talukdar on this inspiring episode of Peak Performance Perspectives as he delves into the holistic approach to well-being with the insightful entrepreneur, TRINN HITCH, the founder and CEO of Jampha Tibetan Pharmacy. Forget the old idea that peak performance is just about physical strength. Trinn reveals her powerful secret: true peak performance blossoms when your mind, body, and spirit are in harmony. Discover her simple yet profound morning routine, starting with compassion-focused mantras as the sun rises. Learn how shifting your focus outward can reduce self-centered thoughts and connect you to a greater sense of purpose. For Trinn, peak performance isn't about constant striving; it's about achieving "mind and heart coherence." Learn what it means to live and listen to your heart's wisdom, rather than being overwhelmed by the constant chatter of your mind. Discover how cultivating this inner alignment fuels a continuous flow state, allowing you to perform at your best without feeling stuck or starting over. Offer: For all purchases a 10% discount NEW10 code may he used on purchases and a free consultation can ve scheduled at heal.me/jampha Bio Born into a faith-based family of military veterans, TrinnвҖҷs life has always been rooted in service. At 20, he dedicated himself to missionary work in rural Georgia, where he witnessed the deep connection between poverty and health. Later, his career in personal security reinforced his calling to protect others. But it was a brutal assaultвҖ”one that nearly cost him his legвҖ”that ultimately set him on the path of healing and his lifeвҖҷs mission: Jampha.Determined to free himself from addictive pain medications, Trinn immersed himself in plant medicine, uncovering the transformative power of terpenes and CBD. His first venture, Starseed, focused on organic pain relief, laying the foundation for what would become Jampha. In 2014, as he deepened his formulations, his mother faced a crisisвҖ”undergoing five brain surgeries in four weeks. Her choice to reject pharmaceuticals became another catalyst for his research, leading him to develop nutraceuticals that honored both science and spirit.Fate intervened once more when Trinn met Amchi Thubten Lekshe, a Seattle native trained in the ancient Sowa Rigpa healing traditions by Tibetan Lamas and Amchi Physicians. This fortuitous connection deepened JamphaвҖҷs evolution, merging Tibetan wisdom with modern plant-based medicine. As the world was reshaped by the COVID-19 pandemic, Jampha expanded its mission, striving to be the highest expression of Tibetan mineral and plant medicine.вҖңLoving kindness must always guide our healing path,вҖқ Trinn affirms. вҖңOur bodies understand their own healing needsвҖ”we just need to support them with the entourage effect of the abundant botanical and mineral resources we have already been gifted.вҖқMore information about Jampha is available on the web at:www.jampha.comTikTok @jhampatibetanwellnessInstagram @jhampatibetanpharmacyFacebook @jamphatibetanwellness Jampha can be reached through www.jampha.com, where products can be purchased online.
