title: You Are Only ONE Decision Away From Completely Changing Your Life!
summary: рҹ‘Ү SUBSCRIBE TO MY YOUTUBE CHANNEL - so this show can reach more people рҹ‘Ү https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCIprGZAdzn3ZqgLmDuibYcw?sub_confirmation=1 Click the Link Below to Subscribe to my email list to MAXOUT your life (all value, no fluff)  https://konect.to/edmylett рҹ’Ҙ Get my exclusive Monday Motivation training in GrowthDay, the worldвҖҷs #1 app for advanced mindset and personal development. Visit https://growthday.com/ed. This show is sponsored by GrowthDay. What if youвҖҷre just ONE decision away from the life you were meant to live? In this special Mashup episode, I bring you the truth about how small shiftsвҖ”made consistentlyвҖ”can completely transform your trajectory. IвҖҷve pulled together some of the most impactful moments from conversations with James Clear, Tony Robbins, and Ben Newman. WeвҖҷre not talking about hype. WeвҖҷre talking about real habits, real standards, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. These are the people I trust, the ones I learn from, and today, weвҖҷre sharing that with YOU. I open this episode by breaking down the invisible separator between the winners and everyone else. ItвҖҷs not luck. ItвҖҷs not some secret hack. ItвҖҷs showing up every dayвҖ”especially when you donвҖҷt feel like it. James Clear then jumps in with the science behind the 1% rule and how your identity changes when you commit to small wins. Tony Robbins takes it even further with the emotional conditioning that locks transformation into your body and your mind. And Ben Newman? He makes it clear why your standards, your rituals, and your energy determine your ceilingвҖ”or break through it. This conversation isnвҖҷt about gimmicks. ItвҖҷs about hunger. ItвҖҷs about desperation. ItвҖҷs about focus. And itвҖҷs about the truth that every one of us is far closer to greatness than we believe. Maybe you've been pacing yourself because you think itвҖҷs going to take 10 years. But what if itвҖҷs actually one decision away? HereвҖҷs the truth: your life doesnвҖҷt change when everything goes right. It changes when you commit to the small things, repeatedly, relentlesslyвҖ”on the hard days, in the quiet moments, when no oneвҖҷs watching. Because the person who lays one brick a dayвҖ”while others only lay bricks when they feel like itвҖ”wins in the end. What You'll Learn: - Why the 1% rule can radically reshape your identity (James Clear) - How to create unstoppable emotional momentum (Tony Robbins) - The real reason most people never change, and how to break free - How hunger and desperation are the fuel to high performance (Ben Newman) - The truth about balance, success, and why one area of your life impacts all the others - What it means to go from possibility thinker to possibility achiever You're closer than you think. You werenвҖҷt made to be average. You were made to do something GREAT. TodayвҖҷs the day you stop pacing, and start sprintingвҖ”with belief, with focus, and with the decision that it starts NOW. title: How Trade Shaped the Modern World
summary: In this episode of Saturday Morning Muse, Andrew Temte explores the evolution of trade from barter systems to modern currency. He discusses the inefficiencies of barter, such as the double coincidence of wants and the challenges of measuring value. Temte highlights the historical advancements that arose from the need to improve trade, including the development of writing, measurement systems, and currency. He emphasizes the importance of trade in human history and its role in fostering cooperation and economic growth.

title: Using Alcohol to Treat Adult ADHD | Breaking Free at 50 | E800
summary: What if the glass of wine you reach for every evening is actually treating something deeper than just stress?  Amy takes us through her journey from teenage experimentation to mommy wine culture, revealing how alcohol became her trusted companion through life's challenges. As a high-functioning professional juggling parenting, caregiving responsibilities, and undiagnosed issues, she found herself using alcohol to treat adult ADHD without even realizing it. It wasn't until her son developed addiction issues that Amy began questioning her own relationship with alcohol. Through family therapy, reading This Naked Mind, and completing The Alcohol Experiment, Amy discovered how alcohol was masking her authentic needs while creating more anxiety and depression. Now alcohol-free for four years, she shares how breaking this cycle led to profound personal growth and genuine joy. In this episode, Annie and Amy discuss: Growing up in a conservative "Leave It to Beaver" household Teenage experimentation with alcohol as rebellion Transitioning from weekend binge drinking to daily wine culture How mommy wine culture normalized problematic drinking Using alcohol to treat adult ADHD without realizing it The connection between disordered eating and alcohol consumption Parenting adopted children with unique challenges How family therapy led to self-discovery Breaking free from alcohol at age 51 Finding authentic joy and peace in midlife And other topicsвҖҰ Episode links: Start reading This Naked Mind - https://thisnakedmind.com/book Join The Alcohol Experiment - www.alcoholexperiment.com Related Episodes: EP 719: Naked Life Story вҖ“ Katie W. - https://thisnakedmind.com/ep-719-naked-life-story-katie-w/ EP 110: Reader Question вҖ“ I stopped drinking alcohol but now I binge on Netflix and ice cream. WhatвҖҷs the deal? - https://thisnakedmind.com/ep-110-reader-question-stopped-drinking-alcohol-now-binge-netflix-ice-cream-whats-deal/ EP 678: ReaderвҖҷs Question вҖ“ How to Stop Restricting Food while Drinking? - https://thisnakedmind.com/ep-678-readers-question-how-to-stop-restricting-food-while-drinking/ Ready to take the next step on your journey?В  Visit https://learn.thisnakedmind.com/podcast-resources for free resources, programs, and more. title: Think Like a Marketer with Bianca Baumann and Mike Taylor
summary: In this episode of the Learning While Working Podcast, Bianca Baumann and Mike Taylor share insights from their latest book, вҖҳThink Like a Marketer, Train Like an L&D Pro: Strategies to Ignite LearningвҖҷ, and dive into actionable strategies that L&D professionals can borrow from the world of marketing. Discover why shifting your mindset from вҖңorder takerвҖқ to strategic business partner is essential for todayвҖҷs L&D professionals. We also uncover how to personalise learning at scale, run effective learning campaigns, and apply marketing-inspired strategies to create learning experiences that resonate вҖ“ and deliver results. About В Bianca Baumann Bianca Baumann is a seasoned learning strategist, international speaker, and author who specialises in innovative workforce enablement solutions. With deep expertise across L&D, employee experience, performance management, and future workforce planning, she partners with global organisations to design data-driven, business-aligned strategies. Known for her work with C-suite leaders and high-performing teams, Bianca brings a sharp focus on transforming human capital into a competitive advantage. About В Mike Taylor Mike Taylor is a learning consultant on a mission to eliminate boring, ineffective training. With hands-on experience across nearly every role in L&D, he combines practical know-how with a passion for finding smarter, more engaging ways to help people learn. A self-described вҖңrecovering bad student,вҖқ Mike brings deep empathy for learners and a talent for simplifying complex topics. He helps organisations design impactful, tech-savvy learning experiences that connect with todayвҖҷs distracted workforce. Key takeaways: Start small and donвҖҷt be intimidated: You donвҖҷt need a big budget or fancy tools to bring marketing thinking into learning. Simple, actionable steps вҖ“ even with no-cost tools вҖ“ make a difference. (And, get others excited to join in!)Adopt a learner-centred mindset, not just content creation: Marketing excels at understanding audiences. Use tactics like research-based personas and journey mapping to make learning experiences resonate and drive business outcomes.Think in campaigns, not just courses: Like marketers, L&D should use content strategically вҖ“ spread it across multiple channels, personalise when possible, and align everything to the bigger business workflow.Chapters: (00:00:00) Introduction(00:06:11) Design learning for cognitive understanding(00:07:19) From order taker to partner(00:11:13) Prioritising actionable sales metrics(00:14:39) Marketing funnel concept for learning(00:17:43) Reframing persona use in design(00:23:25) Evolving learning journeys(00:27:25) Personalisation in marketing technology(00:29:01) Content organisation for enhanced learningLinks from the podcast: Connect with Bianca Baumann on LinkedInConnect with Mike Taylor on LinkedInTrain Like A Marketer WebsiteThink Like a Marketer, Train Like an L&D Pro вҖ“ Book

title: #158- Peak Performance Simplified | Know, Act, Achieve with John James
podcast_name: Peak Performance Perspectives with Mark Talukdar
summary: Welcome to Peak Performance Perspective! In this exciting episode, your host welcomes the insightful John , a professional speaker, NLP Trainer, results coach, and PhD in Clinical Hypnotherapy. John shares his simple yet powerful definition of peak performance: knowing what you want and taking action to get it. John dives deep into this common challenge, revealing that many people know what they don't want but struggle to identify their true goals. Using the engaging analogy of a travel agent, John guides us on how to pinpoint our desired destination in life, the crucial first step towards achieving peak performance. Just like planning a trip, once you know where you want to go, you can figure out the best way to get there вҖ“ whether by car, train, bus, or plane. John emphasizes the importance of self-awareness in this journey. He poses thought-provoking questions to help you cut through the noise of distractions and shiny objects to uncover your genuine aspirations. This episode is packed with actionable insights to help you gain clarity and start moving towards a life of purpose and fulfillment. In our rapid-fire question segment, John shares his favorite NLP technique (anchoring for emotional control), his preference between coffee and green juice (green juice!), the one book he'd take to a deserted island ("Think and Grow Rich"), his guiding quote ("You are what you think"), and the sport that comes to mind when he hears "transformation" or "change" (that's it!). Join us for this inspiring conversation with John James and take the first step towards unlocking your peak performance! title: How to Identify a Narcissist, Top Phrases That Give Them Away with Rebecca Zung on Negotiate Your Best Life #686

title: How To Get 'Unstuck' From Habits  And Thinking Patterns That Are Not Serving You, and Much, Much More With Dr Sophie Mort
summary: This was a fascinating, wide-ranging, amusing and highly useful conversation if you're a human being!  Dr Sophie Mort, known as Dr Soph is a UK Sunday Times best selling author of the book "A Manual For Being Human'В  and in today's podcast we discuss the topic of her new book 'Unstuck', which is just brilliant.  It's a wide-ranging conversation about habit formation, automations in our life, cognitive biases, drama triangles and much more. I guarantee there's be an enjoyable mix of nods of acknowledgement, laughter and aha moments as you listen to it.В   Dr Soph is a clinical psychologist with a Degree in Psychology, Masters Degree in Neuroscience and PhD in Psychology. She's also witty, engaging, insightful and a great communicator.  What You'll Learn:  The Art of Habit Formation: Dive into why over 40% of our daily behaviours are habits, running on autopilot without our conscious decision-making.  Cue-Craving-Reward: Explore the science behind habits, including the crucial role cues play in triggering automatic behaviours.  Willpower & Habit Change: Discover why willpower alone isn't enough and learn to leverage the power of time-of-day biology to change habits effectively.  Understanding Heuristics: Get to know about mental shortcuts that heavily influence our decision-making processes.  Combatting Hyperbolic Discounting: Learn why we often choose immediate rewards over future benefits and how it impacts our decision-making.  Overcoming Self-Sabotage: Delve into the reasons behind self-sabotage and how we can overcome the hurdles that keep us from reaching our full potential.  The Drama Triangle: Understand repeated roles we play in conflict situations and how to break free from them.  Key Takeaways:   Autopilot Awareness: Recognising that a significant portion of our day is driven by habit is the first step to change. BiologyвҖҷs Role: Understanding the importance of timing and how our neurochemistry can be leveraged is crucial for effective habit change. Heuristics and Decision Making: These mental shortcuts explain many of our choices and behaviours, helping us understand and improve them. Be Your Own Motivation: Creating instigation habits and setting realistic goals can boost motivation and habit formation. Overcoming Cognitive Traps: Recognising and acknowledging mental traps like confirmation bias helps in making better decisions. Self-Worth and Community: Self-esteem is deeply tied to belonging and confidence вҖ“ building these can improve overall well-being.  Resources:   Read "Unstuck": Dive into Dr. Moore's comprehensive guide on breaking free from habits. A Manual for Being Human: Explore more insights from Dr. Moore in her first acclaimed book. Connect with Dr. Sophie:  Website: drsoph.com Instagram: @drsoph   Support and Share:  If you found value in this episode, please consider subscribing, rating, and leaving a review on your preferred podcast platform. Your support helps us reach more people with important insights like these.  Share this episode with someone who might be battling with personal habits or looking to improve their mental well-being вҖ“ this conversation could be the breakthrough they need.  Thank you for exploring the paths to becoming unstuck and reshaping your habits with us! See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: 035: The Power of Prayer Journaling in Making Life Decisions with Kari Bartkus
summary: Show Notes Can journaling really help you make better decisions вҖ” and hear from God more clearly? In this episode, spiritual director and journal coach Kari Bartkus shares how prayer journaling creates space to process emotions, clarify your values, and invite God into your decision-making. We talk about journaling methods beyond just pros and cons lists, how to overcome the fear of someone reading your journal, and what to do when you're staring at a blank page. I also share a powerful story of how God used the most random word in my journaling time to redirect my business вҖ” and it all started with simply writing my prayers. Links From This Episode: вңЁ KariвҖҷs Website & Free Journaling Workshop: lovedoesthat.orgвңЁ KariвҖҷs Podcast Episode Featuring Lauren: lovedoesthat.org/177вңЁ Rocketbook Notebook: getrocketbook.comвңЁ Daily Kairos Journal: dailykairos.comвңЁ Val Marie Paper Prayer Journal: valmariepaper.com Bible Verses Mentioned: рҹ“– Romans 8:26 вҖ“ The Spirit intercedes for usрҹ“– James 1:5 вҖ“ If any of you lacks wisdom, ask Godрҹ“– Matthew 7:7 вҖ“ Ask, seek, knockрҹ“– Habakkuk 2:2 вҖ“ Write the vision and make it plain Key Points: рҹ”‘ Journaling helps bring clarity by getting thoughts out of your head and onto paperрҹ”‘ Prayer journaling isnвҖҷt just about writing a list вҖ” itвҖҷs about dialogue with Godрҹ”‘ You can use lists, imagination-based journaling, or art/doodles to explore decisionsрҹ”‘ Journaling reveals patterns, values, and GodвҖҷs voice in surprising waysрҹ”‘ You donвҖҷt have to keep every journal entry вҖ” write freely, then decide what to keepрҹ”‘ ThereвҖҷs no вҖңrightвҖқ way to journal; the best method is the one youвҖҷll actually use Your Action Step for the Week: рҹ–ҠпёҸ Pick up a journal and start writing. If you already journal regularly, try a new method вҖ” whether itвҖҷs list-making, imagination prayer, or doodling your thoughts. And if you're stuck, simply invite God in and write whatвҖҷs on your heart. title: Feeling Your Emotions Without Alcohol | Understanding the Urge to Drink | Alcohol Freedom Coaching | E799
summary: What happens when you finally feel your emotions instead of trying to numb them with alcohol? In this episode, we hear two powerful coaching conversations. Coach Zoe talks with Jill, who is finding her way back to her spiritual side after years of working in a high-stress job where drinking was common. Coach Ruby works with Ambur, a former college athlete and mom of two young kids, who is now 120 days alcohol-free. Both women are learning how to feel their emotions instead of escaping them. TheyвҖҷre on the road to understanding the urge to drink as a sign that they were feeling disconnected from who they really are. These sessions show how self-kindness and body awareness can take the place of alcohol when it comes to handling tough emotions. In JillвҖҷs session with Coach Zoe:   Returning to neglected areas of life, like spirituality, after quitting.   Recognizing how career focus can inadvertently support drinking habits.   Learning to connect with and process bodily sensations and emotions.   Exploring meditation and somatic work as tools for emotional regulation.   Utilizing anchoring techniques to stay present during discomfort.   Understanding self-compassion as a key to emotional processing.   The significance of acknowledging and learning from past drinking experiences   and moreвҖҰ In AmburвҖҷs session with Coach Ruby:   Navigating early motherhood and perfectionism without alcohol   Celebrating 120 days alcohol-free and learning to feel again   Understanding the urge to drink as a desire to escape loneliness   Using meditation, exercise, and truth-telling as new tools   Processing emotions instead of labeling them as вҖңbadвҖқ   Letting go of judgment and learning to sit with discomfort   Replacing sneaky drinking with honest communication   Exploring what a sustainable alcohol-free life looks like   and moreвҖҰ Zoe Ewart is a Certified Naked Mind Senior Coach who brings her experience and understanding to help with the tricky parts of lifeвҖҷs big changes. Her coaching gives you an enjoyable, light-hearted, and safe environment to effortlessly take back control of alcohol so you can feel better physically, mentally, and spiritually. Zoe taught Pilates for 15 years. She has four adult children and more animals than the Ark ever had. Learn more about Coach Zoe: https://thisnakedmind.com/coach/zoe-ewart/В  Ruby Williams is a certified Naked Mind Senior Coach dedicated to helping people rethink their relationship with alcohol. With 19 years of experience in the wine industry, Ruby understands how alcohol can become a regular part of life, especially in social or stressful situations. After finding freedom in 2019, she was empowered to improve her health, lose weight, and achieve lifelong goals. Ruby uses thoughtful questioning to help clients shift their mindset and build a more balanced, fulfilling, and healthier relationship with alcohol. Learn more about Coach Ruby: https://thisnakedmind.com/coach/ruby-williams/

title: Psychologist Explains: Why Menopause IsnвҖҷt Making You Crazy
summary: Hot flashes arenвҖҷt the problemвҖ”gaslighting is. Hilary takes aim at the menopause conversation and asks the question no one else is asking: What if this isnвҖҷt your downfall, but your awakening? In this episode, she breaks down the cultural scripts, conditioning, and quiet misogyny wrapped in every вҖңcrazy menopausal womanвҖқ meme, and replaces it with truth, clarity, and power. This is not a hormonal crisis. This is your moment. title: Dismissive Avoidant Attachment & Depression: Are They Correlated?
summary: 14-Day All-Access Trial: Transform Your Life with Expert-Led Mental Health & Personal Growth Programs https://attachment.personaldevelopmentschool.com/mha-month?utm_source=podcast&utm_campaign=mha-month&utm_medium=organic&el=podcast В  Is there a hidden link between dismissive avoidant attachment and chronic depression? In this deeply insightful episode of The Thais Gibson Podcast, Thais explores the strong correlation between dismissive avoidant attachment styles and low-level, often chronic depression. Learn why emotional repression, unmet needs, and painful belief systems contribute to emotional numbnessвҖ”and how true healing is possible. В  What YouвҖҷll Learn in This Episode: вң”пёҸ Why chronic emotional repression can lead to emotional "toxicity" over time вң”пёҸ How dismissive avoidants unconsciously disconnect from core emotional needs вң”пёҸ The role of unmet needs and negative belief imprints in depression вң”пёҸ Why love and connection are biologically essential for emotional well-being вң”пёҸ How painful childhood associations with vulnerability cause long-term inner conflict вң”пёҸ The importance of reprogramming beliefs about vulnerability, love, and connection вң”пёҸ How gradual exposure and conscious reprogramming can create profound healing В  Thais also shares practical, science-backed strategies to break the cycle of emotional disconnection, reconnect with your own feelings, and heal depressive patterns at the root. title: Clean Pain and Dirty Pain
summary: Have you ever noticed how some emotional pain seems to help you grow, while other types just keep you stuck in a cycle of suffering?  В   I've been fascinated by this distinction for years, and after mentioning it briefly at a recent speaking engagement, I realized I needed to dedicate an entire episode to exploring this powerful concept.  В   Join me this week to learn how to recognize the difference between clean pain and dirty pain in your own life and why making this distinction matters so much. YouвҖҷll hear personal examples of both clean and dirty pain, why we often choose dirty pain to avoid the necessary clean pain, and practical guidance on how to process emotions in a way that leads to growth rather than stagnation.  В   Get full show notes, transcript, and more information here: https://jodymoore.com/512

title: How to never forget to do things. A 4-step system to manage an overwhelmed mind.
summary: TUNE IN TO LEARN: В  Every day we wake up to expanding to-do lists, countless open tabs, and the gnawing feeling that we're falling behind. Sound familiar? В  It's not just you - it's the natural result of living in an accelerating world where AI, content, and expectations multiply faster than we can keep up.В   But what if your frustration with "not getting things done" isn't a personal failure but simply the result of working against your brain's natural wiring? В  In this episode we break down the behavioral science behind why willpower alone fails and how to deal with increasing forgetfulness :) - a practical four-step system will transform how you approach tasks and responsibilities. В  Quit frustration - welcome to getting all the IMPORTANT done consistently and reliably. В  Drawing from Stanford scientist BJ Fogg's groundbreaking behavior model (Behavior = Motivation + Ability + Prompt) and insights from neuroscience, We reveal why our brains function much like apps - without the right trigger, action simply doesn't happen. Listen now, and remember to share this episode with someone who might benefit from breaking free of overwhelm and building systems that actually work. title: Anti-Fragile: How to benefit from uncertainty and build resilience into your systems
podcast_name: Growth Mindset Psychology: The Science of Self-Improvement
summary: A system that grows stronger from chaos? ThatвҖҷs not science fiction. ItвҖҷs natureвҖҷs original design. Nassim Taleb's Antifragile isn't just a book. ItвҖҷs a lensвҖ”a way to see the world not as a series of risks to be avoided, but as opportunities to evolve. In this episode, we explore what it means to thrive in disorder. From ancient myths like the Hydra, to modern jobs and biological systems, we trace the pattern: fragility is punished, antifragility is rewarded. What if you built a life where setbacks made you better, not bitter? Where unpredictability wasnвҖҷt a threat, but a catalyst? ThatвҖҷs the shift weвҖҷre inviting you to make. Takeaways:    Design your systems to gain from shocks, not just resist them.    Embrace small failuresвҖ”they prevent catastrophic ones.    Seek environments where your upside grows with uncertainty. title: The Parent Balancing Act: Supportive Parenting without Overdoing It.
summary: In this episode, we explore a modern parenting paradox: how to be a supportive parent without overdoing it. Joining us again is researcher and family systems therapist Phil Kelver from Kansas, USA, sharing insights from his 20-year study of 50 families. Together, we unpack his fascinating research about the effects of over-investing in our children, how parents can spot the subtle signs of doing too much, and what it takes to find a healthier balance in today's high-pressure parenting culture. A thoughtful, eye-opening conversation for anyone navigating the fine line between care and control. We explore: What the data reveals about over-investment and its impact on both kids and parents Subtle signs that you might be overdoing itвҖ”and how to spot them in your daily life Practical insights to help you stay engaged without becoming enmeshed The real-world pressures making it hard for parents to step back, and how to navigate them with confidence Whether youвҖҷre a parent, educator, or just curious about the changing dynamics of modern family life, this episode offers thoughtful reflection and guidance on finding that elusive middle ground. Tune in for a conversation thatвҖҷs honest, research-informed, and full of takeaways to help you parent with clarityвҖ”not anxiety. title: #356 - ALONEMENT: HOW TO BE ALONE & ABSOLUTELY OWN IT WITH FRANCESCA SPECTER
podcast_name: Your Dream Life with Kristina Karlsson
summary: Hello!  In this weekвҖҷs episode, I had the pleasure of sitting down with the inspiring Francesca SpecterвҖ”author of Alonement: How to Be Alone and Absolutely Own It.  And wow, what a beautiful and important conversation this turned out to be.  If the idea of being alone feels scary or uncomfortable - or maybe even a bit boring - then this episode is 100% for you.  WeвҖҷre diving into why solitude isnвҖҷt something to escapeвҖҰ itвҖҷs something to embrace.  Francesca shares so honestly her own journey as an extrovert learning to love her own company, and how solitude has become a joyful, nourishing part of her life - how it can become that for you too - and how it benefits you.  We talk about:    The difference between loneliness and alonementвҖ”and why it matters.    How to carve out alone time (even with a full life) without guilt.    Practical tips to enjoy your own company and use solitude as a tool for creativity and clarity.    How journaling, rituals, and walking in nature can become sacred acts of self-connection.    YouвҖҷll likely walk away with a new perspective on what it means to be aloneвҖ”and how choosing solitude can actually bring you closer to yourself, your dreams, and your deepest clarity.  So light a candle, grab a cuppa, or pop in your ear buds and go walking... and press play on this warm and soul-nourishing chat.  Whether you're an introvert, extrovert, or somewhere in between, thereвҖҷs something truly powerful here for you.  Because you, my friend, are worth taking time for. podcast_name: Peak Performance Perspectives with Mark Talukdar
summary: In this special episode of the Peak Performance Perspectives Podcast, host Mark Talukdar talks with Tracey Sundkvist- a brave woman with a big heart. Her story is full of hard times, but also full of hope, healing, and new beginnings. Tracey grew up in a very hard place. As a young girl, she felt lost, scared, and alone. Bad things happened that no child should go through. But instead of giving up, Tracey chose to fight for a better life. She shares how she lived through pain, abuse, and addiction and how she found her way out. Mark and Tracey talk about what it's like to carry heavy feelings and how hard it can be to ask for help. Tracey opens up about the moment she said вҖңenough is enough.вҖқ That moment changed her whole life. She started a journey of healing, learning, and helping others. In this episode, you will hear how Tracey: Faced her darkest moments with courage Found support through therapy, coaching, and deep inner work Learned to forgive herself and others Found her voice and started sharing her story to inspire people Built a life full of meaning, faith, and love This episode is for anyone who has ever felt stuck, alone, or not good enough. ItвҖҷs for those who are on their own healing path or who want to understand others better. TraceyвҖҷs story is a light in the dark. It shows that no matter how far you fall, you can rise again. Mark brings calm and care to the conversation. He asks deep questions and gives Tracey the space to share her truth. Together, they talk about real pain and real hope. You will leave this episode feeling moved, inspired, and stronger than before. рҹҺ§ Tune in now to hear TraceyвҖҷs powerful journey. title: Episode 16: Behind the Food Blogger: Aiken Chia on Comfort Eating, Identity & Healing
podcast_name: Breaking the Cycle with Natassia Dsouza
summary: This episode of Breaking the Cycle Takes Singapore features my client and one of SingaporeвҖҷs most loved food and travel creators вҖ” Aiken Chia. Together, we explore the layered relationship heвҖҷs had with food: from using it to soothe childhood wounds to realizing that even in an industry where bigger bodies are more accepted, emotional struggle doesnвҖҷt simply disappear. Aiken shares what it was like to grow up with food as his safest outlet, how that attachment shaped his adulthood, and what it really means to regulate your nervous system without turning to eating. We talk about the emotional cost of constantly performing wellness on the outside while feeling dysregulated on the inside. This isnвҖҷt a weight loss story. ItвҖҷs a healing story. ItвҖҷs about detaching from food not to restrict, but to reconnect. If food has been your anchor, your escape, or your identity, this is one you donвҖҷt want to miss.

title: Health Care and Weight Loss with Dr. Jennifer Zelovitzky - Spring 2025
summary: In this empowering episode, Dr. Jennifer ZelovitzkyвҖ”Medical Doctor, Certified Menopause Practitioner, and host of the Medsplaining PodcastвҖ”joins Gina for a no-nonsense conversation about owning your health, trusting your body, and navigating weight loss with confidence. From understanding how your doctor can be a true partner in your journey to recognizing that medications arenвҖҷt a magic fix, Dr. Jennifer offers practical, science-backed insights on everything from aging and menopause to supplements and muscle health. This is a must-listen for anyone ready to shift their mindset, advocate for their own wellness, and make sustainable, meaningful change beyond the scale. title: Tim Talk | Characteristics of Complex Trauma: Create What They Hate
summary: People from Complex Trauma often create the very things they hate and are trying to avoid - like abandonment, disrespect and repeating the same mistakes. Why is that? And what can they do to change? This is a lecture originally published on YouTube on Aug 6, 2021. title: Throwback to Ep81: You Cannot Outperform Yourself
summary: Start Your Transformation NowвҒ В  How often have you set goals or wanted something badly only to find that you fail over and over in achieving it? If you can say yes to the line above, youвҖҷre not alone, this is the pattern for most people and the cause is simple, people are setting goals for things that are not in their self-image. People are trying to perform at levels that are outside their self-image and subconscious identity. Many years ago I remember hearing the motivational speaker Les Brown said, вҖңYou cannot operate outside of the context of the vision you have for yourself.вҖқ Which basically means, you cannot be, do or have what you are not. In this episode I talk about:[1:24] What your self-image is and how it was created[2:21] The cycle of self-identity reinforcement[5:57] The illusion of self-image[15:53] Why youвҖҷll never live up to your full potential[27:22] How to reverse engineer your life from the inside out[31:01] And, How nothing in your life will change until you change ThereвҖҷs an old Tibetan saying, вҖңWhat you are on the inside is what you are on the outside.вҖқ If you want more in your life you must BE more in life. There is absolutely no escape from this concept. You must first be it in your mind before you can have it in your life. Listen, apply, and enjoy!В  Transformational TakeawayВ  You cannot outperform yourself! title: This Kind of Stress Could Actually Heal You: With Jeff Krasno
summary: What if the answer to burnout, brain fog, and hormone chaos isnвҖҷt less stressвҖҰ but better stress? This week, IвҖҷm joined by Jeff Krasno вҖ”author of Good Stress, host of the top-rated podcast Commune, and co-founder of Commune, a revolutionary digital wellness platform thatвҖҷs helped millions rethink how they live, heal, and grow. At 50, Jeff was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes. Instead of giving in to decline, he chose to radically change his lifeвҖ”through cold plunges, fasting, heat, breathwork, tough conversations, and a deep dive into the science of resilience. What happened next was nothing short of transformation. Jeff's approach is both deeply human and brilliantly backed by researchвҖ”and it couldnвҖҷt be more relevant for women in midlife. If you've ever felt inflamed, exhausted, or stuck in a body that no longer feels like yoursвҖҰ this episode will meet you there, and hand you a way forward. вңЁ CONNECT WITH JEFF:Instagram вҶ’ @jeffkrasnoWebsite вҶ’ www.onecommune.comBook вҶ’ Good StressPodcast вҶ’ Commune with Jeff Krasno вңЁ CONNECT WITH ME:Personal Instagram вҶ’ @chels_hollandBusiness Instagram вҶ’ @notyoung.coWebsite + Blog вҶ’ www.notyoung.comThe Baseline (our bestselling perimenopause supplement) вҶ’ Check it out here

title: Using Data to Make the World Better with Andrea Jones-Rooy
podcast_name: Make it a Great One with Dr. Dan
summary: Dr. Dan interviews Andrea Jones-Rooy вҖ“ data scientist, educator, podcast host, comedian, and circus performer вҖ“ about how data informs our journeys to being our best selves. Based on her life of weaving in between the worlds of science and art, Andrea believes that itвҖҷs at the intersection of science and art that we can make the biggest, most life-changing, and world-changing discoveries. AndreaвҖҷs multi-hyphenate careers (she is equally comfortable entertaining at comedy clubs, speaking to Fortune 500 companies, and sitting behind the podcast mic) prove data is the foundation of how we live, how we make decisions, and how we can make the world a better place. Dr. Dan and Andrea define data and then discuss the data related to our current moment in history. They explore why democratizing data science empowers everyoneвҖ”not just statisticiansвҖ”to make sense of the numbers shaping our world. From political polarization to public health to how we read headlines, Dr. Dan and Andrea tell us how we can all use data for good.В В  Andrea Jones-Rooy hosts the podcast Behind The Data where she discusses how data is actually collected, its limitations, and offers advice on how to factor it into decision-making. Andrea believes thereвҖҷs no such thing as being just a вҖңmath personвҖқ or a вҖңcreativeвҖқ вҖ“ she insists weвҖҷre all capable of brilliance in both. вҖңItвҖҷs all about finding the right way to unlock those skills and discovering what weвҖҷre truly capable of.вҖқВ  Listeners will learn from, laugh, and love todayвҖҷs discussion - even if they hate math!В  For more information www.jonesrooy.com and follow @jonesrooy on Instagram. Please listen, follow, rate, and review Make It a Great One on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or wherever you listen to podcasts. Follow @drdanpeters on social media. Visit www.drdanpeters.com and send your questions or guest pitches to podcast@drdanpeters.com. title: How Do the Best Coaches Inspire Real Change? | Dr. Marcia Reynolds
summary: Curious about your own resilience? As a coach, you can take the Resilience Assessment for free вҖ” our way of supporting you through the We Care About Coaches initiative. This episode is brought to you by theвҒ  Resilience Institute Certifications and hosted by вҒ Brad HookвҒ .  Order your copy of Brad's new book, вҒ Start With ValuesвҒ , now! The guest: Dr. Marcia Reynolds is a Master Certified Coach (MCC), former president of the ICF, and author of several bestsellers including Coach the Person, Not the Problem. Known for her groundbreaking work on reflective inquiry and emotional intelligence, she helps leaders and coaches ignite breakthroughs that stick. Key Takeaways Great coaching creates a safe space for people to rethink who they are and what they want. title: 1075. This Is Your Brain on ANXIETY: What No One Tells You About Mental Illness w/ Dr. Daniel Amen

title: Sabina Nawaz: Why Pressure, Not Power, Shapes Effective Leadership
summary: Sabina Nawaz is a powerhouse executive coach and leadership strategist who has helped leaders from Fortune 500 companies to innovative startups transform their mindsets and unlock their leadership potential. With over 25 years of experienceвҖ”including a pivotal management role at MicrosoftвҖ”Sabina blends emotional intelligence with practical tools to help leaders manage pressure, communicate authentically, and embrace continuous growth. As an author and sought-after speaker, her candid insights encourage leaders to move beyond comfort zones, embrace vulnerability, and create a positive, lasting impact on their teams and organizations.  В Takeaways:  Authenticity is Multifaceted: Sabina emphasizes that authenticity isnвҖҷt singularвҖ”leaders need to adapt to their circumstances and roles, using authenticity as a guide for growth rather than an excuse to avoid change.  Pressure and Power Dynamics Matter: She explains that itвҖҷs not just power, but pressure, that often corrupts behavior in leaders. Managing both is essential, and pressure can either define or refine leadership, depending on how itвҖҷs handled.  Communication and Vulnerability Win Trust: The most effective leaders are those who communicate clearly, align their words and body language, and arenвҖҷt afraid to express vulnerability. This builds stronger connections and lifelong loyalty among teams.  В Sound Bites:  вҖңPressure doesnвҖҷt have to define you. It can refine youвҖ”just like pressure builds diamonds.вҖқ  вҖңAuthenticity, when used as an excuse for not changing, actually prevents growth. We have multiple authentic selves, depending on our roles.вҖқ  вҖңIf youвҖҷre a leader, arrange your face before you walk into that meeting. Your resting face carries more weight than you think.вҖқ  Quote by Mick:  "I tell leaders, if thereвҖҷs one habit that you can have today, itвҖҷs presence. And presence for your team is going to mean something different for all the individuals that you lead or that you manage. And you got to understand how to be present for them." title: Turning Personal Grief Into a Global Mission for Happiness | Mo Gawdat | E103
summary: Mo Gawdat was thriving in his career as Chief Business Officer at Google X, enjoying wealth, success, and a loving family. However, his life took a dramatic turn after the tragic loss of his son, Ali. This loss made Mo realize that happiness isnвҖҷt defined by external factors but by how we perceive and navigate lifeвҖҷs challenges. Letting go of everything that once defined success, including his career, Mo reinvented himself as an advocate for happiness, dedicating his life to helping others live with less stress and more joy. In this episode, Mo joins Ilana to share how he transformed his life after tragedy and reveals his formula for finding happiness, managing stress, and creating lasting fulfillment. Mo Gawdat is an Egyptian entrepreneur, four-time bestselling author, and former Chief Business Officer at Google X. He founded the mission #OneBillionHappy, dedicated to helping others lead happier lives. In this episode, Ilana and Mo will discuss: (00:00) IntroductionВ  (02:07) Growing up in Egypt with a Passion for Tech (05:38) Coding and Landing a Job at IBM (11:02) Building Trust and Success in Sales (16:14) Struggling with Happiness Despite Success (21:35) Discovering the Algorithm of Happiness (27:02) The Devastating Loss of His Son (33:01) Turning Grief Into a Global Mission of Happiness (40:00) Leaving Google in Pursuit of True Fulfillment (46:47) Understanding and Managing Stress (01:00:24) Why Life is Like a Video Game Mo Gawdat is an Egyptian entrepreneur, four-time bestselling author, and former Chief Business Officer at Google X. With a background in engineering and over 30 years in the tech industry, he has held leadership roles at IBM, NCR, Microsoft, and Google, where he helped launch operations in over 50 countries. Mo developed a formula for happiness, which he shares in his book Solve for Happy, and founded the mission #OneBillionHappy, dedicated to helping others lead happier lives. title: What Anger Is Really Hiding (And Why ItвҖҷs So Dangerous)В
summary: What if the worst thing ever said to you wasnвҖҷt just a memoryвҖ”but a scar that shaped who you are? In this episode, IвҖҷm opening up about a tough truth that most of us are either living withвҖ”or have dished out ourselves: anger and emotional volatility. Whether you're on the receiving end of it or you're the one dishing it out, todayвҖҷs message is a wake-up call. IвҖҷm not just sharing conceptsвҖ”IвҖҷm pulling straight from my own story, from childhood scars to leadership missteps, and how I finally learned that the strongest people donвҖҷt intimidateвҖ”they protect. You deserve relationships, workplaces, and a life where you feel safe to be your full self. WeвҖҷre not tiptoeing around the issue. WeвҖҷre calling it out. If someone in your life makes you feel small, anxious, or afraid to speak freelyвҖ”youвҖҷve got to draw the line. Because youвҖҷre not just allowing itвҖҰyouвҖҷre teaching them itвҖҷs okay. And thatвҖҷs not love. ThatвҖҷs not leadership. ThatвҖҷs not life. IвҖҷm also giving you the toolsвҖ”real, practical habitsвҖ”that have helped me move from a reactive man to a regulated one. And if you're the one struggling with anger, here's your moment. IвҖҷm not judging youвҖ”I've been you. I know what it means to erupt, to regret, to hurt the people you love most. But you can flip the switch. You can become the kind of person your family, your team, and your future self can count on. No more band-aids over bullet wounds. ItвҖҷs time to make real change. Key Takeaways:   How to recognize emotional abuse disguised as вҖңpassionвҖқ or вҖңleadershipвҖқ   The difference between a strong man and a scared one hiding behind anger   Why the contrast of вҖңhigh highsвҖқ in toxic relationships can deceive you   Concrete steps to regulate anger before it destroys your identity and others   How your emotional patterns directly impact your kids and future generations   The one trait every safe, authentic, high-performing relationship has You were born to do something great. And that starts with living a life where you're safe to be you. It's not just about loveвҖ”it's about respect, belief, and emotional safety. LetвҖҷs stop normalizing anger, and start creating environments that heal. title: EP 64 Florence Belkacem : Cueillir les signes envoyГ©s par nos mort.e.s tant aimГ©.e.s.
summary: Et si les morts continuaient Г  nous parler ? Dans cet Г©pisode je reГ§ois la journaliste et autrice Florence Belkacem. Tout commence avec un domino. Un voisin le tend Г  Florence en lui disant : В« Il est Г  vous. Vous comprendrez plus tard. В» Plus tard, cвҖҷest la mort de son pГЁre.  Elle part en Kabylie, sur les traces de sa famille paternelle. LГ -bas, elle dГ©couvre que le domino est le jeu le plus populaire. Elle en est persuadГ©e : un fil invisible la relie Г  cette terre et Г  ceux qui lвҖҷont prГ©cГ©dГ©e. ГҖ la mort de sa mГЁre, ce sont des coccinelles qui viennent Г  elle. Dans les moments de chagrin ou de doute, elles apparaissent. Pour Florence, il nвҖҷy a pas de hasard : sa mГЁre continue de veiller sur elle, autrement. Elle dГ©couvre aussi quвҖҷelle porte le prГ©nom de son arriГЁre-grand-mГЁre, dont elle ignorait jusquвҖҷГ  lвҖҷexistence. Et au fil de ce deuil, cвҖҷest toute une mГ©moire transgГ©nГ©rationnelle qui se rГ©veille. Un Г©pisode sensible et lumineux, pour celles et ceux qui ont aimГ©, perdu, espГ©rГ© вҖ” et qui se demandent si, parfois, lвҖҷinvisible ne cherche pas simplement Г  nous susurrer : В« Je ne suis pas loin, je veille sur toi." title: Alexandra Garcia: Why Difficult Pursuits Matter вҖ” Resilience, Running & the Mountains

Kilimanjaro in 2017 and completing the Grand Canyon rim-to-rim in 2018, Alex has pursued adventures that push her limits вҖ” including backpacking rugged trails in Puerto Rico and continuously seeking what she calls вҖңdifficult pursuits.вҖқ  В   Now based in Western North Carolina near Pisgah National Forest, Alex is not only an outdoor athlete and writer but also a changemaker. Blanc, and sheвҖҷs also training for a Fastest Known Time (FKT) attempt on a challenging trail route.В   В   Whether sheвҖҷs writing, running, or leading with purpose, Alexandra is passionate about personal growth, emotional resilience, and helping others find the courage to take on their own version of hard. title: How to Stop Feeling Responsible for Everyone: A Guide for the Exhausted вҖҳStrong One
podcast_name: How To Love Yourself No Matter What
summary: Welcome to the How to Love Yourself No Matter What podcast with Amanda Hess. In this episode, Amanda dives deep into a topic so many of us struggle with: feeling responsible for everyone around us. If youвҖҷre the person everyone relies on, the peacekeeper holding it all together, or someone whoвҖҷs secretly exhausted but scared to stop, this episode is for you. Drawing from her personal experiences and work as a certified life coach, Amanda unpacks why so many women feel trapped by the belief that they must be the strong one, and how this thinking leads to burnout, resentment, and the loss of self. She explores both societal and personal conditioning that teaches women to prioritize everyone elseвҖҷs needs above their own and gets candid about the physical, emotional, and mental toll this takes. But Amanda doesnвҖҷt stop thereвҖ”she offers practical strategies for breaking this cycle, including the power of tiny acts of self-care, regulating your nervous system, emotional processing, and the radical act of putting yourself at the top of your own list. If youвҖҷre ready to set healthier boundaries, stop overfunctioning, and reclaim your sense of self, tune in and join Amanda as she shares the tools and mindset shifts you need to let go of what was never yours to carry. LetвҖҷs get started on the path to freedom, fulfillment, and self-loveвҖ”one small shift at a time. title: Hey, WaitвҖҰ Alcohol Is Keeping You Small: with Sarah Rusbatch | #3
summary: Welcome back to the вҖңHey, WaitвҖқ Podcast, hosted by embodiment coach, educator, and author Jay Moon Fields. Each episode weвҖҷll be sharing stories, insights, interviews, and practical tools to help you shift how you show up in your relationships, and in your life.  В   In todayвҖҷs episode, Jay Moon Fields is joined by Award Winning Author, Wellbeing Coach, and Keynote Speaker, Sarah Rusbatch. SarahвҖҷs journey toward sobriety has covered a large portion of her life, and today she works tirelessly to support women all over the globe to create and live their best lives as they age.  В   SarahвҖҷs вҖңHey, WaitвҖқ moment began by choosing a better life for herself, and she is generous enough to share her story with Jay throughout the course of the episode. They discuss at length вҖңGrey Area DrinkingвҖқ, what it is, and how it impacts the entire lives of those that it affects.  В   ItвҖҷs a canвҖҷt miss conversation for anyone willing to try something different in an effort to improve their life. If youвҖҷd like to connect with Sarah for coaching directly, visit her website and set up a meeting at: https://sarahrusbatch.com/  В   And Hey, Wait! Before you go, make sure to subscribe to the вҖңHey, WaitвҖқ Podcast so you can follow along with every new episode as soon as it is released. And be sure to connect with her across all social media platforms so you can submit your questions to the Podcast. You just might be featured on a future episode!      Key Takeaways:  В     We CanвҖҷt Metabolize Alcohol the Same Way in our 40s and 50s as we did in our 20s.    ItвҖҷs Not About Alcohol, ItвҖҷs About the Whole Picture of What Is and IsnвҖҷt Working for You.    Grey Area Drinking is the place between an вҖңAlcoholicвҖқ and вҖңTake-it-or-Leave-ItвҖқ Drinkers.    For Grey Area Drinkers itвҖҷs easier to drink None Than One.    Alcohol Use Disorder in Women Has Increased 80% in the last 30 Years.    Alcohol Keeps Us SmallвҖ“ And Keeps Us Trapped in a Life We DonвҖҷt Want.    The Goal IsnвҖҷt To Be Sober, the Goal Is to Love Yourself So Much You DonвҖҷt Want to Drink.    How Hard it Is at the StartвҖҰ Is Not How Hard It Will Be Forever.    Many Women Swap Alcohol for Sugar.        Your Homework:  В     Put Your Hand on Your Heart and Ask: вҖңHow Do I Feel, and What do I Need?вҖқ    Know that Your Insides MatterвҖҰ And the Right People Will Meet You There. title: When Losing the Path Is Finding the Way
summary: Lately IвҖҷve been feeling lostвҖҰ How about you? ItвҖҷs not surprising. The world is undergoing some truly unprecedented things, and itвҖҷs creating unprecedented changes in many of our lives. Navigating the unprecedented means we have to venture into uncharted territory. And that feels a whole lot like being lost.In this episode of The Gathering Room, IвҖҷm talking about feeling lost during times of personal and global upheaval, letting go of whatвҖҷs familiar, and stepping into the unknown to awaken to a new consciousness.If youвҖҷre familiar with my work, youвҖҷll know IвҖҷve long believed that we need to experience a radical change of consciousness in order to move forward as a species in love and harmony and care for the planet and each other. That journey of awakening will require us to go to places weвҖҷve never been before. And when youвҖҷre going into the unknown, the path itself must disappearвҖ”it must become a non-path or what is known in Zen as вҖңthe pathless path.вҖқ No one has ever traveled the path youвҖҷre meant to travel. Your way is unique. It lies beyond any maps. So as you move forward not knowing where you are going, there will come a time when youвҖҷll feel like youвҖҷve lost the path completely. (Maybe thatвҖҷs where you are right now.)You may find yourself feeling things like: Am I on the right path? Is there even a path? I donвҖҷt know where I am or who I am anymoreвҖ”am I the only one who feels this way?HereвҖҷs the thing: The moment that you feel lost might be the moment that your soul has finally slipped the leash of cultural conditioning. It may feel strange and alienating, but itвҖҷs a sign that youвҖҷre not off track, youвҖҷre unmappedвҖ”and you are free.At these times I follow a practice called вҖңNaming the Shape of the Unknown,вҖқ where you:Allow the unknown to simply be.Put words on what you donвҖҷt know.Sit quietly with this named not-knowing.You donвҖҷt have to solve a problem, you just have to make a little space where you can be aware of your own not-knowing and how it makes you feel.For example, if you feel stuck about your career, instead of running around working on your resume, pause and just see where the pathless path has taken you. You may find yourself feeling things like: I donвҖҷt know what I want, I donвҖҷt know whether the life I built still fits me, I donвҖҷt know whatвҖҷs coming next, and IвҖҷm terrified.All you have to do is sit with these feelings. Sit with the terror, sit with the pain. Trust that the river knows where itвҖҷs going, even if you donвҖҷt. If you can sit with those вҖңI donвҖҷt knowвҖқ phrases, it may be uncomfortable or painful, but you start to realize that it is also intimacy with the mystery.Your not-knowing can be the map to the place youвҖҷve never seen, to the person youвҖҷre becoming that youвҖҷve never been, to your new home.Tune in for the full episode to hear more about the practice of sitting with the unknown, how our living spaces mirror our inner lives, and how to use imagination and yearning to channel new possibilities. IвҖҷll also answer listener questions on how to remain on the fluid path while setting strong boundaries, how to comfort yourself through grief and uncertainty, and why transformation is a lifelong spiral. And IвҖҷll lead you through my Space, Silence, and Stillness meditation to help you reconnect with your inner compassesвҖ”the only guidance you ever need. Remember that The Gathering Room is not a room with walls. It is a clearing where lost ones find each other and remember they were never alone. So if youвҖҷre lost, tune in and be lost with us. WeвҖҷll find each other in a place none of us has ever known before.  CONNECT WITH US   Follow Martha on Instagram The Gathering Room Show Notes Join Martha for a Live Episode of The Gathering Room via Instagram See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: How to Stop Negative Thoughts & Reset Your Mind for Positive Thinking
summary: In todayвҖҷs episode, you are getting a step-by-step guide on how to change your mindset, stop negative thoughts, and make your mind work for you.В  What if the voice in your head вҖ” the one that criticizes, spirals, and second-guesses everything вҖ” could actually help you?В  In this powerful conversation, Mel sits down with one of the world's leading experts on the mind, Dr. Ethan Kross, PhD. He's a neuroscientist, award-winning psychologist, professor at the University of Michigan, director of The Emotion & Self Control Laboratory, and leading expert on the science of emotion, self-talk, and mental resilience.В  Today, he's here to teach you how to stop negative thoughts from running your life вҖ” using simple, science-backed tools.В  In this episode, Dr. Kross shares specific tactics, strategies, and insights you can use to break free from spiraling and reset your mind for positivity.В  If you're tired of overthinking, doubting yourself, or feeling stuck in your own headвҖҰ this oneвҖҷs for you.В  Class is in session, because after you listen to this, your brain will not be the same.В  For more resources, click here for the podcast episode page.В  If you liked this episode, youвҖҷll love listening to this one next: 6 Simple Science-Backed Hacks That Will Make Your Life Better Connect with Mel:В  В  Get MelвҖҷs #1 bestselling book, The Let Them TheoryWatch the episodes on YouTubeFollow Mel on InstagramВ The Mel Robbins Podcast InstagramMel's TikTokВ Sign up for MelвҖҷs personal letterВ Subscribe to SiriusXM Podcasts+ to listen to new episodes ad-freeDisclaimer

title: Sex Appeal: What If Owning Your Truth Is the Real Turn-On? | Emily Paulsen & Lauren White
podcast_name: Curious Life of a Childfree Woman
summary: What do we really mean when we talk about вҖңsex appealвҖқ? And what if it has very little to do with how you lookвҖ”and everything to do with how you feel about yourself? In this episode of Curious Life of a Childfree Woman, Emily Paulsen sits down with Lauren White, a business sexologist and certified sexological bodyworker who helps women access deeper levels of pleasure, confidence, and magnetism in every part of life. Together, they explore how erotic energy influences how you lead, connect, and move through the worldвҖ”not just how you show up in the bedroom. YouвҖҷll hear Lauren break down what it really means to be magnetic, why repelling the wrong people is essential to attracting the right ones, and how power and pleasure are deeply connected. This conversation challenges tired narratives about sexuality and instead invites you to embrace the parts of yourself that make you feel most alive. If youвҖҷve ever felt unsure of your presence, stuck in self-doubt, or curious about how to feel more turned on by your life, this episode offers a grounded and liberating perspective. Key Takeaways: Magnetism starts from within. True sex appeal isnвҖҷt about external validationвҖ”itвҖҷs about turning ourselves on first. The willingness to repel is essential. Being magnetic requires a bold willingness to be seen clearly, even if it means turning some people off. Sexual energy is life energy. When weвҖҷre disconnected from our erotic selves, it impacts our ability to be present, powerful, and fulfilled in all areas of life. Fulfillment flows through the web of our lives. Every threadвҖ”professional, personal, sensualвҖ”is connected and reinforcing. Turn-on is a compass. If something doesnвҖҷt light you up, ask yourself: Why am I tolerating this?В  вҖ” Host Emily Paulsen is an accomplished entrepreneur and happily childfree woman shining a light on the often overshadowed childfree-by-choice perspective. Whether interviewing innovative experts or positioning leaders to scale through her Brand Studio, Electric Collab, EmilyвҖҷs power lies in allowing people to feel seen and celebrated for who they are . SheвҖҷs spent years honing the ability to deeply understand and amplify others in an honest, high-impact way.В  Learn more about Emily on her website: www.curiouslifeofachildfreewoman.comВ В  And connect with her on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/curiouslifeofachildfreewoman/В  вҖ” The guest on this episode, Lauren White, is The Business Sexologist - an award winning sexologist, certified sexological bodyworker and business mentor for women who want to lock in their most powerful and potent frequency to get the next-level love, the money and pleasure. She is the host of White Hot Business Podcast, an international speaker and the author of Permission: Personal liberation for switched on women.В  Connect with Lauren on Instagram:www.instagram.com/laurenwhiteau And book a session her website:В  www.laurenwhite.com.au/session

title: Kelsey Grammer Reveals All: Lessons on Love, Loss, Healing & Remembering   (Pt 1)
summary: Full Video Available on YouTube вҒ @JamieKernLimaOfficialвҒ . Are You Ready to believe in YOU?рҹҷҢвҒ  jamiekernlima.comвҒ  рҹ‘Ҳ Sign up for my FREE Inspirational Newsletter here and youвҖҷll ALSO get special prompt questions to help you grow in your self-worth-building that pair with each episode!рҹ©·В  Make sure to click the вҖңFollowвҖқ button for the show on your favorite podcast app, so youвҖҷll be the first to get each episode!В  Get your tissues out, and get ready to remember the experiences, feelings and people who matter most to you in your life! This is a side of Kelsey Grammer youвҖҷve never seen before, heвҖҷs truly heart-wide-open in this soulful, vulnerable, emotional interview!  В How do you remember the people in your life who you loved, and have lost? And have you considered how that impacts your life, your healing, your spirit and your joy today? Today weвҖҷre talking about love, loss, healing,В  rememberingвҖҰ and celebrating a joy-filled lifeвҖҰwith my guest today, Kelsey Grammer. В Kelsey is a Golden Globe Award winning, Emmy Award winning, Tony Award winning, Screen Actors Guild Award winning, PeopleвҖҷs Choice Award winning actor, comedian and producer. He first gained fame for his role as Dr. Frasier Crane on this hit TV show Cheers, and later itвҖҷs spin-off Frasier, making primetime television history playing one of the longest-running roles, for more than 20 years, by a single actor. He is the founder of the Faith American Brewing Company, a husband to his wife Kayte, and the father of 7 children! It was recently announced heвҖҷll be playing BEAST in the much anticipated AvengerвҖҷs Doomsday, and today, weвҖҷre actually seeing a deeply personal and intimate part of Kelsey, in perhaps one of the most important and meaningful works of his life, KelseyвҖҷs brand new book: where he explores love, loss, healing and celebrating the memory of a life filled with joy. In Karen: A Brother Remembers, Kelsey shares the tragic story of the death of his sister, Karen, who was brutally murdered at the age of eighteen, and the journey of his own path to healing in his life. In Karen: A Brother Remembers, Kelsey aims to help others who have experienced similar loss, offering solace and encouragement to cherish the love they knew, however brief, on their own path toward healing. title: Episode 322: Put Your Past In Your Past with Beverly Engel
summary: Beverly Engel is back, and IвҖҷm so grateful to talk with someone whoвҖҷs spent decades helping people break free from trauma-driven relationship patterns. If youвҖҷve ever felt stuck in the same story - same red flags, different partner - youвҖҷre not alone. In this episode, Beverly brings clarity to the messy, courageous work of breaking free and putting your past in your past.  Beverly is a renowned psychotherapist and author of 27 self-help books, including her latest, Put Your Past In Your Past. In this conversation, we get into how hard it is to break lifelong patterns, especially when you were never taught how to feel your feelings, let alone trust them. But thatвҖҷs where the work is and thatвҖҷs how we begin to change everything. Healing is messy. ItвҖҷs uncomfortable. But itвҖҷs also liberating as hell.  HereвҖҷs what else we discuss in this episode:   What trauma reenactment actually is and how to begin interrupting the cycle (7:56) How to create a вҖңtriggers listвҖқ and begin connecting the dots between past pain and present-day reactions (10:51) Why you canвҖҷt heal what you donвҖҷt feel and what to do when the emotions finally surface (18:44) The fear of naming your trauma in relationships (and how to work through it) (40:08) Tools for building self-compassion and protecting yourself moving forward (43:40)  Learn more about Beverly Engel: Beverly is an internationally recognized psychotherapist and expert on trauma. She has written 27 books on the related subjects of abuse, shame, and empowerment, including The Emotionally Abusive Relationship and Healing Your Emotional Self. Her books have been translated into many languages, including Spanish, Japanese, Chinese, Korean, Greek, Turkish, and Polish. She has a blog on Psychology Today titled The Compassion Chronicles and her writing has been featured in Oprah Magazine, Cosmopolitan, The Chicago Tribune and The Washington Post, to name a few. title: Allergy Season Survival Guide: Natural Remedies & Daily Habits That Actually Help
summary: If thereвҖҷs one thing a lot of people are battling right now, itвҖҷs seasonal allergies вҖ” and with the sniffles, the sneezes, and the brain fog, itвҖҷs more than just annoying. WeвҖҷre Covering: WhatвҖҷs actually happening in your body when allergies strikeThe difference between allergy symptoms and a coldWhy staying hydrated helps control histamine (your allergy culprit)The surprising herbal tea with antihistamine benefitsThe plant pigment that can naturally reduce inflammationSpirulina: gross but effective (and how to make it bearable)Homeopathic optionsThe one thing to do before bed to reduce nighttime symptomsHow bursts of movement may actually help clear out congestionAnd the underrated power of kindness as medicineResources Mentioned: Sabadilla (homeopathic remedy for sneezing)RhinAllergy by Boiron (combination homeopathic formula)Stinging Nettle Tea (herbal support with antihistamine properties)Quercetin-rich foods: red onion, broccoli, raspberries, apples, parsleyVitamin C (bonus support during allergy season)Spirulina (immune-modulating blue-green algae)And if this episode helped clear your head вҖ” or just made you laugh through your congestion вҖ” feel free to send it to a friend, leave a kind review, or shout us out. From moving to California in search of growth to facing homelessness, abusive relationships, and the weight of impostor syndrome, Peter shares how this experience became the catalyst for deep healing, mindset shifts, and building a life rooted in purpose. рҹ’Ҙ In this conversation, we explore the difference between simply taking action versus taking inspired action, how trusting your intuition can change everything, and why surrounding yourself with the right people is essential for growth. title: always winning | worthiness with Ethan Poetic 5/6
summary: рҹҺҷпёҸ Today on the Always Winning Podcast, I'm joined by Ethan Poetic to talk about one of my favorite letters in W.I.N.N.I.N.G.!  W for Worthiness вқӨпёҸ EthanвҖҷs story is deeply powerful and inspiring. From growing up in a disadvantaged household to becoming a two-time college graduate, published author, keynote speaker, and mental health advocate, Ethan shares how he rewrote the narrative that was written for him. We dive into what it means to know your worth from the inside out, how to help others recognize their value, and why it's so important to set boundaries, pour into others, and stay intentional about your time and energy. вңЁ He also shares the systems and daily habits that keep him grounded and how he turned adversity into purpose by showing up in his community and helping others do the same. Whether you're struggling to believe in yourself or ready to step fully into your power, this episode will light a fire in you. рҹ’Ҙ рҹ“ҚConnect with Ethan here: ethanspeaks.comрҹ“І IG + TikTok: @EthanPoetic23

title: 5 Free, Fast and Easy Ways to Manifest Effectively: Turning Short Daily Tasks into Moments for Manifestation
podcast_name: All Up In Your Biz with Yvonne Tchrakian
summary: In this solo episode, Yvonne answers some of the many questions posed by the members of her Manifestie Bestie Club and community. Answering those questions has inspired Yvonne to share some simple yet powerful manifestation tips and insights you wonВҙt have heard elsewhere. Including how to use the little things in life to get your vibe high and keep it there for most of each day and how to continue to effectively manifest when you are going through emotionally difficult times. As well as how to turn the time you have to spend on daily tasks into manifestation boosting moments.  YvonneВҙs had lots of questions and feedback from her Manifestie Bestie community, too many questions to answer in just one episode, so keep an eye out for part 2.  KEY TAKEAWAYS   You canВҙt be high vibe all of the time, everyone has bad days or periods in their life. But you do need to have the tools and develop the skills to lift yourself out of a low vibe cycle. Use some of the little things that please you or bring you joy to generate or sustain a high vibe.В  Having high vibes does not necessarily mean being high energy, colourful or lively, it can be feeling content in yourself and happy with your life or certain aspects of your life. You can pepper your life with subtle reminders and signs that lift your vibe and prompt you to manifest e.g. An affirmation on your coffee cup. Yvonne shares several more practical examples of how to do this. Pausing to notice and enjoy something helps keep your vibe high. Always be manifesting, many of the methods Yvonne shares take seconds to do. Consistency is key. Incorporate manifestation into the tasks you do every day e.g. Use affirmations while brushing your teeth. Listen to high vibe songs. Learn to visualise yourself to sleep. Stop making excusesвҖҰ.anyone can do it.  BEST MOMENTS  вҖңMy high vibes are different to your high vibes.вҖқ  вҖңItВҙs all about your energy and what you are putting outвҖҰthe universe is matching your vibrational frequency.вҖқ  вҖңIf you are manifesting abundance, you need to treat finding 2 euros like finding 200 euros.вҖқ  вҖңYouВҙve got to learn how to snap out of it.вҖқ  вҖңYou are always manifestingвҖҰ manifestation is all about your thoughts, your feelings, beliefs and how you react.вҖқ  вҖңStart becoming aware of your surroundings and seeing them in the positive.вҖқ  вҖңAll of that is free, itвҖҷs stuff you are already doing, so no excuses.вҖқ  вҖңManifestation is all youвҖҰyou either want to manifest it or you donВҙt.вҖқ  EPISODE RESOURCES  ManifestieBestie Club reel - https://www.instagram.com/yvonnetchrakian_manifest/reel/DFQIVOQsf8y  VALUABLE RESOURCES  website: pausepenny.com  Instagram: @yvonnetchrakian_manifest @pause.penny  LinkedIn:В https://www.linkedin.com/in/yvonne-tchrakian-64257619/  HOST BIO  Yvonne started her career as a corporate lawyer for one of Ireland's top law firms. After the loss of a baby during pregnancy, Yvonne founded Pause. Penny - which was created to inspire people to manifest their dream lives. She is also a mom of four. Yvonne is a serial manifester, manifesting everything from her dream jobs (yes, multiple), her soulmate, dream house and even her twins!

title: #138 How Can4Cancer Raised $20M in 10yrs: Inspiring a World-Class Wellbeing Program Connected to Curing Cancer
summary: Listen to an inspiring story that begins not in a boardroom, but in the most unexpected of places: a fruit and vegetable aisle. We delve into the incredible journey of Can4Cancer, a Commbank employee-driven initiative that has raised a staggering $20 million for vital cancer research! Commonwealth Bank of Australia (CommBank) is one of the largest banks in Australia with over 48,000 employees. We hear from a range of people making this dream come to life from the CEO of Commbank Matt Comyn, to the researchers benefiting from the cause and where the money is being used. We'll journey back to that very first Can4Cancer event, a remarkable feat involving over 140 riders, showcasing the dedication and hard work that laid the foundation for this extraordinary success. "...As scientists doing what we're doing, seeing there's a whole community there supporting us really helps us to get out of bed in the morning and go "this is why!"...we have a responsibility to all these people who have put their lives on hold a little bit to do the training, to get the money, and and that's what we're then the way then using for our research..."- Jeff Holst, Cancer Researcher and recipient of Can4Cancer funding. This isn't just a story about fundraising; it's a testament to the power of human connection and collective action. You'll hear about:In this Episode Andrew and his guests talk about: 2:35 How a chance meeting between Andrew and Bruno Maurel led to the creation of Can4Cancer. 4:40 ThereвҖҷs now so much more to Can4Cancer than just a bike ride. 5:35 Matt ComynвҖҷs reflections on Can4Cancer and how it has grown over the years. 12:35 Clive Van HorenвҖҷs thoughts on the amazing growth of Can4Cancer and the inclusion of multiple events. 16:30 The part played by researcher Professor Jeff Holst in finding a cure for cancer. 19:30 That vital part that the money Can4Cancer raises plays in securing more funding. 24:05 Rose SpiteriвҖҷs inspiring story of participating in Can4Cancer walks. 28:00 How Rose manages to walk 21km despite being in intense pain. 31:30 How John McClelland helps CBA and Tour De Cure run one of the world's largest charity events and why John is so passionate about the cause. 34:20 How John sees Can4Cancer evolving with the community and some of the events he is hoping to add in the future. 38:35 Why Sallie Rodd says she has the best job at Commbank, looking after Can4Cancer. 41:00 why Can4Cancer has the highest engagement of any internal program and the goal to get 1 in 5 CBA employees participating. 43:05 What the relationship between CBA and Tour De Cure means to Bruno Maurel and why Tour De CureвҖҷs work isnвҖҷt done just yet. 48:10 Brunos mission to cure all cancers by 2050. title: Here's My Story on How I Learned to Help Others Heal Their Trauma Symptoms
podcast_name: The Crappy Childhood Fairy Podcast with Anna Runkle
summary: I make it VERY clear that I'm not a therapist or doctor, but sometimes viewers ask why I didn't go the route of becoming a therapist, and how I learned to do what I do. In this video, I give the full story on how I recovered from my own Complex PTSD symptoms, and how teaching my method grew from a few people to millions of people over the course of 30 years. My focus is not on childhood or the origins of trauma (this is the special expertise of licensed clinicians). Rather, I focus on practical ways to regulate the nervous system, and principles and techniques to get your life together and NOT fall into the old self-defeating behaviors that used to drag you down.В В  Need Online Therapy? https://betterhelp.com/CCF Try the FREE Daily Practice Course: FREE PDF Download: https://bit.ly/3Y4263I  рҹҹў Order My New Book, RE-REGULATED: https://bit.ly/4dtGVze  Take my FREE Course or Quizzes: https://bit.ly/40M2YeW  Enroll in my Courses or Membership: https://bit.ly/3HP5qcf  Send your questions & comments to: hello@crappychildhoodfairy.com  NEED ONLINE THERAPY? *BetterHelp* can connect you with a licensed, online therapist: https://betterhelp.com/CCF

title: #156- 7 Keys to Success | David FradinвҖҷs Formula for Product and Life Wins
podcast_name: Peak Performance Perspectives with Mark Talukdar
summary: In this amazing episode of the Peak Performance Perspectives Podcast, host Mark Talukdar talks with David Fradin, a product management expert with over 50 years of experience! David has worked with big companies like Apple and HP. He has helped people become great leaders and build products that really work. He knows how to lead teams, build trust, and make smart decisions. Mark and David talk about what makes a great product and a great team. David shares simple ways to know what customers really want. He says you need to listen, learn, and ask the right questions. ThatвҖҷs how you make a product people love. He also talks about being a leader. A good leader builds trust, shares the big picture, and gives people what they need to do their best. David shares his "7 Keys to Success" and explains how they help in life and business. These keys are things like having a clear mission, good plans, strong support, and the right tools. He says when these 7 keys are in place, teams work better and people feel more confident. ItвҖҷs like building a strong bridge you need all the parts to work together. They also talk about product management what it is and why it matters. David says product managers are like the glue that holds things together. They help teams talk to each other and make sure everyone is working toward the same goal. He shares fun stories and tips for being a good product manager. This episode is full of smart lessons made simple. YouвҖҷll learn how to: вң… Understand what people really wantвң… Build trust in your teamвң… Lead with kindness and purposeвң… Use tools that make work easierвң… Create products people loveвң… Avoid common mistakes that waste time and money David also talks about the future. He shares how AI, data, and technology are changing the way we build and sell products. He says being curious, kind, and clear will help you stay ahead. Bio: David Fradin was classically trained as an HP Product Manager and was then recruited by Apple to bring the first hard disk drive on a PC to market and later became the Apple /// Business Unit Manager at the same level as Steve Jobs. He is the author of "Building Insanely Great Products," "Organizing and Managing Insanely Great Products," and the Wiley published "Successful Product Design and Management" all available now on Amazon. He has trained companies such as Cisco on these topics worldwide. His mission is to help products succeed. Connect with him: www.spicecatalyst.com Get book at: Building Insanely Great Products Links to all my books are at www.davidfradin.com

title: Food Addiction with Sandra Elia - Spring 2025
summary: In this guest expert episode, Gina Livy sits down with Sandra Elia to unpack the deeper truths behind food addiction and emotional eating. From the moment you reach for food, Gina challenges listeners to ask, вҖңWhatвҖҷs my intention?вҖқвҖ”because real change starts with awareness. Sandra shares how food can shift from fuel to emotional survival, offering comfort, connection, and control, especially in the hardest moments. They discuss how ultra-processed foods hijack hunger cues and keep you chasing satisfaction that never comes. With raw honesty, Sandra opens up about how recovery isnвҖҷt a one-time fixвҖ”itвҖҷs a daily practice built on structure, self-awareness, and resilience. They explore how recognizing early relapse patterns can prevent spirals, and how love, not shame, is the real catalyst for sustainable change. If you've ever felt like you're вҖңfailing,вҖқ this episode will remind you: you're not failing, you're learning. You can find the full video hosted at: https://www.facebook.com/groups/livymethodspring2025 Sandra is the founder of The Food Addiction Recovery Program and the author of "Never Enough: Three Pillars of Food Addiction Recovery". title: From Bottling It Up to Speaking with Clarity - The Cost of Staying Silent - Part 3

Whether your reactions come out as silence, sarcasm, people-pleasing, or late-night overthinking, itвҖҷs time to change the script.  This is the final part of our three-part series, The Cost of Staying Silent, and weвҖҷre going deep on how to shift from emotional suppression to self-expression, without blowing up, shutting down, or second-guessing everything you say.  If youвҖҷve ever asked yourself, вҖңWhy canвҖҷt I just say what I mean?вҖқ, this episode gives you a simple method that can shift everything.  In this episode, youвҖҷll learn:   Why bottling up your emotions keeps you stuck in resentment and self-doubt    How quiet reactions (like avoidance and people-pleasing) drain your energy and damage your relationships    The TriggerвҖ“PauseвҖ“Respond method: a simple, powerful tool for emotionally intelligent communication    Real coaching stories that show how small shifts lead to big breakthroughs    How to express what you actually need, even if youвҖҷve been silent for years    The reflection question that will unlock your emotional clarity    If youвҖҷre ready to stop saying вҖңIвҖҷm fineвҖқ when youвҖҷre not, and start speaking up without fear or guilt, this episode will meet you right where you are, with honesty, tools, and a gentle nudge toward more peace and power in your everyday life. summary: ItвҖҷs time to rewire for wellnessвҖ”your brain holds the blueprint for healing, and Dr. Steven Resnick is here to show you how! Dr. Resnick has co-authored a textbook entitled Practical Neuroimaging in Stroke and has published articles in the Journal of the Peripheral Nervous System, the Journal of Neurology, and the Journal of Neurology, Neurosurgery, and Psychiatry. He has lectured extensively on stroke prevention, acute ischemic stroke, practical Neuroimaging in cerebrovascular disease, and other related topics. Clinical research includes studies of drug therapies to treat Neuromuscular diseases.
