title: When Complex Trauma Looks Like a Personality Disorder: Dr. Kirk Honda on What Most Therapists Miss
summary: What if your "personality disorder" was actually trauma? The truth is, most therapists still donвҖҷt know how to spot complex PTSD вҖ” and way too many people get mislabeled in the process. In this episode, Andrea has compiled the best moments from her past conversations with Dr. Kirk Honda вҖ” licensed therapist and host of Psychology in Seattle. title: 430: From Overthinking to Embodied: The Feminine Healing You DidnвҖҷt Know You Needed
summary: Feminine embodiment, somatic healing, and nervous system regulation decoded. В  This is the conversation your healing has been craving. рҹ’Ӣ Monica is pulling back the velvet curtain and invites you inside the most potent portal of transformation: her high-touch 3-Day Immersion. This isnвҖҷt a rehearsed sales pitch вҖ” itвҖҷs a roundtable of real women who lived it. From sacred rage to sobbing releases, from nervous system rebirths to "holy sh*t, I'm a new woman" вҖ” this is what happens when safety, sisterhood, and somatic healing collide. В  Whether youвҖҷve done all of the group programs and 1:1 work, are swimming in self-help, or you're secretly wondering, вҖңDo I really need something in-person?вҖқ вҖ” their stories are the permission slip your body has been waiting for. В  INSIDE THE EPISODE: В  рҹҚҜ What it actually feels like to be seen, held, and cracked openВ  рҹҚҜ Why in-person work catapults your healing beyond anything youвҖҷll get on ZoomВ  рҹҚҜ The sacred power of rage вҖ” and how yelling became a feminine healing toolВ  рҹҚҜ The money fears they had before jumping in вҖ” and why they'd do it 10x overВ  рҹҚҜ The trauma-informed magic behind вҖңbeing caughtвҖқ when you let goВ  рҹҚҜ How their relationships, careers, and confidence completely shifted after the ImmersionВ  рҹҚҜ Why this isnвҖҷt a retreat вҖ” itвҖҷs a full activation and rewiringВ  рҹҚҜ Shattering the guilt around spending on yourself вҖ” and reclaiming self-worth through investment рҹҚҜ Masculine/feminine polarity in real time: how softening actually protects your feminineВ  рҹҚҜ The power of being witnessed in your grief, rage, and radiance рҹҚҜ What integration and continuous growth post-Immersion looks like вҖ” why these results LAST As Mentioned In The Episode вҖ”В  В  вҮў Tickets are now LIVE for The Immersion 2025: (Get $500 off through May 15th, 2025): https://monicayateshealth.com/pages/immersion?utm_source=podcast&utm_medium=podcast&utm_campaign=atlanta-2025 вҮў NEW!!! title: A Re-Education of the Gut-Brain Connection
summary: Our guest today is the brilliant Uma Naidoo, MD, a Harvard board-certified psychiatrist, professional chef, author, and nutrition specialist. Dr. Uma's book is called This is Your Brain on Food. It's a full guide to the surprising foods that fight things like depression, PTSD, ADHD, anxiety, OCD, and more. We go super cutting edge in this one as we talk about the undeniable relationship between our mental health and diet/nutrition. Morning Microdose is a podcast curated by Krista Williams and Lindsey Simcik, the hosts and founders of Almost 30, a global community, brand, and top rated podcast. With curated clips from the Almost 30 podcast, Morning Mircodose will set the tone for your day, so you can feel inspired through thought provoking conversationsвҖҰall in digestible episodes that are less than 10 minutes. Wake up with Krista and Lindsey, both literally and spiritually, Monday-Friday. If you enjoyed this conversation, listen to the full episode on Spotify here and on Apple here.

title: How To Handle Relapse Without Losing Hope
podcast_name: Inside The Mind Of An Addict
summary: Relapse can feel like a major setback, but it doesnвҖҷt have to define your recovery journey. In this episode, we take an honest, compassionate look at relapse: why it happens, what it really means, and how to move forward without shame or defeat. Whether you're supporting someone in recovery or facing your own challenges, you'll hear real-life examples, mindset shifts, and practical strategies to help turn a relapse into a turning point, not a dead end. Recovery isnвҖҷt a straight line, and you donвҖҷt have to walk it alone. Learn more about ongoing recovery support at: FamilyRecoveryAcademy.online

summary: Are your everyday frustrations quietly revealing the deeper truths youвҖҷve been avoiding?  WeвҖҷve all had that momentвҖ”waiting endlessly in line, overwhelmed, angry, and ready to explode. But what if that experience isnвҖҷt just annoying? What if itвҖҷs the key to uncovering buried stress and unresolved stories that are quietly running your life?  In this raw and revealing episode ofВ Stress Management for Highly Sensitive People, youвҖҷll discover:    How frustration and impatience are powerful signals pointing you to your next breakthrough.    A surprising way to shift your perspective by questioning what you believe about othersвҖ”especially when youвҖҷre convinced theyвҖҷre wrong. Press play now to turn a stressful moment into a portal for deep clarity, compassion, and unexpected inner freedom.  Todd  Dreaming of a stress-free, balanced life? Are you a highly sensitive person (HSP) or someone who identifies as hypersensitive or neurodivergent? Whether youвҖҷre interested in learning how to lower stress, handle stress and pressure, or reduce stress through practical techniques, we provide insights and support based on The Work of Byron Katie. title: The Woman Who Raised Me - My Mom's Incredible Mindset with Julie Wilson
podcast_name: Achieve Your Goals with Hal Elrod
summary: This episode is incredibly long overdue but the stars aligned, and I had the chance to interview someone who has been instrumental in helping me become the man I am today. That person is none other than my mom, Julie Wilson. I canвҖҷt wait for you to hear her story and mine from her perspective.  YouвҖҷve heard me talk about many of the difficult chapters in my lifeвҖ”losing my sister, surviving a near-fatal car crash, battling cancerвҖ”but today, youвҖҷll hear those stories through the eyes of the woman who lived them right alongside me. And when I say literally, I mean literally beside me.  My mom has been through more than most: the heartbreaking loss of a daughter (my sister), raising a rebellious teen (yep, that was me), watching me fight for my life multiple times, and most recently, surviving a serious fall that shattered her heel and broke her wrist. And yet, she remains one of the most joyful, optimistic, and resilient people I know. Her strength doesnвҖҷt come from avoiding hardshipвҖ”it comes from choosing laughter, faith, and service in the face of it.  Whether youвҖҷre a parent, a child, or someone simply navigating a tough season with lifeвҖҷs curveballs, this episode is packed with love, wisdom, and great perspective. YouвҖҷll learn how the positive mindset that I have today started when I was very young, why my family chooses to focus on the good things and not on the things that are out of our control, and how my mom lives with unshakable joy, even when life gets tough.  KEY TAKEAWAYS   How the words вҖңCanвҖҷt Change ItвҖқ is more than a phraseвҖ”itвҖҷs our way of life How my mom turned the loss of her daughter into a mission of healing for others Why giving tough love is necessary sometimes, especially when raising teenagers The positive mindset my mom had after a devastating fall from a ladder Her simple advice on true happiness and feeling young at heart That itвҖҷs better to give than to receive and why my mom is compelled to help others  Get The Full Show Notes  To get full access to today's show notes, including audio, transcript, and links to all the resources mentioned, visitВ MiracleMorning.com/585  Subscribe, Rate & Review  I would love if you could subscribe to the podcast and leave an honest rating & review. This will encourage other people to listen and allow us to grow as a community. The bigger we get as a community, the bigger the impact we can have on the world. title: Masonry and Misunderstanding: The Master's Carpet Controversy
summary: We examine Edmund Ronayne's anti-Masonic book "The Master's Carpet," exploring how a dispute over Grand Lodge fund allocation after the 1871 Chicago Fire led to one of history's most influential attacks on Freemasonry. вҖў Born in Ireland in 1832, Ronayne moved through multiple religious organizations before joining Freemasonry вҖў Rose to Senior Warden and eventually Worshipful Master of Keystone Lodge No. 639 in Chicago вҖў Became disillusioned when he felt the Grand Lodge misused relief funds after the Chicago Fire вҖў Rather than working within the system, published a 400-page book revealing and distorting Masonic ritual вҖў His arguments about Baal worship and distorted interpretations of Masonic symbols still appear in modern anti-Masonic rhetoric вҖў Most anti-Masonic arguments trace back to three main sources: Templar confessions, Leo Taxil hoax, and Ronayne's work вҖў Understanding opponents' arguments helps Masons better respond to misconceptions вҖў Critical thinking is essential when examining both pro and anti-Masonic literature Join us at the upcoming Grand Lodge Communication where On the Level podcast will be at the Three Ruffian Cigars Freedom Party on Sunday night at the local Shrine from 6-9pm. #podcast #freemasonry #bluelodge #scottishrite #shriner #masonicpodcast Support the show

title: #262: The Four "Its" of Problem Solving
podcast_name: The Simplicity Parenting Podcast with Kim John Payne
summary: Kim shares a simple yet powerful approach for when things are heading in a difficult direction with children. The process includes four steps: calm it down, work it out, put it right, and move it on. He emphasizes that meaningful resolution only begins once emotions have settled, so calming the situation is the essential first step. This might involve quiet presence, gentle touch, or a calming story. Once calm has returned, parent and child can begin to reflect, understand what happened, make amends, and move forward together. These steps help children feel emotionally supported and teach valuable tools for conflict resolution and resilience. вқӨпёҸВ Support the Podcast рҹ“‘В Simplicity Parenting Starter Kit рҹ“ІВ Request a Consultation with Kim John Payne рҹ“ҡВ Simplicity Parenting Book Store

title: How to Love Someone with Mental Illness with Dr. Michelle D. Sherman
summary: Have you ever struggled to support a close friend or family member dealing with a mental health condition? How do you know when you're truly helping rather than enabling? When should you push, simply show up, or even step away? If these questions resonate with you, youвҖҷre not aloneвҖ”at least 20% and possibly up to 40% of people will face this reality at some point in their lives. On this weekвҖҷs podcast, youвҖҷll meet a psychologist who shares her research and clinical experiences.  Listen and learn:   How to differentiate between supporting and enabling a loved one Key questions to ask yourself if you're in a caregiving role The impact of online support groups and AI on mental health care Strategies for handling difficult or unpleasant caregiving situations How to find affordable mental health resources locally and nationally Common challenges of caring for aging family members and how to cope  Links  www.SeedsofHopeBooks.com  ABOUT OUR GUEST  Dr. Michelle D. Sherman is a licensed clinical psychologist with over 25 years of experience, specializing in individual and couples therapy. She was a professor in the Department of Family Medicine and Community Health at the University of Minnesota Medical School and is a board-certified expert in couples and family psychology. Her work focuses on supporting families dealing with mental illness, PTSD, and trauma. She is the author of Loving Someone with a Mental Illness or History of Trauma: Skills, Hope, and Strength for Your Journey.  Like the Show?   Leave us a review Check out ourВ YouTube channel

title: Mind Over Pain: how I used mindful self-relaxation to eliminate back/hip pain I had for a year
summary: Have you ever wondered if your mind could actually heal your body? В  Not through some mystical force, but through simple, deliberate attention? В  Pain had become my unwelcome companion. В  Despite no obvious injury, a nagging pain in my hip forced me to move differently, think differently, and ultimately feel different. В  And through a very simple mindfulness practice I got rid of it. В  The experience revealed a profound truth about our bodies: we're integrated ecosystems where everything connects. В  As someone who battled frequent illness in younger years, I've witnessed firsthand how nervous system regulation dramatically improves immune function, energy levels, and even decision-making ability. When your nervous system remains in a state of tension, recovery processes never fully activate, cellular energy is wasted, and you're primed for short-term thinking rather than choices that benefit your long-term wellbeing.В   Learning to control your nervous system might be the most important skill you can develop, with benefits ranging from pain elimination to enhanced immunity to better life choices.В   This simple practice we talk about on this episode could transform your health and wellbeing more powerfully than any supplement or trendy wellness protocol. What might change in your life if you learned to truly listen to what your body is telling you? В  Simple body scan protocol - on YouTube В  Text Me Your Thoughts and Ideas Support the show  Brought to you by Angela Shurina  EXECUTIVE & OPTIMAL PERFORMANCE COACH

title: Artificial (Lack Of) Intelligence... and Your Soul
summary: Now there's a title! This episode covers a multitude but, in essence, goes straight to the heart of why we, as humans, are losing our humanity or, as one scientist puts it, "losing our soul". This hasn't got an awful lot to do with Artificial Intelligence.  Instead, it's got to do the "Knowledge" or the accumulation of what passes for knowledge. In essence, it's all about how thinking divorces from reality, how thinking distances is from our true nature, how accumulated thought - including the 70k thoughts that are swilling around in your head every day - has separated from our "perfect virtue". On the one hand, truth is, this is a process that's been going on for over 7,000 years. On the other hand, this is a process that you can not just stop in its tracks but reverse in a couple of weeks, during which you'll have taken your very own evolutionary quantum leap. Listen and be...

title: 440: Elegant Success: What No One Talks About
summary: Unshakeable: Redefining Success on Your Own Terms  In the pursuit of success, many individuals find themselves chasing external markersвҖ”such as wealth, recognition, or statusвҖ”believing that these achievements will bring them happiness and fulfillment. However, as discussed in the podcast episode, true success, or "elegant success," is fundamentally about creating a life that aligns with your personal values and brings genuine satisfaction.  Host Tonya Leigh explores the concept of elegant successвҖ”success that is rooted in personal values, alignment, and ease, rather than societal pressures to do more and be more. Tonya shares her reflections on the importance of creating a fulfilling life that feels good on the inside, as well as the challenges many are facing in today's uncertain world. Drawing from her personal experiences, including navigating her own emotional landscape during difficult times, she emphasizes the significance of managing one's nervous system and internal world to cultivate resilience and peace.В   Tune in for insights that challenge traditional definitions of success and encourage listeners to redefine what it means to thrive.  Talking Points:  00:33 - Rethinking Success  01:15 - Uncertainty and the Nervous System  03:02 - Leading Through Uncertainty  05:44 - Elegant Success: What No One Tells You  07:08 - The Messiness of Success  10:07 - Life and Business Changes  13:10 - Trusting Your Gut  16:21 - Releasing with Grace  18:05 - Feeling Lost Before Clarity  20:15 - Shedding Old Identities  23:04 - The Emptiness of Misaligned Success  25:01 - Reconnecting with Your Essence  26:05 - Navigating Back to Your North Star  Quotes:  "What if the real flex is creating a life that feels as good on the inside as it looks on the outside?"  "Elegant success is creating the life you desire without abandoning yourself in the process."  "I've encountered so much peace by just accepting that it's going to feel elegant and then it's going to feel messy again."  "You cannot evolve and expect everyone to come with you."  "The doubt that you may be feeling, well, it's not failure, it's just feedback."  "I started to trade my truth for someone else's checklist, and I was shaping my life around approval instead of alignment." title: Top 10 Signs You Have a Dismissive Avoidant Attachment Style
summary: 14-Day All-Access Trial: Transform Your Life with Expert-Led Mental Health & Personal Growth Programs https://attachment.personaldevelopmentschool.com/mha-month?utm_source=youtube&utm_campaign=mha-month&utm_medium=organic&utm_content=pod-05-07-25&el=youtube-podcast В  В  Do you feel uncomfortable with vulnerability, tend to pull away in relationships, or avoid conflict at all costs? In this episode of The Thais Gibson Podcast, Thais shares the 10 most common signs of dismissive avoidant attachment and the powerful subconscious programming behind them. Whether you relate to these traits or recognize them in a partner, this episode offers clarity and a compassionate path forward. В  What YouвҖҷll Learn in This Episode: вң”пёҸ Why DAs avoid vulnerability and emotional intimacy вң”пёҸ How they subconsciously escape from emotional discomfort вң”пёҸ The impact of covert emotional neglect on identity and core wounds вң”пёҸ Why dismissive avoidants fear conflictвҖ”and how it plays out in relationships вң”пёҸ How shame, fear of criticism, and a belief of being вҖңdefectiveвҖқ shape their behavior вң”пёҸ Why DAs tend to lose feelings suddenlyвҖ”and how it's linked to fear, not fact вң”пёҸ What it means when DAs say, вҖңIвҖҷm just not capable of being in a relationshipвҖқ вң”пёҸ Simple reprogramming steps to become securely attached If youвҖҷve ever said вҖңI donвҖҷt do emotions,вҖқ or dated someone who shuts down the moment things get real, this episode is your blueprint to understanding and transforming dismissive avoidant attachment from the inside out. Meet Your Host: Thais Gibson is the founder of The Personal Development School, best-selling author, and world-renowned expert on attachment theory. With a Ph.D. and over 13 certifications in CBT, somatic healing, and subconscious reprogramming, Thais has helped over 70,000 students transform their emotional patterns and relationships. title: 228: The Danger of Staying the Same
podcast_name: Health, Habits, and Epic Living | Helping Women Lose Weight and Create Healthy Habits
summary: The Danger of Staying the SameВ  В  In this thought-provoking episode, Dr. Sherry Price delves into the often-overlooked risks of remaining in our comfort zones. She explores how the fear of change can hinder personal growth, health, and overall well-being.В  Change can be difficult, but staying the same can be harder in the long run.В  True freedom and transformation begin when we embrace the call to change.В      рҹ§  Key TakeawaysВ        Understanding Complacency: How staying the same can lead to stagnation and apathy in various aspects of life.В          Health Implications: Not changing certain habits can negatively impact your physical and mental health.В          Embracing Change: Practical steps to overcome the fear of change and initiate positive transformations.В          Mindset Shifts: Cultivating a growth mindset to navigate life's transitions effectively.В        рҹӣ пёҸ Resources & LinksВ        Visit epicyou.com for more insights and resources.В          Explore Dr. Sherry Price's programs:В          EpicYOU 1:1 CoachingВ          Metabolic MakeoverВ          Drink Less LifestyleВ          Connect on Instagram: @drsherryprice

title: Good Girl Gone Bad Feat. Michelle Dennis
summary: They say "you plan and God laughs" and this statement couldn't be more real in this season. For the Clue In Segment, Host, Chidinma shares the upcoming book by Kennedy Ryan, Can't Get Enough  and the R&B album, Heavy by Sir. In addition, the movie Sinners and the TV show, Paradise are on Chidinma's radar. For the Main Segment, Chidinma is joined by the Creator of the Life Unfiltered and Unscripted Podcast , Michelle Dennis to discuss womanhood, various archetypes, societal pressures and the concept of being a "good" or "bad" woman. title: 403: 10 Ways Integrating Buddhist Psychology into Your Everyday Life Cultivates Contentment
podcast_name: The Simple Sophisticate - Intelligent Living Paired with Signature Style
summary: "Buddhism is not a belief system. It's not about accepting certain tenets or believing a set of claims or principles. In fact, it's quite the opposite. It's about examining the world clearly and carefully, about testing everything and every idea. Buddhism is about seeing. It's about knowing rather than believing or hoping or wishing. It's also about not being afraid to examine anything and everything, including our own personal agendas . . . The message is always to examine and see for yourself. When you see for yourself what is trueвҖ”and that's really the only way that you can genuinely know anythingвҖ”then embrace it. Until then, just suspend judgment and criticism. The point of Buddhism is to just see. That's all . . . An ordinary person is simply one who is not awake in the moment; a buddha is a person who is. That's all." вҖ”Steve Hagan, author of Buddhism Plain and Simple: The practice of Being Aware, Right Now, Every Day  To be fully human and see the humanity in all others. To have compassion for all sentient beings, understanding that they too experience all of the senses and are capable of emotions. This way of walking through life, engaging with it subtly, yet powerfully, infusing both our daily life and others, with the ability to be and express our full and most capable selves, is to live with awareness. To see and engage fully without expectation or harm.  For example, one of the ladybird's gifts is their appetite for many pests in the garden, effectively being a natural pest deterrent; the talent of bees is to disperse pollen which plays a crucial role in the entire food production chain; and as we consider any human on earth throughout history who gives of their talents that further contributed positively to the world. These brave and determined souls, without each one, the world would not be as we know it today.  When we celebrate the humanity of one another, we set each other free to discover the treasures we each uniquely have within us. Then it is each individual's job, at times daunting, but most primarily exhilarating, to share with the world what we have realized is our dharma - what we can uniquely share with the world that also energizes us while we engage in the giving.  There is much confusion about what Buddhism is, and unlike what many Google searches will retrieve when we try to figure it out, it is not a religion. The Dalai Lama himself states it most directly, вҖңBuddhist teachings are not a religion, they are a science of the mind.вҖқ In other words, it is an approach to understanding our own mind, NOT being told what to think nor HOW to live concretely. Rather the concepts of Mindfulness, Awareness, Compassion, Appreciation and Courage are many of the fundamental skills, which are also the core concepts of cultivating a life of contentment, a simply luxurious life.  Tune in to discover more about how Buddhist psychology cultivates an everyday life of contentment.  В   Find the Show Notes on The Simply Luxurious Life blog - https://thesimplyluxuriouslife.com/podcast403

title: STOP HATING YOURSELF: Why your worst enemy might live in your own head
summary: In this episode of LATE BLOOMERS, Rich and Rox dive deep into the quiet epidemic of self-hatred. We explore where it really comes from: childhood trauma, rejection, bullying, addiction, neurodivergence, and the survival strategies we learned just to make it through. Whether it's self-sabotage, choosing the wrong people, or never feeling good enough, self-hate often hides in plain sight. We share our own storiesвҖ”gambling addiction, self-harm, broken relationships, and shameвҖ”and the long, messy road toward finally learning to treat ourselves with compassion. If youвҖҷve ever struggled with that voice in your head that says youвҖҷre not enough, this is the episode you need. We also break down some practical ways to start changing the script: how to recognise your inner critic, track your self-talk, accept compliments without deflecting, and understand that self-hatred is something you learnedвҖ”which means itвҖҷs something you can unlearn.

title: What a Renowned Cardiologist Has Learned About Health and Life After 40 Years | Dr. Sheldon Goldberg
summary: In this special episode on wisdom, Zac and Jay sit down with Dr. Sheldon Goldberg вҖ” known affectionately as Dr. G вҖ” a pioneering interventional cardiologist whose medical career spans over five decades. Dr. Goldberg serves as Clinical Professor of Medicine at the Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania and Director of Cardiovascular Education and Research at Pennsylvania Hospital. With over 150 publications, 8,000 citations, and a central role in the groundbreaking STRESS trial that helped establish the use of coronary stents, Dr. GoldbergвҖҷs impact on the field is both deep and lasting. Together, they explore not just the evolution of cardiology вҖ” from the earliest days of interventional procedures to todayвҖҷs cutting-edge treatments вҖ” but also the deeper lessons heвҖҷs learned about healing, resilience, and what keeps the human heart beating in more ways than one. The conversation moves from the hard truths about smoking, alcohol, and lifestyle choices to a candid critique of AmericaвҖҷs broken medical system. Dr. Goldberg reflects on the emotional toll of medicine, the difficulty of balancing work and family, and why humility and human connection matter more than ever in an era increasingly dominated by AI and corporate healthcare. Zac and Jay also dig into the personal: What does a man whoвҖҷs saved thousands of lives regret? What keeps him motivated? And what would he tell the next generation of doctors who are stepping into a vastly different world? This is a rich, revealing, and profoundly human conversation about medicine, mortality, and meaning. title: 396: Rha Goddess on Breaking Free From Survival and Living Your True Purpose
summary: "What you believe will determine the reality you live."  What if the key to your freedom isnвҖҷt about striving harder, but surrendering to the truth thatвҖҷs already inside you?  In todayвҖҷs Soul Talk, I sit down with the incredible Rha Goddess, visionary author, spiritual coach, and a real force of nature. Rha doesnвҖҷt just talk about purpose, that changed everything: being shot at point-blank range and walking away untouched. That moment didnвҖҷt just wake her up, it called her deeper into her mission.  We go deep into the myths weвҖҷve been sold about money, work, and worthiness. We talk about survival consciousness, burnout, belief systems, and how most of us are still chasing someone elseвҖҷs version of success. Rha breaks it all down and shows us how to rise, how to listen to the whispers of your soul and build a life rooted in alignment, joy, and truth.  This isnвҖҷt just a conversation. ItвҖҷs a transmission, a remembering; a call to come home to yourself.  Tune in and learn how to stop living from fear, start trusting your inner wisdom, and finally answer the call on your life.  Timestamps:В    (00:03:04) - Rha's origin story and her calling rooted in liberation. (00:07:10) -The life-altering moment: Being shot at point-blank range. Why is it so hard to shift out of survival mode? How do we challenge limiting beliefs around work and worth? Can you succeed without fully believing in yourself? What is вҖңIntentional AmbitionвҖқ and how do we practice it?  In This Episode You Will Learn:В    How to recognize and respond to the subtle nudges of your soul in the face of uncertainty. Why your survival mindset may be sabotaging your success, and how to shift into a state of abundance and trust. The hidden power of believing in yourВ idea, even before you fully believe in yourself The root of our limiting beliefs around money, work, and worthiness. What вҖңIntentional AmbitionвҖқ really means and how to apply it. title: Reflection // Tools to Build Mental Wellness
summary: Welcome to this special reflection episode with the legendary Paul Roos, taken from our full length episode recorded in 2024.  Paul as a legend of the AFL game, both as a player and a coach. He played for Fitzroy and the Sydney Swans, earning countless honours, including a spot in the Australian Football Hall of Fame.  After his playing days, he took on the role of coach and led the Sydney Swans to a historic premiership win in 2005. His innovative approach and focus on team dynamics have made a huge impact on the sport.  Beyond his impressive football career, Paul is a dedicated advocate for culture, leadership, men's mental health and well-being. He believes strongly in the power of mindfulness and meditation and has incorporated these practices into his life and work. As a father of two sons, heвҖҷs passionate about raising emotionally intelligent and resilient young men who both soft & strong at the same time. We jump into this deeply in that incredible and open conversation. title: EP52: The Diddy Trial, Day 1
summary: Hey friends, in this episode I'm breaking down what went down on Day 1 of the Diddy trial. The trial officially kicked off on Monday, May 5th in Manhattan. Since this is a federal case, the general public isnвҖҷt allowed inside the courtroomвҖ”but a few members of the press and close family are permitted, mainly to protect the privacy and safety of the jurors. So hereвҖҷs what we know so far: twelve jurors will be selected, along with six alternates. Opening statements are expected to begin on May 12, and the entire trial is anticipated to run for at least eight weeks. Stay tunedвҖ”I'll be keeping you posted every step of the way.
